target: A mesa da sala de reuniões é grande.
english: The meeting room table is big.

target: Eu trouxe o contrato para o escritório.
english: I brought the contract to the office.

target: A história do Brasil é fascinante.
english: Brazil's history is fascinating.

target: Os pratos da comida mineira são fartos.
english: The dishes of Minas Gerais cuisine are generous.

target: Eu gosto das pessoas daqui.
english: I like the people from here.

target: A escola das crianças é perto de casa.
english: The kids' school is near home.

target: O resultado do exame sai amanhã.
english: The exam result comes out tomorrow.

target: Uma garrafa de água gelada, por favor.
english: A bottle of cold water, please.

target: As notícias do dia são preocupantes.
english: Today's news is worrying.

target: O ponto de ônibus fica na esquina.
english: The bus stop is at the corner.

target: A vista do apartamento é incrível.
english: The apartment view is incredible.

target: Eu coloquei os copos na pia.
english: I put the glasses in the sink.

target: A chuva do verão é forte no Rio.
english: The summer rain is heavy in Rio.

target: O trânsito na hora do rush é terrível.
english: Rush hour traffic is terrible.

target: As toalhas estão no armário do banheiro.
english: The towels are in the bathroom cabinet.

target: Eu comprei uma passagem de avião para Salvador.
english: I bought a plane ticket to Salvador.

target: O porteiro do prédio é muito prestativo.
english: The building doorman is very helpful.
grammar: 'Porteiro' is a very common figure in Brazilian apartments — the doorman/concierge who manages building access.

target: A greve dos motoristas atrapalhou tudo.
english: The drivers' strike disrupted everything.

target: Ele vai ao shopping comprar roupa.
english: He's going to the mall to buy clothes.

target: A qualidade dos produtos é boa.
english: The product quality is good.

target: Os moradores do prédio reclamaram do barulho.
english: The building residents complained about the noise.

target: Uma colher de açúcar no café.
english: A spoonful of sugar in the coffee.

target: A festa na casa da Ana foi ótima.
english: The party at Ana's house was great.

target: O bairro da Lapa é famoso pela vida noturna.
english: The Lapa neighborhood is famous for its nightlife.

target: As plantas do escritório precisam de água.
english: The office plants need water.

target: Eu preciso ir à farmácia comprar remédio.
english: I need to go to the pharmacy to buy medicine.

target: O horário do almoço é das doze às duas.
english: Lunch time is from twelve to two.

target: A comida da minha mãe é a melhor.
english: My mom's cooking is the best.

target: Os livros da estante são do meu irmão.
english: The books on the shelf are my brother's.

target: Eu fui à praia com os amigos.
english: I went to the beach with friends.

target: A cozinha do apartamento é pequena.
english: The apartment kitchen is small.

target: O mercado perto da minha casa é bom.
english: The market near my house is good.

target: As flores da praça são coloridas.
english: The flowers in the square are colorful.

target: Eu deixei a carteira no carro.
english: I left my wallet in the car.

target: A prova da faculdade é na terça.
english: The college exam is on Tuesday.

target: Eu gosto muito de praia.
english: I really like the beach.
grammar: 'Gostar de' always requires the preposition 'de.' You can't say 'gosto praia' — it must be 'gosto de praia.'

target: Ela não gosta de acordar cedo.
english: She doesn't like waking up early.
grammar: 'Gostar de' + infinitive for activities: 'não gosta de acordar.' The 'de' is always required before the verb.

target: Vocês gostam de comida picante?
english: Do you like spicy food?
grammar: Question with 'gostar de': the 'de' stays even in questions. 'Comida picante' = spicy food.

target: Eu preciso de ajuda com essa mudança.
english: I need help with this move.
grammar: 'Precisar de' means 'to need.' The 'de' is required: 'preciso de ajuda,' not 'preciso ajuda.' However, before infinitives, many Brazilians drop the 'de.'

target: A gente gosta de viajar no Carnaval.
english: We like to travel during Carnival.
grammar: 'A gente gosta de' — remember 'a gente' takes singular: 'gosta' not 'gostamos.' 'No Carnaval' = during Carnival.

target: Ele prefere café sem açúcar.
english: He prefers coffee without sugar.
grammar: 'Preferir' doesn't need a preposition — just 'prefiro café' or 'prefiro ir a pé.' Unlike 'gostar de' and 'precisar de.'

target: Eu preciso de mais tempo para terminar.
english: I need more time to finish.
grammar: 'Precisar de' = to need (something). Like 'gostar de,' the preposition 'de' is mandatory: 'preciso de mais tempo.'

target: Ela gosta de ler antes de dormir.
english: She likes to read before bed.
grammar: 'Gostar de' + infinitive: 'gosta de ler.' 'Antes de' = before. 'Antes de dormir' = before sleeping.

target: Nós preferimos ir de carro.
english: We prefer to go by car.
grammar: 'Preferir' = to prefer. Unlike 'gostar' and 'precisar,' it does NOT take 'de': 'preferimos ir' (not 'de ir').

target: Vocês precisam de alguma coisa?
english: Do you need anything?
grammar: 'Precisar de' in questions: 'Vocês precisam de alguma coisa?' 'Alguma coisa' = something/anything.

target: Eu gosto de cozinhar para os amigos.
english: I like cooking for friends.
grammar: 'Gostar de' + infinitive: 'gosto de cozinhar.' 'Para os amigos' = for friends. Purpose with 'para.'

target: Ele prefere trabalhar de manhã.
english: He prefers to work in the morning.

target: Ela precisa de férias urgente.
english: She urgently needs a vacation.

target: A gente gosta de experimentar restaurantes novos.
english: We like trying new restaurants.

target: Eu prefiro água com gás.
english: I prefer sparkling water.

target: Eles gostam de futebol americano.
english: They like American football.

target: Eu preciso de um favor.
english: I need a favor.

target: Ela gosta muito do trabalho dela.
english: She really likes her job.
grammar: When 'gostar de' is followed by a definite article, contractions happen: gostar do (de + o), gostar da (de + a), gostar dos/das.

target: Nós preferimos ficar em casa hoje.
english: We prefer to stay home today.

target: Vocês gostam de sertanejo?
english: Do you like sertanejo music?
grammar: Sertanejo is Brazil's most popular music genre — a country-style music with modern pop influences. It's everywhere in Brazil.

target: Eu preciso de silêncio para estudar.
english: I need silence to study.

target: Ele gosta de acordar cedo para correr.
english: He likes waking up early to run.

target: Ela prefere morar perto do trabalho.
english: She prefers to live near work.

target: A gente precisa de mais espaço.
english: We need more space.

target: Eu gosto de tomar banho de chuva.
english: I like showering in the rain.

target: Vocês preferem mesa dentro ou fora?
english: Do you prefer a table inside or outside?

target: Ele precisa de um computador novo.
english: He needs a new computer.

target: Eu não gosto de trânsito.
english: I don't like traffic.

target: Ela gosta de filmes de suspense.
english: She likes thriller movies.

target: Nós precisamos de paciência para esperar.
english: We need patience to wait.

target: Eu prefiro pagar com Pix.
english: I prefer to pay with Pix.

target: A gente gosta de fazer trilha.
english: We like hiking.
grammar: 'Fazer trilha' means to hike. Brazilians use 'fazer' in many leisure expressions: fazer mergulho (dive), fazer escalada (climb), fazer rapel (rappel).

target: Eles preferem viajar de avião.
english: They prefer to travel by plane.

target: Eu preciso de mais informações.
english: I need more information.

target: Ela não gosta de gente mal-educada.
english: She doesn't like rude people.

target: Vocês gostam de churrasco?
english: Do you like barbecue?

target: Eu prefiro o horário da manhã.
english: I prefer the morning schedule.

target: Ela precisa de uma folga.
english: She needs a day off.

target: A gente gosta de dormir com a janela aberta.
english: We like sleeping with the window open.

target: Nós preferimos comida caseira.
english: We prefer home cooking.
grammar: 'Comida caseira' means homemade food. In Brazil, homemade food is highly valued — 'comida de verdade' (real food) is a common expression.

target: Eu gosto de andar descalço.
english: I like walking barefoot.

target: Ele precisa de orientação.
english: He needs guidance.

target: Vocês preferem carne ou peixe?
english: Do you prefer meat or fish?

target: Eu gosto de ouvir música ao vivo.
english: I like listening to live music.

target: Ela gosta de tirar fotos.
english: She likes taking photos.

target: A gente precisa de uma resposta até sexta.
english: We need an answer by Friday.

target: Eu prefiro não falar sobre isso.
english: I'd rather not talk about that.

target: Eles gostam de jogos de tabuleiro.
english: They like board games.

target: Eu preciso de um carro para trabalhar.
english: I need a car for work.

target: Ela prefere o café mais forte.
english: She prefers stronger coffee.

target: A gente gosta de passar o Natal em família.
english: We like spending Christmas with family.

target: Ela é alta e magra.
english: She's tall and thin.
grammar: Adjectives must agree with the noun in gender: alto/alta, magro/magra. Masculine -o becomes feminine -a.

target: O restaurante é mais caro do que eu pensava.
english: The restaurant is more expensive than I thought.
grammar: Comparatives use 'mais + adjective + do que': mais caro do que = more expensive than. The 'do' is optional in speech.

target: Meu irmão mais novo é o mais alto da família.
english: My younger brother is the tallest in the family.
grammar: 'Mais novo' = younger, 'mais alto' = tallest/taller. 'O mais + adjective + da' = the most...of (superlative).

target: A comida está deliciosa.
english: The food is delicious.
grammar: Adjectives agree in gender: 'deliciosa' (fem.) matches 'comida' (fem.). Masculine would be 'delicioso.'

target: Ele é um cara muito simpático.
english: He's a very nice guy.
grammar: 'Simpático' = friendly/nice. In Brazilian Portuguese, this is a very common compliment. Agrees with masculine 'cara.'

target: O apartamento novo é maior que o antigo.
english: The new apartment is bigger than the old one.
grammar: 'Maior' means bigger/larger. It's an irregular comparative — you don't say 'mais grande.' Similarly: menor (smaller), melhor (better), pior (worse).

target: Ela é muito inteligente e dedicada.
english: She's very intelligent and dedicated.
grammar: Two adjectives with 'e' (and): both must agree with the subject. 'Inteligente' is the same for both genders.

target: O tempo está horrível hoje.
english: The weather is terrible today.
grammar: 'Horrível' = horrible/awful. Adjectives ending in -vel are the same for masculine and feminine.

target: As ruas são estreitas e cheias de gente.
english: The streets are narrow and full of people.
grammar: Adjectives ending in -o change to -as in feminine plural: estreito → estreitas. Adjectives ending in -e stay the same for both genders: grande → grandes.

target: Ele é menos tímido do que parece.
english: He's less shy than he seems.
grammar: 'Menos + adjective + do que' is how you form comparatives of inferiority: menos tímido do que = less shy than.

target: A praia estava vazia e tranquila.
english: The beach was empty and peaceful.
grammar: Two adjectives: 'vazia e tranquila' — both feminine to match 'praia.' Masc. would be 'vazio e tranquilo.'

target: Os filhos dela são educados e obedientes.
english: Her children are polite and obedient.
grammar: 'Educados e obedientes' — plural adjectives agree with plural noun 'filhos.' 'Dela' = de + ela = her.

target: Essa é a melhor pizza da cidade.
english: This is the best pizza in the city.
grammar: Superlatives use 'o/a mais' + adjective or irregular forms: o melhor (the best), o pior (the worst), o maior (the biggest).

target: O chefe é bastante exigente.
english: The boss is quite demanding.
grammar: 'Bastante' = quite/very. Unlike 'muito,' 'bastante' does NOT change form: always 'bastante exigente.'

target: A minha avó é muito carinhosa.
english: My grandmother is very affectionate.
grammar: 'Carinhosa' — feminine -osa ending to match 'avó' (grandmother). Masculine: 'carinhoso.'

target: O filme foi tão bom quanto o livro.
english: The movie was as good as the book.
grammar: 'Tão + adjective + quanto' means 'as... as': 'tão bom quanto' = as good as. You can also use 'como' instead of 'quanto.'

target: A rua é escura e perigosa à noite.
english: The street is dark and dangerous at night.
grammar: 'Escura e perigosa' — both adjectives in feminine to match 'rua' (street). 'À noite' = at night.

target: Ele é gordo e baixo, mas muito engraçado.
english: He's heavy and short, but very funny.
grammar: 'Gordo e baixo' — masculine adjectives matching 'ele.' Physical descriptions use 'ser' (permanent traits).

target: As meninas são tão estudiosas quanto os meninos.
english: The girls are as studious as the boys.
grammar: 'Tão...quanto' = as...as (comparison of equality). 'Tão estudiosas quanto' = as studious as.

target: O café está quente e forte.
english: The coffee is hot and strong.
grammar: Adjectives ending in -e don't change for gender: quente is the same for masculine and feminine. Only number changes: quentes.

target: Ela tem cabelos longos e olhos escuros.
english: She has long hair and dark eyes.

target: Esse bairro é mais seguro do que o outro.
english: This neighborhood is safer than the other one.

target: A casa é espaçosa e bem iluminada.
english: The house is spacious and well-lit.

target: O pior dia da semana é segunda-feira.
english: The worst day of the week is Monday.

target: Eles são muito barulhentos.
english: They're very noisy.

target: A cidade é bonita, mas cara.
english: The city is beautiful but expensive.

target: Ele é o funcionário mais antigo da empresa.
english: He's the longest-serving employee in the company.

target: A minha mala é mais pesada que a sua.
english: My suitcase is heavier than yours.

target: O bebê é muito fofo.
english: The baby is very cute.
grammar: 'Fofo/fofa' means cute and cuddly. Brazilians use it for babies, animals, and anything adorable.

target: As praias brasileiras são maravilhosas.
english: Brazilian beaches are wonderful.

target: Ela é muito responsável e organizada.
english: She's very responsible and organized.

target: O trânsito é menos pior de manhã.
english: Traffic is less bad in the morning.

target: A comida mineira é farta e saborosa.
english: Minas Gerais food is hearty and tasty.

target: Ele é meio preguiçoso, mas muito talentoso.
english: He's a bit lazy, but very talented.
grammar: 'Meio' before an adjective means 'kind of/a bit.' In informal Brazilian Portuguese, it doesn't change for gender: 'ela é meio louca' (not 'meia louca').

target: Os irmãos dele são completamente diferentes.
english: His siblings are completely different.

target: A viagem foi longa, mas agradável.
english: The trip was long but pleasant.

target: Ele é um homem forte e corajoso.
english: He's a strong and brave man.

target: As crianças são mais rápidas que os adultos.
english: The children are faster than the adults.

target: A situação é complicada demais.
english: The situation is too complicated.
grammar: 'Demais' after an adjective means 'too much': 'complicada demais' = too complicated. It's very common in Brazilian Portuguese.

target: Ela é a pessoa mais gentil que eu conheço.
english: She's the kindest person I know.

target: O hotel é simples, mas limpo e confortável.
english: The hotel is simple but clean and comfortable.

target: Meu pai é teimoso e orgulhoso.
english: My dad is stubborn and proud.

target: A água do rio é clara e fria.
english: The river water is clear and cold.

target: Esse restaurante é tão bom quanto caro.
english: This restaurant is as good as it is expensive.

target: Ela é bem humorada e divertida.
english: She's good-humored and fun.

target: O projeto é ambicioso, mas viável.
english: The project is ambitious but feasible.

target: Os prédios são altos e modernos.
english: The buildings are tall and modern.

target: A minha cachorra é pequena e branca.
english: My dog is small and white.
grammar: Colors agree with gender and number: branco/branca/brancos/brancas. Some colors don't change: laranja, rosa, violeta.

target: Ele é jovem demais para esse cargo.
english: He's too young for this position.

target: A sala é ampla e arejada.
english: The room is spacious and airy.

target: O problema é pior do que imaginamos.
english: The problem is worse than we imagined.

target: As flores são vermelhas e amarelas.
english: The flowers are red and yellow.

target: Minha mãe é a mulher mais forte que eu conheço.
english: My mom is the strongest woman I know.

target: O filme é interessante, mas muito longo.
english: The movie is interesting but very long.

target: Ela é loira de olhos azuis.
english: She's blonde with blue eyes.
grammar: 'De olhos azuis' is how you describe eye color in Portuguese — literally 'of blue eyes.' Same pattern for hair: de cabelos pretos.

target: O bolo ficou perfeito.
english: The cake turned out perfect.

target: A estrada é sinuosa e perigosa.
english: The road is winding and dangerous.

target: Ele é tão paciente quanto o pai dele.
english: He's as patient as his dad.

target: As noites de verão são quentes e agradáveis.
english: Summer nights are warm and pleasant.

target: O quarto é pequeno, mas aconchegante.
english: The room is small but cozy.
grammar: 'Aconchegante' means cozy/warm/inviting. It's an adjective ending in -e, so it doesn't change for gender: o quarto aconchegante, a casa aconchegante.

target: Ela é muito parecida com a mãe.
english: She looks a lot like her mom.

target: O professor é rigoroso, mas justo.
english: The teacher is strict but fair.

target: A casa dos meus avós é antiga e charmosa.
english: My grandparents' house is old and charming.

target: O melhor café da cidade é nessa rua.
english: The best café in the city is on this street.

target: Ele ficou vermelho de vergonha.
english: He turned red with embarrassment.
grammar: 'Ficar' + adjective means 'to become': ficar vermelho (turn red), ficar triste (become sad), ficar bravo (get angry).

target: As praças são arborizadas e bem cuidadas.
english: The squares are tree-lined and well-maintained.

target: O serviço é rápido e eficiente.
english: The service is fast and efficient.

target: Ela é magra e atlética.
english: She's thin and athletic.

target: O pai dele é mais velho que a mãe.
english: His dad is older than his mom.

target: A música brasileira é alegre e contagiante.
english: Brazilian music is joyful and infectious.

target: Eles são muito unidos.
english: They're very close-knit.

target: A festa foi animada e divertida.
english: The party was lively and fun.

target: O atendimento nesse hotel é excelente.
english: The service at this hotel is excellent.

target: Ela ficou surpresa com a notícia.
english: She was surprised by the news.

target: O trânsito está cada vez pior.
english: Traffic is getting worse and worse.
grammar: 'Cada vez + comparative' means 'more and more' or 'increasingly': cada vez pior (worse and worse), cada vez melhor (better and better).

target: As crianças estão agitadas hoje.
english: The kids are hyper today.

target: Ele é careca e usa barba.
english: He's bald and has a beard.

target: A paisagem é deslumbrante.
english: The landscape is breathtaking.

target: O jantar estava maravilhoso.
english: The dinner was wonderful.

target: Ela é a menos tímida do grupo.
english: She's the least shy in the group.

target: O vento está forte demais para ir à praia.
english: The wind is too strong to go to the beach.

target: As pessoas aqui são acolhedoras e calorosas.
english: The people here are welcoming and warm.

target: O trabalho é cansativo, mas gratificante.
english: The work is tiring but rewarding.

target: Ela é morena de cabelos cacheados.
english: She's dark-haired with curly hair.
grammar: In Brazil, 'morena' is a broad term that can mean brunette, olive-skinned, or dark-haired. It's used much more widely than in other Portuguese-speaking countries.

target: O parque é enorme e muito verde.
english: The park is huge and very green.

target: Meu chefe é mais novo do que eu.
english: My boss is younger than me.

target: A vista daqui é a mais bonita da cidade.
english: The view from here is the most beautiful in the city.

target: Ele é sério no trabalho, mas brincalhão em casa.
english: He's serious at work but playful at home.

target: A camiseta está apertada demais.
english: The T-shirt is too tight.

target: Os avós dela são super ativos.
english: Her grandparents are super active.

target: O suco está gelado e refrescante.
english: The juice is ice-cold and refreshing.

target: Ela ficou brava com a situação.
english: She got angry about the situation.
grammar: 'Bravo/brava' in Brazil means angry (not brave). This is a common false friend — 'corajoso' means brave.

target: O bairro é calmo durante o dia.
english: The neighborhood is calm during the day.

target: As respostas dele foram curtas e grossas.
english: His answers were short and rude.

target: O cachorro é grande e peludo.
english: The dog is big and furry.

target: A reunião foi produtiva.
english: The meeting was productive.

target: Ele é tímido no começo, mas depois se abre.
english: He's shy at first, but opens up later.

target: A comida está salgada demais.
english: The food is too salty.

target: Os dias estão mais curtos no inverno.
english: The days are shorter in winter.

target: Ela é a profissional mais qualificada da equipe.
english: She's the most qualified professional on the team.

target: Tudo bem com você?
english: Is everything okay with you?
grammar: 'Tudo bem' is the most common greeting in Brazil. You can answer with 'tudo bem,' 'tudo ótimo,' or just 'tudo.'

target: Por favor, pode repetir?
english: Please, can you repeat that?

target: Com licença, onde fica o banheiro?
english: Excuse me, where's the bathroom?
grammar: 'Com licença' is used when you need to get past someone or interrupt politely. It's more common than 'desculpe' for these situations.

target: Desculpa, eu não entendi.
english: Sorry, I didn't understand.

target: Muito prazer em conhecer você.
english: Very nice to meet you.

target: Fica à vontade.
english: Make yourself at home.
grammar: 'Ficar à vontade' means to make yourself comfortable. Brazilians say this constantly when you visit their home or office.

target: Valeu, cara!
english: Thanks, man!
grammar: 'Valeu' is the most casual way to say 'thanks' in Brazil. It literally comes from 'valer' (to be worth). Very informal but used everywhere.

target: Não tem de quê.
english: You're welcome.

target: Pode deixar que eu resolvo.
english: Don't worry, I'll take care of it.
grammar: 'Pode deixar' means 'leave it to me' or 'don't worry about it.' It's a very reassuring Brazilian expression.

target: Que legal!
english: How cool!
grammar: 'Que legal' is the most common exclamation of approval in Brazil. 'Legal' literally means 'legal' but is used like 'cool/nice/great.'

target: Sei lá, não faço ideia.
english: I dunno, I have no idea.
grammar: 'Sei lá' is extremely common casual speech for 'I don't know.' Much more colloquial than 'não sei.'

target: Fala sério!
english: No way! / Are you serious?

target: Beleza, a gente se vê amanhã.
english: Cool, see you tomorrow.
grammar: 'Beleza' means 'beauty' but is used as 'okay/cool/deal.' 'Beleza?' can also be a greeting: 'E aí, beleza?' = Hey, what's up?

target: Eu acho que sim.
english: I think so.

target: Imagina, não foi nada.
english: No worries, it was nothing.
grammar: 'Imagina' is how Brazilians casually say 'don't mention it' or 'no worries.' Used when someone thanks you or apologizes.

target: Cuidado com o degrau!
english: Watch out for the step!

target: Tomara que dê certo.
english: I hope it works out.
grammar: 'Tomara que' means 'I hope that' and is followed by the subjunctive. But you'll hear it so often, you'll learn it as a set phrase.

target: Deixa eu ver...
english: Let me see...

target: Que saudade!
english: I miss it so much!

target: Faz tempo que não nos vemos.
english: It's been a while since we've seen each other.

target: Tá bom, eu concordo.
english: Alright, I agree.
grammar: 'Tá bom' is the casual contraction of 'está bom' — used constantly to mean 'okay/alright.' Also: 'tá' for 'está,' 'tô' for 'estou.'

target: Meu Deus, que susto!
english: My God, what a scare!

target: De jeito nenhum!
english: No way!

target: Pode falar, eu estou ouvindo.
english: Go ahead, I'm listening.

target: Que pena, eu não vou poder ir.
english: What a shame, I won't be able to go.
grammar: 'Que pena' means 'what a shame/pity.' Very common for expressing regret or disappointment.

target: Nossa, que calor!
english: Wow, it's so hot!
grammar: 'Nossa' (short for 'Nossa Senhora' = Our Lady) is the most common exclamation in Brazil. Used for surprise, shock, or emphasis.

target: Boa sorte na prova!
english: Good luck on the exam!

target: E aí, tudo certo?
english: Hey, everything good?
grammar: 'E aí' is a very casual greeting, like 'hey' or 'what's up.' Often followed by 'tudo bem?' or 'tudo certo?'

target: Sério mesmo?
english: Really? For real?

target: Sem problemas, pode contar comigo.
english: No problem, you can count on me.

target: Que bom que você veio!
english: I'm glad you came!
grammar: 'Que bom que' means 'how good that' or 'I'm glad that.' It's the natural way to express that you're pleased about something.

target: Com certeza, pode contar.
english: Absolutely, you can count on it.

target: Tchau, até mais!
english: Bye, see you later!

target: É isso aí!
english: That's right! / That's it!

target: Calma, vai dar tudo certo.
english: Relax, everything will work out.
grammar: 'Dar certo' is one of the most important Brazilian expressions. It means 'to work out/to go well.' 'Vai dar certo' = it'll be fine.

target: Pois é, a vida não é fácil.
english: Yeah, life isn't easy.
grammar: 'Pois é' is a common filler/agreement expression meaning 'yeah' or 'exactly' or 'that's how it is.' Used to show empathy or agreement.

target: Deus me livre!
english: God forbid!

target: Já volto, espera um minutinho.
english: I'll be right back, wait just a minute.
grammar: The diminutive '-inho' softens requests. 'Um minutinho' (a little minute) sounds much more polite than 'um minuto.'

target: Não esquenta a cabeça com isso.
english: Don't worry about it.
grammar: 'Esquentar a cabeça' literally means 'to heat your head.' 'Não esquenta' is casual for 'don't worry/don't stress.'

target: Cara, que difícil!
english: Man, that's tough!

target: Parabéns pelo trabalho!
english: Congratulations on the work!

target: Fique tranquilo, está tudo sob controle.
english: Don't worry, everything's under control.

target: Eu adorei, ficou perfeito!
english: I loved it, it turned out perfect!

target: Não acredito!
english: I can't believe it!

target: Olha só, que coincidência!
english: Look at that, what a coincidence!
grammar: 'Olha só' is a very common conversation opener meaning 'look' or 'check this out.' Used to draw attention to something.

target: Pelo amor de Deus, para com isso!
english: For the love of God, stop that!

target: Fica pra próxima.
english: Maybe next time.
grammar: 'Fica pra próxima' is the polite way to turn down an invitation while leaving the door open for future plans.

target: Obrigado pela ajuda, eu agradeço.
english: Thanks for the help, I appreciate it.

target: Que coisa, hein!
english: Wow, what a thing, huh!

target: Bom dia! Dormiu bem?
english: Good morning! Did you sleep well?

target: Boa tarde, como vão as coisas?
english: Good afternoon, how's everything going?

target: Boa noite, descansa bem!
english: Good night, rest well!

target: Vai com Deus!
english: Go with God! (Take care!)

target: Saúde!
english: Bless you! (after a sneeze)

target: Que vergonha, eu esqueci o nome dele.
english: How embarrassing, I forgot his name.

target: Não se preocupa, tudo tem solução.
english: Don't worry, everything has a solution.

target: Tô ligado, pode deixar.
english: I'm on it, don't worry.
grammar: 'Tô ligado' literally means 'I'm connected/on' — it means 'I understand' or 'I'm aware.' Very colloquial Brazilian slang.

target: Que chato, choveu o dia inteiro.
english: How annoying, it rained all day.
grammar: 'Que chato' means 'how annoying/what a bummer.' 'Chato' is one of the most useful adjectives in Brazilian Portuguese.

target: Falou, até depois!
english: Alright, see you later!
grammar: 'Falou' (literally 'spoke') is a casual goodbye in Brazil, like 'later' or 'peace out.' Often combined: 'falou, valeu!'

target: Eu tô de boa.
english: I'm all good. / I'm chill.
grammar: 'Tô de boa' means 'I'm fine/relaxed/chill.' Very informal but used constantly among friends.

target: Bora!
english: Let's go!
grammar: 'Bora' is a contraction of 'vamos embora' (let's go). It's possibly the most-used casual word in Brazilian Portuguese.

target: Que massa!
english: That's awesome!

target: Pera aí, deixa eu pensar.
english: Hang on, let me think.
grammar: 'Pera aí' is a contraction of 'espera aí' (wait there). Very common in casual speech.

target: Oxe, como assim?
english: Huh? What do you mean?
grammar: 'Oxe' is an exclamation from Northeastern Brazil expressing surprise or confusion. It's spread across the whole country.

target: Tá ligado?
english: You know what I mean?

target: É nóis!
english: We're in this together! / That's us!

target: Manda ver!
english: Go for it!

target: Que absurdo isso!
english: That's outrageous!

target: Se cuida, viu?
english: Take care, okay?
grammar: 'Viu' (literally 'did you see') is added at the end of sentences as a tag question meaning 'okay?' or 'got it?'. Very Brazilian.

target: Vai nessa!
english: Go for it! / Off you go!

target: Eu tô nem aí.
english: I don't care.
grammar: 'Tô nem aí' is a casual way to say 'I don't care.' Somewhat dismissive — use with friends, not your boss.

target: Que maravilha!
english: How wonderful!

target: Eu tava brincando, relaxa.
english: I was kidding, relax.

target: Ainda bem que deu certo.
english: Thank goodness it worked out.
grammar: 'Ainda bem que' means 'thank goodness that' or 'it's a good thing that.' Expresses relief.

target: Aí é demais!
english: That's too much! / That's going too far!

target: Tô morrendo de fome!
english: I'm starving!
grammar: 'Morrendo de' + noun is how Brazilians exaggerate: morrendo de fome (starving), morrendo de sono (dead tired), morrendo de rir (dying of laughter).

target: Cadê o controle remoto?
english: Where's the remote control?
grammar: 'Cadê' is a uniquely Brazilian word meaning 'where is.' It's informal and used constantly. It comes from 'que é de' (what is of).

target: Nem me fale!
english: Don't even get me started!

target: Tô a fim de comer pizza.
english: I feel like eating pizza.
grammar: 'Estar a fim de' means 'to feel like' or 'to be in the mood for.' Casual but very common in everyday Brazilian speech.

target: Já é!
english: Done deal! / Let's do it!

target: Que alívio, achei que tinha perdido.
english: What a relief, I thought I'd lost it.

target: É pra já!
english: Right away!

target: Oi, tudo bom?
english: Hi, how are you?

target: Eu não aguento mais!
english: I can't take it anymore!

target: Tamo junto!
english: We're in this together!
grammar: 'Tamo junto' is a contraction of 'estamos juntos.' It's a way of showing solidarity and friendship in Brazil.

target: Que horror!
english: How horrible!

target: Até que enfim!
english: Finally! / At last!

target: De nada, foi um prazer.
english: You're welcome, it was a pleasure.

target: Coitado, ele não merece isso.
english: Poor thing, he doesn't deserve that.
grammar: 'Coitado/coitada' means 'poor thing.' Used to express sympathy. It's not rude — it's genuinely empathetic.

target: Eu tô por fora do assunto.
english: I'm out of the loop on that.

target: Capaz! Eu nunca faria isso.
english: No way! I would never do that.

target: É assim que funciona aqui.
english: That's how it works here.

target: Tô atrasado, vou correndo!
english: I'm late, I'm running!

target: Que sorte a nossa!
english: How lucky we are!

target: Nada a ver, não foi isso que eu disse.
english: That's not right, that's not what I said.
grammar: 'Nada a ver' means 'nothing to do with it' or 'that's not it.' A very common expression for disagreement.

target: Boa viagem! Manda notícias.
english: Have a good trip! Send news.

target: Que fofo!
english: How cute!

target: Faz parte, vida que segue.
english: It happens, life goes on.
grammar: 'Faz parte' means 'it's part of life' or 'it happens.' 'Vida que segue' = life goes on. Together, they form a very Brazilian philosophical acceptance.

target: Eu mereço férias!
english: I deserve a vacation!

target: Que demora, hein!
english: Taking long enough, huh!

target: Mentira! Não é possível.
english: No way! That can't be.

target: Bom apetite!
english: Enjoy your meal!

target: Eu acho que não, mas vou verificar.
english: I don't think so, but I'll check.

target: Pega leve, ele está passando por um momento difícil.
english: Go easy on him, he's going through a tough time.
grammar: 'Pegar leve' means 'to go easy on' or 'to take it easy.' The opposite is 'pegar pesado' (to be harsh/to overdo it).

target: Que nada, foi fácil.
english: Nah, it was easy.

target: Eu tô de saco cheio disso.
english: I'm fed up with this.

target: Deixa para lá, não vale a pena.
english: Forget about it, it's not worth it.
grammar: 'Deixa para lá' means 'let it go/forget it.' 'Valer a pena' means 'to be worth it' — both are expressions you'll hear daily.

target: Tem certeza?
english: Are you sure?

target: Ai meu Deus!
english: Oh my God!

target: Tudo tranquilo, graças a Deus.
english: Everything's fine, thank God.

target: Pois não? Em que posso ajudar?
english: Yes? How can I help you?
grammar: 'Pois não' is a formal/polite way to say 'how can I help you.' You'll hear it from shopkeepers, receptionists, and servers.

target: Que saco, perdi o ônibus!
english: How annoying, I missed the bus!

target: Vamos combinar para a semana que vem.
english: Let's make plans for next week.

target: Boa sorte! Vai dar tudo certo.
english: Good luck! Everything will work out.

target: Sabe de uma coisa? Eu desisto.
english: You know what? I give up.

target: Tá na hora de ir embora.
english: It's time to leave.

target: Eu tô com uma preguiça...
english: I'm feeling so lazy...

target: Que dia lindo!
english: What a beautiful day!

target: Manda um abraço para a sua família.
english: Send a hug to your family.

target: Eu tô precisando de um café.
english: I'm needing a coffee.

target: Vamos nessa!
english: Let's do this!

target: Que bacana, parabéns!
english: How nice, congratulations!
grammar: 'Bacana' means nice/cool/great. It's a bit more grown-up than 'legal' and less slangy than 'massa.'

target: Posso te fazer uma pergunta?
english: Can I ask you a question?

target: A gente se fala depois.
english: We'll talk later.

target: Eu tô morrendo de sono.
english: I'm dead tired.

target: Que loucura!
english: How crazy!

target: Você é muito gente boa.
english: You're really a nice person.
grammar: 'Gente boa' literally means 'good people' but is used to describe someone who's kind, cool, and easygoing. A big compliment in Brazil.

target: Nem pensar!
english: Out of the question!

target: Fiz besteira, me desculpa.
english: I messed up, I'm sorry.
grammar: 'Fazer besteira' means to mess up or make a mistake. 'Besteira' can also mean something silly or nonsense.

target: Pode entrar, a casa é sua.
english: Come in, make yourself at home.

target: Bom final de semana!
english: Have a good weekend!

target: Está tudo bem sim, obrigado.
english: Everything is fine, thanks.

target: Que susto você me deu!
english: You scared me!

target: Menos mal que a gente chegou a tempo.
english: At least we arrived on time.

target: Eu tô na correria hoje.
english: I'm in a rush today.
grammar: 'Na correria' means in a rush/hustle. It captures the hectic pace of Brazilian city life. Very common in São Paulo.

target: Relaxa, não precisa se preocupar.
english: Relax, you don't need to worry.

target: Que delícia!
english: How delicious!

target: Eu tô perdido, pode me ajudar?
english: I'm lost, can you help me?

target: Que bagunça!
english: What a mess!

target: Eu vou te contar uma coisa.
english: I'm going to tell you something.

target: Cai fora!
english: Get out of here! / Beat it!

target: Que novidade, hein!
english: What else is new, huh!

target: Pode ficar tranquilo.
english: You can relax. / Don't worry.

target: Tô de olho em você!
english: I've got my eye on you!

target: Graças a Deus!
english: Thank God!

target: Eita, quase caí!
english: Whoa, I almost fell!
grammar: 'Eita' is an exclamation common in the Northeast and now used nationally. It expresses surprise or a near miss.

target: Eu tô doido pra ir embora.
english: I'm dying to leave.

target: Até amanhã, descansa!
english: See you tomorrow, get some rest!

target: Senta aí, faz de conta que é da casa.
english: Sit down, act like you're at home.
grammar: 'Fazer de conta' means 'to pretend.' 'Faz de conta que é da casa' is a warm invitation to feel comfortable.

target: É por aí mesmo.
english: That's about right.

target: Eu falei com o gerente ontem.
english: I spoke with the manager yesterday.
grammar: The pretérito perfeito is the simple past. Regular -ar verbs: -ei, -ou, -amos, -aram. 'Falar' → 'falei' (eu).

target: Ela comprou um vestido novo para a festa.
english: She bought a new dress for the party.
grammar: Regular -ar past: comprar → comprou (ele/ela). Third person singular: drop -ar, add -ou.

target: Nós almoçamos juntos na sexta passada.
english: We had lunch together last Friday.
grammar: 'Almoçamos' is the same for present and past (nós form). Context tells you which tense: 'ontem almoçamos' = past, 'sempre almoçamos' = present.

target: Vocês gostaram do restaurante?
english: Did you like the restaurant?
grammar: Regular -ar past: gostar → gostaram (vocês). Third person plural: drop -ar, add -aram.

target: Eu trabalhei até tarde ontem.
english: I worked until late yesterday.
grammar: Regular -ar past: trabalhar → trabalhei (eu). First person: drop -ar, add -ei.

target: Ele mudou de emprego mês passado.
english: He changed jobs last month.
grammar: Regular -ar past: mudar → mudou. 'Mês passado' = last month — a key time expression for past tense.

target: A gente viajou para Minas Gerais no feriado.
english: We traveled to Minas Gerais on the holiday.
grammar: Regular -ar past: viajar → viajou. With 'a gente,' use third person singular: 'a gente viajou.'

target: Eu comi muito no churrasco.
english: I ate a lot at the barbecue.
grammar: Regular -er verbs in the past: -i, -eu, -emos, -eram. 'Comer' → 'comi' (eu), 'comeu' (ele/ela).

target: Ela vendeu o carro na semana passada.
english: She sold the car last week.
grammar: Regular -er past: vender → vendeu (ela). Second conjugation past: drop -er, add -eu.

target: Nós saímos cedo do trabalho ontem.
english: We left work early yesterday.
grammar: Regular -ir verbs in the past: -i, -iu, -imos, -iram. 'Sair' → 'saí' (eu), 'saiu' (ele/ela).

target: Vocês assistiram ao jogo ontem?
english: Did you watch the game yesterday?
grammar: Regular -ir past: assistir → assistiram (vocês). Third conjugation past: drop -ir, add -iram.

target: Eu liguei para você, mas não atendeu.
english: I called you, but you didn't answer.
grammar: Regular -ar past: ligar → liguei. Note the spelling change: g → gu before -ei to keep the /g/ sound.

target: Ele estudou a noite inteira para a prova.
english: He studied all night for the exam.
grammar: Regular -ar past: estudar → estudou. 'A noite inteira' = the whole night. Time expression reinforces past.

target: A gente chegou atrasado por causa do trânsito.
english: We arrived late because of traffic.
grammar: Regular -ar past: chegar → chegou. With 'a gente,' always singular: 'a gente chegou.'

target: Eu paguei a conta do restaurante.
english: I paid the restaurant bill.
grammar: Regular -ar past: pagar → paguei. Spelling change: g → gu before -ei (like ligar → liguei).

target: Ela preparou um jantar especial.
english: She prepared a special dinner.
grammar: Regular -ar past: preparar → preparou. The -ou ending is the hallmark of third-person -ar past.

target: Nós voltamos de viagem ontem à noite.
english: We came back from the trip last night.
grammar: Regular -ar past: voltar → voltamos (nós). The -amos ending is the same as present! Context tells which.

target: Vocês receberam o meu e-mail?
english: Did you receive my email?
grammar: Regular -er past: receber → receberam (vocês). Second conjugation plural past: -eram.

target: Eu morei em Salvador por dois anos.
english: I lived in Salvador for two years.
grammar: Regular -ar past: morar → morei (eu). 'Por dois anos' = for two years — 'por' for duration.

target: Ele encontrou um emprego novo.
english: He found a new job.
grammar: Regular -ar past: encontrar → encontrou. 'Emprego novo' — adjective after noun is the default position.

target: A gente combinou de se encontrar no shopping.
english: We agreed to meet at the mall.
grammar: Regular -ar past: combinar → combinou. 'Combinar de' = to agree to / to arrange. 'A gente combinou.'

target: Eu esqueci de comprar o pão.
english: I forgot to buy the bread.
grammar: Regular -er past: esquecer → esqueci (eu). 'Esquecer de' = to forget to. -er past first person: -i.

target: Ela perguntou se eu queria ir junto.
english: She asked if I wanted to go along.
grammar: Regular -ar past: perguntar → perguntou. 'Se' introduces indirect questions: 'perguntou se eu queria.'

target: Nós jantamos num restaurante italiano.
english: We had dinner at an Italian restaurant.
grammar: Regular -ar past: jantar → jantamos. Same form as present — context ('num restaurante italiano') clarifies past.

target: Vocês aproveitaram as férias?
english: Did you enjoy the vacation?
grammar: Regular -ar past: aproveitar → aproveitaram. 'Aproveitar' = to enjoy/take advantage of.

target: Eu corri cinco quilômetros hoje de manhã.
english: I ran five kilometers this morning.
grammar: Regular -er past: correr → corri (eu). First person -er/-ir past: drop ending, add -i.

target: Ele começou um curso de inglês.
english: He started an English course.
grammar: Regular -ar past: começar → começou. Spelling change: ç stays before -ou (no change needed here).

target: A gente curtiu muito a praia.
english: We really enjoyed the beach.
grammar: 'Curtir' means to enjoy/have fun. In the past: 'curti a festa' = I enjoyed the party. Very common in informal Brazilian Portuguese.

target: Eu respondi todas as mensagens.
english: I replied to all the messages.
grammar: Regular -er past: responder → respondi. First person -er past: -i. 'Todas as mensagens' = all the messages.

target: Ela cozinhou um prato delicioso.
english: She cooked a delicious dish.
grammar: Regular -ar past: cozinhar → cozinhou. 'Um prato delicioso' = a delicious dish.

target: Nós caminhamos pela orla da praia.
english: We walked along the beachfront.
grammar: Regular -ar past: caminhar → caminhamos. 'Pela orla' = por + a = along the waterfront.

target: Vocês entenderam a explicação?
english: Did you understand the explanation?
grammar: Regular -er past: entender → entenderam. Plural -er past: -eram. 'A explicação' = the explanation.

target: Eu lavei toda a louça depois do jantar.
english: I washed all the dishes after dinner.
grammar: Regular -ar past: lavar → lavei. 'Toda a louça' = all the dishes. 'Depois do' = after the.

target: Ele terminou o relatório a tempo.
english: He finished the report on time.
grammar: Regular -ar past: terminar → terminou. 'A tempo' = on time (just in time). 'No prazo' = by the deadline.

target: A gente passeou pelo centro histórico.
english: We strolled through the historic center.
grammar: Regular -ar past: passear → passeou. 'Pelo centro' = por + o = through/around the center.

target: Eu perdi a carteira no ônibus.
english: I lost my wallet on the bus.
grammar: Regular -er past: perder → perdi. First person: -i. 'Perder' = to lose. 'Carteira' = wallet.

target: Ela limpou a casa inteira no sábado.
english: She cleaned the entire house on Saturday.
grammar: Regular -ar past: limpar → limpou. 'A casa inteira' = the whole house. 'No sábado' = on Saturday.

target: Nós compramos os ingressos online.
english: We bought the tickets online.
grammar: Regular -ar past: comprar → compramos. 'Online' is used as-is in Brazilian Portuguese.

target: Vocês tomaram café da manhã?
english: Did you have breakfast?
grammar: Regular -ar past: tomar → tomaram. 'Tomar café da manhã' = to have breakfast (a set expression).

target: Eu mudei de ideia sobre a viagem.
english: I changed my mind about the trip.
grammar: Regular -ar past: mudar → mudei. 'Mudar de ideia' = to change one's mind (fixed expression).

target: Ele gastou muito dinheiro na reforma.
english: He spent a lot of money on the renovation.
grammar: Regular -ar past: gastar → gastou. 'Gastar' = to spend (money). 'Na reforma' = on the renovation.

target: A gente cantou karaokê a noite inteira.
english: We sang karaoke all night long.
grammar: Regular -ar past: cantar → cantou. 'A noite inteira' = the whole night. 'A gente cantou.'

target: Eu resolvi o problema do computador.
english: I fixed the computer problem.
grammar: Regular -er past: resolver → resolvi. 'Resolver' = to solve/fix. Very common in daily Brazilian Portuguese.

target: Ela mandou a mensagem errada para o grupo.
english: She sent the wrong message to the group.
grammar: Regular -ar past: mandar → mandou. 'Mandar' = to send. 'Mensagem errada' = wrong message.

target: Nós dividimos a pizza entre quatro.
english: We split the pizza among four.
grammar: Regular -ir past: dividir → dividimos. Same as present form — past meaning clear from 'pizza entre quatro.'

target: Vocês visitaram o museu?
english: Did you visit the museum?
grammar: Regular -ar past: visitar → visitaram. 'O museu' = the museum (masculine noun).

target: Eu acordei com dor de cabeça.
english: I woke up with a headache.
grammar: Regular -ar past: acordar → acordei. 'Dor de cabeça' = headache (literally 'pain of head').

target: Ele entregou o projeto no prazo.
english: He delivered the project on time.
grammar: Regular -ar past: entregar → entregou. 'No prazo' = on time/by the deadline.

target: A gente alugou uma casa na praia.
english: We rented a beach house.
grammar: Regular -ar past: alugar → alugou. 'A gente alugou' — remember singular conjugation with 'a gente.'

target: Eu bebi muita água de coco na praia.
english: I drank a lot of coconut water at the beach.
grammar: Regular -er past: beber → bebi. 'Água de coco' = coconut water — a beloved Brazilian drink.

target: Ela gravou a reunião para ouvir depois.
english: She recorded the meeting to listen to later.
grammar: Regular -ar past: gravar → gravou. 'Gravar' = to record. 'Para ouvir depois' = to listen later.

target: Nós arrumamos a sala para a festa.
english: We set up the room for the party.
grammar: Regular -ar past: arrumar → arrumamos. 'Arrumar' = to tidy up/arrange. 'Para a festa' = for the party.

target: Vocês experimentaram o açaí daqui?
english: Did you try the açaí from here?
grammar: Regular -ar past: experimentar → experimentaram. 'Experimentar' = to try (food/experience). 'O açaí daqui' = the açaí from here.

target: Eu consertei a torneira do banheiro.
english: I fixed the bathroom faucet.
grammar: Regular -ar past: consertar → consertei. 'Consertar' = to fix/repair. 'Torneira' = faucet/tap.

target: Ele marcou uma consulta com o dentista.
english: He scheduled a dentist appointment.

target: A gente dançou forró a noite inteira.
english: We danced forró all night.

target: Eu guardei os documentos na gaveta.
english: I kept the documents in the drawer.

target: Ela decorou o apartamento para o Natal.
english: She decorated the apartment for Christmas.

target: Nós esperamos quase uma hora na fila.
english: We waited almost an hour in line.

target: Vocês jogaram futebol no fim de semana?
english: Did you play soccer on the weekend?

target: Eu organizei todas as fotos da viagem.
english: I organized all the trip photos.

target: Ele apresentou o projeto para a diretoria.
english: He presented the project to the board.

target: A gente nadou no rio a tarde inteira.
english: We swam in the river all afternoon.

target: Eu imprimi o documento que você pediu.
english: I printed the document you asked for.

target: Ela almoçou com as amigas no sábado.
english: She had lunch with her friends on Saturday.

target: Nós assistimos a um show de samba.
english: We watched a samba show.

target: Vocês provaram a comida baiana?
english: Did you try Bahian food?

target: Eu deixei as chaves em cima da mesa.
english: I left the keys on the table.

target: Ele ajudou os colegas com o projeto.
english: He helped his colleagues with the project.

target: A gente explorou a cidade inteira a pé.
english: We explored the entire city on foot.

target: Eu aprendi muito nesse curso.
english: I learned a lot in this course.

target: Ela escolheu o restaurante para o jantar.
english: She chose the restaurant for dinner.

target: Nós economizamos bastante esse mês.
english: We saved quite a bit this month.

target: Vocês descansaram no feriado?
english: Did you rest on the holiday?

target: Eu desci as escadas correndo.
english: I ran down the stairs.

target: Ele cancelou a reunião de última hora.
english: He canceled the meeting at the last minute.

target: A gente conversou até tarde.
english: We talked until late.

target: Eu troquei o pneu do carro na estrada.
english: I changed the car tire on the road.

target: Ela plantou flores no jardim.
english: She planted flowers in the garden.

target: Nós pintamos o quarto do bebê.
english: We painted the baby's room.

target: Vocês conheceram alguém interessante?
english: Did you meet anyone interesting?

target: Eu perdi o ônibus e cheguei atrasado.
english: I missed the bus and arrived late.

target: Ele tocou violão na festa.
english: He played guitar at the party.

target: A gente comeu demais no rodízio.
english: We overate at the all-you-can-eat.
grammar: 'Rodízio' is a Brazilian dining style where servers bring endless rounds of meat (or pizza, sushi, etc.) to your table. Very popular.

target: Eu mandei a encomenda pelo correio.
english: I sent the package by mail.

target: Ela arrumou as malas na véspera.
english: She packed the bags the night before.

target: Nós participamos de um workshop.
english: We participated in a workshop.

target: Vocês avisaram o chefe?
english: Did you let the boss know?

target: Eu escrevi um e-mail longo demais.
english: I wrote an email that was too long.

target: Ele ganhou uma promoção no trabalho.
english: He got a promotion at work.

target: A gente apostou no jogo e perdeu.
english: We bet on the game and lost.

target: Eu cortei o dedo cortando cebola.
english: I cut my finger cutting onion.

target: Ela achou a carteira que tinha perdido.
english: She found the wallet she had lost.

target: Nós brindamos com champanhe.
english: We toasted with champagne.

target: Vocês tiraram fotos do pôr do sol?
english: Did you take pictures of the sunset?

target: Eu reservei uma mesa para quatro.
english: I reserved a table for four.

target: Ele apertou o botão errado.
english: He pressed the wrong button.

target: A gente aproveitou o desconto e comprou tudo.
english: We took advantage of the discount and bought everything.

target: Eu acordei assustado com o barulho.
english: I woke up startled by the noise.

target: Ela convidou todos os amigos para a festa.
english: She invited all her friends to the party.

target: Nós trocamos de lugar no avião.
english: We switched seats on the plane.

target: Vocês acabaram o trabalho?
english: Did you finish the work?

target: Eu tomei um susto quando vi a conta.
english: I got a shock when I saw the bill.

target: Ele parou de fumar há seis meses.
english: He stopped smoking six months ago.
grammar: 'Parar de' + infinitive means 'to stop doing something.' 'Há' + time means 'ago': 'há seis meses' = six months ago.

target: A gente adorou a viagem ao Pantanal.
english: We loved the trip to the Pantanal.

target: Eu emprestei dinheiro para um amigo.
english: I lent money to a friend.
grammar: 'Emprestar' in Brazilian Portuguese means both 'to lend' and 'to borrow.' Context clarifies: 'emprestei PARA ele' = I lent to him. 'Emprestei DELE' = I borrowed from him.

target: Ela completou o curso em dois anos.
english: She completed the course in two years.

target: Nós perdemos o voo e tivemos que remarcar.
english: We missed the flight and had to rebook.

target: Vocês ganharam o jogo?
english: Did you win the game?

target: Eu notei que ele estava nervoso.
english: I noticed he was nervous.

target: Ele telefonou três vezes, mas ninguém atendeu.
english: He called three times, but nobody answered.

target: A gente jogou cartas até meia-noite.
english: We played cards until midnight.

target: Eu comparei os preços antes de comprar.
english: I compared prices before buying.

target: Ela pintou o cabelo de loiro.
english: She dyed her hair blonde.

target: Nós dobramos as roupas e guardamos.
english: We folded the clothes and put them away.

target: Vocês provaram a feijoada?
english: Did you try the feijoada?
grammar: Feijoada is Brazil's national dish — a rich black bean stew with pork. Traditionally served on Wednesdays and Saturdays.

target: Eu agradeci pelo presente.
english: I thanked them for the gift.

target: Ele fechou a porta com força.
english: He slammed the door.

target: A gente descansou o dia inteiro no domingo.
english: We rested the whole day on Sunday.

target: Eu pedi a conta e fui embora.
english: I asked for the bill and left.

target: Ela explicou a situação com calma.
english: She explained the situation calmly.

target: Nós acampamos perto de uma cachoeira.
english: We camped near a waterfall.

target: Vocês entraram pela porta dos fundos?
english: Did you come in through the back door?

target: Eu pensei muito antes de decidir.
english: I thought a lot before deciding.

target: Ele misturou todos os ingredientes.
english: He mixed all the ingredients.

target: A gente pesquisou bastante antes de viajar.
english: We researched a lot before traveling.

target: Eu colei um cartaz na parede.
english: I taped a poster on the wall.

target: Ela usou o meu computador emprestado.
english: She used my computer on loan.

target: Nós subimos a montanha em três horas.
english: We climbed the mountain in three hours.

target: Vocês compraram as passagens?
english: Did you buy the tickets?

target: Eu cortei o bolo em oito pedaços.
english: I cut the cake into eight pieces.

target: Ele anotou o endereço num papel.
english: He wrote down the address on a piece of paper.

target: A gente torceu para o Flamengo.
english: We cheered for Flamengo.
grammar: 'Torcer para' means to root for/cheer for a team. Flamengo is Brazil's most popular soccer team, with fans across the entire country.

target: Eu misturei português com espanhol sem querer.
english: I accidentally mixed Portuguese with Spanish.

target: Ela completou trinta anos ontem.
english: She turned thirty yesterday.

target: Nós rimos muito com aquela história.
english: We laughed a lot at that story.

target: Vocês notaram a diferença?
english: Did you notice the difference?

target: Eu estraguei a surpresa sem querer.
english: I accidentally ruined the surprise.

target: Ele assistiu a cinco filmes no fim de semana.
english: He watched five movies on the weekend.

target: A gente limpou tudo antes de ir embora.
english: We cleaned everything before leaving.

target: Eu perguntei o caminho para um morador.
english: I asked a local for directions.

target: Ela levou o cachorro para passear.
english: She took the dog for a walk.

target: Nós comemoramos o aniversário no restaurante.
english: We celebrated the birthday at the restaurant.

target: Vocês ficaram satisfeitos com o resultado?
english: Were you satisfied with the result?

target: Eu baixei o aplicativo que você recomendou.
english: I downloaded the app you recommended.

target: Ele consertou o celular em meia hora.
english: He fixed the phone in half an hour.

target: A gente almoçou numa churrascaria famosa.
english: We had lunch at a famous steakhouse.

target: Eu estacionei o carro na garagem.
english: I parked the car in the garage.

target: Ela mudou o visual completamente.
english: She completely changed her look.

target: Nós nos encontramos no aeroporto por acaso.
english: We ran into each other at the airport by chance.

target: Vocês passearam de barco?
english: Did you go on a boat ride?

target: Eu choveu muito e eu me molhei todo.
english: It rained a lot and I got all wet.

target: Ele confirmou a reserva por telefone.
english: He confirmed the reservation by phone.

target: A gente pulou Carnaval no Rio.
english: We celebrated Carnival in Rio.
grammar: 'Pular Carnaval' is the Brazilian expression for celebrating/partying during Carnival. Literally 'to jump Carnival.'

target: Eu copiei os arquivos para o pen drive.
english: I copied the files to the flash drive.

target: Ela regou as plantas enquanto eu viajei.
english: She watered the plants while I was traveling.

target: Nós trocamos presentes no Natal.
english: We exchanged gifts at Christmas.

target: Vocês trancaram a porta?
english: Did you lock the door?

target: Eu coloquei os pratos na mesa.
english: I put the plates on the table.

target: Ele aceitou a proposta de emprego.
english: He accepted the job offer.

target: A gente surfou ondas enormes.
english: We surfed huge waves.

target: Eu abri a janela porque estava abafado.
english: I opened the window because it was stuffy.

target: Ela atravessou a rua sem olhar.
english: She crossed the street without looking.

target: Nós devolvemos o livro à biblioteca.
english: We returned the book to the library.

target: Vocês guardaram as sobras na geladeira?
english: Did you put the leftovers in the fridge?

target: Eu ensaiei a apresentação várias vezes.
english: I rehearsed the presentation several times.

target: Ele montou o móvel sozinho.
english: He assembled the furniture by himself.

target: A gente pescou a tarde inteira.
english: We fished all afternoon.

target: Eu pendurei o quadro na parede.
english: I hung the picture on the wall.

target: Ela contou uma piada e todo mundo riu.
english: She told a joke and everyone laughed.

target: Nós dirigimos seis horas até chegar.
english: We drove six hours to get there.

target: Vocês ficaram em qual hotel?
english: Which hotel did you stay at?

target: Eu passei protetor solar, mas me queimei mesmo assim.
english: I put on sunscreen, but got sunburned anyway.

target: Ele ensinou português para estrangeiros.
english: He taught Portuguese to foreigners.

target: A gente juntou dinheiro para a viagem.
english: We saved up money for the trip.

target: Eu acordei cedo e fui correr.
english: I woke up early and went for a run.

target: Ela cuidou das crianças enquanto eu trabalhei.
english: She took care of the kids while I worked.

target: Nós procuramos o restaurante por uma hora.
english: We looked for the restaurant for an hour.

target: Vocês levaram guarda-chuva?
english: Did you bring an umbrella?

target: Eu recebi o pagamento na sexta.
english: I received the payment on Friday.

target: Ele errou o endereço e se perdeu.
english: He got the address wrong and got lost.

target: A gente comemorou com uma cerveja gelada.
english: We celebrated with a cold beer.

target: Eu anotei tudo no caderno.
english: I wrote everything down in the notebook.

target: Ela comprou lembrancinhas para a família.
english: She bought souvenirs for the family.

target: Nós reclamamos do serviço no hotel.
english: We complained about the hotel service.

target: Vocês escutaram aquele barulho?
english: Did you hear that noise?

target: Eu trouxe bolo para o escritório.
english: I brought cake to the office.

target: Ele melhorou muito depois do tratamento.
english: He improved a lot after the treatment.

target: A gente voltou para casa a pé.
english: We walked back home.

target: Eu pratiquei português com os moradores.
english: I practiced Portuguese with the locals.

target: Ela depositou o cheque no banco.
english: She deposited the check at the bank.

target: Nós filmamos tudo durante a viagem.
english: We filmed everything during the trip.

target: Vocês contaram para os pais?
english: Did you tell your parents?

target: Eu cozinhei o jantar de aniversário.
english: I cooked the birthday dinner.

target: Ele negociou um preço melhor.
english: He negotiated a better price.

target: A gente mergulhou no rio gelado.
english: We dove into the freezing river.

target: Eu assinei o contrato ontem.
english: I signed the contract yesterday.

target: Ela quebrou o vaso sem querer.
english: She accidentally broke the vase.

target: Nós esperamos o pôr do sol na praia.
english: We waited for the sunset at the beach.

target: Eu entrevistei três candidatos hoje.
english: I interviewed three candidates today.

target: Eu fui ao médico na terça-feira.
english: I went to the doctor on Tuesday.
grammar: 'Ir' and 'ser' share the same past tense forms: fui, foi, fomos, foram. Context tells you which verb it is: 'fui ao médico' = I went.

target: Ela fez um bolo de chocolate incrível.
english: She made an incredible chocolate cake.
grammar: 'Fazer' is irregular in the past: fiz, fez, fizemos, fizeram. 'Fez' is one of the most common past tense forms you'll hear.

target: Nós tivemos uma reunião longa hoje.
english: We had a long meeting today.
grammar: 'Ter' in the past: tive, teve, tivemos, tiveram. Completely irregular — no pattern to follow, just memorize.

target: Vocês vieram de carro ou de ônibus?
english: Did you come by car or by bus?
grammar: 'Vir' in the past: vim, veio, viemos, vieram. Don't confuse 'veio' (came) with 'viu' (saw).

target: Eu disse que não podia ir.
english: I said I couldn't go.
grammar: 'Dizer' in the past: disse, disse, dissemos, disseram. The eu and ele/ela forms are the same: 'eu disse' = 'ele disse.'

target: Ele trouxe comida para todo mundo.
english: He brought food for everyone.
grammar: 'Trazer' in the past: trouxe, trouxe, trouxemos, trouxeram. The 'x' is pronounced like 'ss.'

target: A gente pôde sair mais cedo ontem.
english: We were able to leave early yesterday.
grammar: 'Poder' in the past: pude, pôde, pudemos, puderam. Note the circumflex on 'pôde' (past) to distinguish from 'pode' (present).

target: Eu soube da notícia pelo jornal.
english: I found out the news through the newspaper.
grammar: 'Saber' in the past: soube, soube, soubemos, souberam. In the past, 'saber' often means 'to find out' rather than 'to know.'

target: Ela quis ir embora mais cedo.
english: She wanted to leave earlier.
grammar: 'Querer' in the past: quis, quis, quisemos, quiseram. Completely irregular — the stem changes to 'quis-.'

target: Nós demos um presente para ela.
english: We gave her a gift.
grammar: 'Dar' in the past: dei, deu, demos, deram. Short and irregular — very common forms to know.

target: Eu vi um acidente na estrada.
english: I saw an accident on the road.
grammar: 'Ver' in the past: vi, viu, vimos, viram. Very short forms — don't confuse 'vi' (I saw) with 'vim' (I came).

target: Ele pôs as chaves na mesa.
english: He put the keys on the table.
grammar: 'Pôr' in the past: pus, pôs, pusemos, puseram. In casual speech, many Brazilians say 'coloquei' instead of 'pus.'

target: Vocês foram ao show ontem?
english: Did you go to the concert yesterday?
grammar: 'Ir' past: vocês foram. 'Foram' is past of both 'ir' and 'ser' — context tells which. Here: 'went.'

target: Eu fiz tudo que pude.
english: I did everything I could.
grammar: 'Fazer' past: eu fiz. 'Tudo que pude' = everything I could. 'Poder' past: eu pude.

target: A gente teve sorte de encontrar lugar.
english: We were lucky to find a spot.
grammar: 'Ter' past: a gente teve. 'Ter sorte de' = to be lucky to. Remember 'a gente' = singular.

target: Ela disse que vai pensar no assunto.
english: She said she'll think about it.
grammar: 'Dizer' past: ela disse. 'Disse que vai' — the reported speech keeps the original tense in casual Brazilian Portuguese.

target: Eu trouxe o documento que você pediu.
english: I brought the document you asked for.
grammar: 'Trazer' past: eu trouxe. Note the stem change: traz- → troux-. 'Que você pediu' = that you asked for.

target: Nós viemos de taxi porque choveu.
english: We came by taxi because it rained.
grammar: 'Vir' past: nós viemos. Don't confuse 'viemos' (came) with 'vimos' (saw). 'De taxi' = by taxi.

target: Ele soube a verdade ontem.
english: He found out the truth yesterday.
grammar: 'Saber' past: ele soube. The past of 'saber' often means 'found out' — a completed discovery.

target: Vocês deram uma olhada no projeto?
english: Did you take a look at the project?
grammar: 'Dar' past: vocês deram. 'Dar uma olhada' = to take a look — a very common expression.

target: Eu quis te ligar, mas era muito tarde.
english: I wanted to call you, but it was too late.
grammar: 'Querer' past: eu quis. 'Quis te ligar' = wanted to call you. 'Era muito tarde' = it was too late.

target: A gente foi ao cinema ver um filme brasileiro.
english: We went to the movies to see a Brazilian film.
grammar: 'Ir' past: a gente foi. 'Ir ao cinema' = to go to the movies. 'Ver' = to see/watch.

target: Ela fez uma viagem incrível para Fernando de Noronha.
english: She took an incredible trip to Fernando de Noronha.
grammar: 'Fazer' past: ela fez. 'Fez uma viagem' = took a trip. 'Fazer viagem' is the natural expression.

target: Eu tive que trabalhar no feriado.
english: I had to work on the holiday.
grammar: 'Ter' past: eu tive. 'Tive que' = I had to (obligation). 'No feriado' = on the holiday.

target: Ele veio me visitar de surpresa.
english: He came to visit me by surprise.
grammar: 'Vir' past: ele veio. 'Veio me visitar' = came to visit me. 'De surpresa' = as a surprise.

target: Nós dissemos a verdade desde o começo.
english: We told the truth from the beginning.
grammar: 'Dizer' past: nós dissemos. 'Desde o começo' = from the beginning.

target: Eu pus o dinheiro na carteira.
english: I put the money in my wallet.
grammar: 'Pôr' past: eu pus. Very irregular: pôr → eu pus, ele pôs, nós pusemos. 'Na carteira' = in the wallet.

target: Vocês viram aquele programa na TV?
english: Did you see that TV show?
grammar: 'Ver' past: vocês viram. Don't confuse 'viram' (saw) with 'vieram' (came). 'Aquele programa' = that show.

target: Ela deu uma festa surpresa para o marido.
english: She threw a surprise party for her husband.
grammar: 'Dar' past: ela deu. 'Dar uma festa' = to throw a party. 'Festa surpresa' = surprise party.

target: A gente pôde descansar no final de semana.
english: We were able to rest on the weekend.
grammar: 'Poder' past: a gente pôde. Note the circumflex: 'pôde' (past) vs. 'pode' (present).

target: Eu fui o primeiro a chegar na festa.
english: I was the first to arrive at the party.
grammar: Here 'fui' comes from 'ser' (to be), not 'ir' (to go). 'Eu fui o primeiro' = I was the first. Context clarifies.

target: Ele fez questão de pagar o jantar.
english: He insisted on paying for dinner.
grammar: 'Fazer' past: ele fez. 'Fazer questão de' = to insist on — a common fixed expression.

target: Nós tivemos uma surpresa agradável.
english: We had a pleasant surprise.
grammar: 'Ter' past: nós tivemos. 'Uma surpresa agradável' = a pleasant surprise.

target: Eu vi ela no supermercado ontem.
english: I saw her at the supermarket yesterday.
grammar: 'Ver' past: eu vi. 'Vi ela' is informal; formal would be 'a vi.' But 'vi ela' is standard in speech.

target: Vocês foram felizes nessa decisão.
english: You were wise in that decision.
grammar: 'Ser/Ir' past: vocês foram. Here 'foram felizes' = were happy, so it's past of 'ser.'

target: Ela trouxe flores para a mãe.
english: She brought flowers for her mom.
grammar: 'Trazer' past: ela trouxe. 'Trouxe flores para a mãe' = brought flowers for (her) mom.

target: A gente fez amizade com os vizinhos.
english: We became friends with the neighbors.
grammar: 'Fazer' past: a gente fez. 'Fazer amizade com' = to become friends with — a useful expression.

target: Eu soube que ele vai se mudar.
english: I found out that he's going to move.
grammar: 'Saber' past: eu soube. 'Soube que' = I found out that. Past of 'saber' implies learning new info.

target: Ele quis ajudar, mas não conseguiu.
english: He wanted to help, but couldn't.
grammar: 'Querer' past: ele quis. 'Quis ajudar, mas não conseguiu' = wanted to help but couldn't.

target: Nós demos a volta na cidade de bicicleta.
english: We went around the city by bike.
grammar: 'Dar' past: nós demos. 'Dar a volta' = to go around. 'De bicicleta' = by bicycle.

target: Eu fiz uma besteira enorme.
english: I made a huge mistake.
grammar: 'Fazer' past: eu fiz. 'Fazer besteira' = to do something stupid/make a blunder.

target: Ela veio de avião do Recife.
english: She came by plane from Recife.

target: Vocês tiveram problemas na viagem?
english: Did you have problems on the trip?

target: A gente disse que ia chegar às oito.
english: We said we'd arrive at eight.

target: Eu pude ver a paisagem do avião.
english: I was able to see the landscape from the plane.

target: Ele deu um abraço apertado em todo mundo.
english: He gave everyone a tight hug.

target: Nós fomos os últimos a sair.
english: We were the last to leave.

target: Eu trouxe um souvenir para você.
english: I brought a souvenir for you.

target: Ela fez de tudo para ajudar.
english: She did everything she could to help.

target: Vocês viram o pôr do sol?
english: Did you see the sunset?

target: A gente teve que cancelar a viagem.
english: We had to cancel the trip.

target: Eu fui promovido no trabalho.
english: I was promoted at work.

target: Ele disse que estava cansado demais.
english: He said he was too tired.

target: Nós fizemos um churrasco no sábado.
english: We had a barbecue on Saturday.

target: Eu quis experimentar, mas já tinha acabado.
english: I wanted to try it, but it was already gone.

target: Ela veio correndo quando ouviu a notícia.
english: She came running when she heard the news.

target: Vocês foram à praia mesmo com chuva?
english: Did you go to the beach even with rain?

target: A gente soube na hora que era mentira.
english: We knew right away that it was a lie.

target: Eu dei o meu melhor na entrevista.
english: I gave my best at the interview.

target: Ele fez uma piada e todo mundo riu.
english: He told a joke and everyone laughed.

target: Nós tivemos um dia maravilhoso.
english: We had a wonderful day.

target: Eu vim de metrô porque estava chovendo.
english: I came by subway because it was raining.

target: Ela pôs a mesa para o jantar.
english: She set the table for dinner.

target: Vocês disseram algo para ele?
english: Did you say anything to him?

target: A gente quis ajudar, mas ela recusou.
english: We wanted to help, but she refused.

target: Eu fiz uma promessa para mim mesmo.
english: I made a promise to myself.

target: Ele foi o melhor aluno da turma.
english: He was the best student in the class.

target: Nós trouxemos sobremesa para a festa.
english: We brought dessert for the party.

target: Eu soube que ela passou no concurso.
english: I heard she passed the civil service exam.
grammar: 'Concurso' in Brazil refers to competitive civil service exams. Passing one is a huge achievement and guarantees a stable government job.

target: Ela viu o arco-íris depois da chuva.
english: She saw the rainbow after the rain.

target: Vocês puderam resolver o problema?
english: Were you able to solve the problem?

target: A gente deu sorte com o tempo.
english: We got lucky with the weather.

target: Eu fui ao dentista pela primeira vez aqui.
english: I went to the dentist for the first time here.

target: Ele fez uma surpresa para a namorada.
english: He surprised his girlfriend.

target: Nós tivemos que esperar duas horas.
english: We had to wait two hours.

target: Eu disse tudo o que pensava.
english: I said everything I was thinking.

target: Ela trouxe um presente para cada um.
english: She brought a gift for each person.

target: Vocês vieram sozinhos?
english: Did you come by yourselves?

target: A gente fez uma pausa para o café.
english: We took a break for coffee.

target: Eu vi um documentário sobre a Amazônia.
english: I watched a documentary about the Amazon.

target: Ele deu uma caminhada longa no parque.
english: He took a long walk in the park.

target: Nós fomos a um casamento lindo.
english: We went to a beautiful wedding.

target: Eu fiz hora extra a semana inteira.
english: I worked overtime all week.

target: Ela quis saber todos os detalhes.
english: She wanted to know all the details.

target: Vocês fizeram reserva no hotel?
english: Did you make a hotel reservation?

target: A gente veio direto do aeroporto.
english: We came straight from the airport.

target: Eu tive uma ideia para o projeto.
english: I had an idea for the project.

target: Ele pôs tudo no lugar antes de sair.
english: He put everything back before leaving.

target: Nós soubemos na última hora.
english: We found out at the last minute.

target: Eu dei uma olhada no cardápio.
english: I took a look at the menu.

target: Ela foi a primeira da família a se formar.
english: She was the first in the family to graduate.

target: Vocês trouxeram roupa de banho?
english: Did you bring swimwear?

target: A gente fez um passeio de barco.
english: We went on a boat tour.

target: Eu vim a pé porque o ônibus atrasou.
english: I came on foot because the bus was late.

target: Ele disse que vai voltar semana que vem.
english: He said he'll come back next week.

target: Nós pudemos aproveitar a praia.
english: We were able to enjoy the beach.

target: Eu fiz as pazes com ela.
english: I made peace with her.
grammar: 'Fazer as pazes' means to make up/reconcile after a fight. Always plural: 'as pazes,' never 'a paz' in this expression.

target: Ela viu o show de fogos de artifício.
english: She saw the fireworks show.

target: Vocês foram bem na prova?
english: Did you do well on the exam?

target: A gente teve uma conversa séria.
english: We had a serious conversation.

target: Eu quis ir embora, mas fiquei.
english: I wanted to leave, but I stayed.

target: Ele trouxe a família toda para o Brasil.
english: He brought his whole family to Brazil.

target: Nós demos risada a noite inteira.
english: We laughed all night.

target: Eu fui honesto com ele sobre a situação.
english: I was honest with him about the situation.

target: Ela fez uma lista de tudo que precisava.
english: She made a list of everything she needed.

target: Vocês viram as cataratas do Iguaçu?
english: Did you see Iguaçu Falls?

target: A gente soube pelo noticiário.
english: We found out from the news.

target: Eu tive que pedir desculpas.
english: I had to apologize.

target: Ele veio de ônibus porque o carro quebrou.
english: He came by bus because his car broke down.

target: Nós fizemos uma trilha na Serra Gaúcha.
english: We hiked in Serra Gaúcha.

target: Eu disse para ele ter cuidado.
english: I told him to be careful.

target: Ela deu aulas durante dez anos.
english: She taught for ten years.

target: Vocês puderam aproveitar o feriado?
english: Were you able to enjoy the holiday?

target: A gente quis ir, mas não deu.
english: We wanted to go, but it didn't work out.
grammar: 'Não deu' is extremely common Brazilian Portuguese for 'it didn't work out' or 'it wasn't possible.' Short for 'não deu certo.'

target: Eu fui ao mercado e trouxe tudo.
english: I went to the market and brought everything.

target: Ele fez uma promessa e cumpriu.
english: He made a promise and kept it.

target: Nós tivemos que improvisar.
english: We had to improvise.

target: Eu vi a torre de São Paulo do avião.
english: I saw the São Paulo tower from the plane.

target: Ela veio com toda a família.
english: She came with her whole family.

target: Vocês fizeram tudo o que pediram?
english: Did you do everything they asked?

target: A gente deu uma volta pelo bairro.
english: We went for a walk around the neighborhood.

target: Eu disse que concordava com a proposta.
english: I said I agreed with the proposal.

target: Ele trouxe vinho para o jantar.
english: He brought wine for dinner.

target: Nós fomos à feira livre no sábado.
english: We went to the street market on Saturday.

target: Eu fiz a barba antes de sair.
english: I shaved before going out.

target: Ela pôde tirar uma folga na sexta.
english: She was able to take Friday off.

target: Vocês souberam do acidente?
english: Did you hear about the accident?

target: A gente viu golfinhos durante o passeio.
english: We saw dolphins during the tour.

target: Eu dei o meu número de telefone para ela.
english: I gave her my phone number.

target: Ele fez um esforço enorme para chegar a tempo.
english: He made an enormous effort to arrive on time.

target: Nós quisemos ajudar de todas as formas.
english: We wanted to help in every way.

target: Eu fui a Salvador pela primeira vez.
english: I went to Salvador for the first time.

target: Ela disse que ia pensar e me ligar.
english: She said she would think about it and call me.

target: Vocês trouxeram protetor solar?
english: Did you bring sunscreen?

target: A gente teve uma experiência incrível.
english: We had an incredible experience.

target: Eu fiz de tudo para não atrasar.
english: I did everything I could to not be late.

target: Ele veio pessoalmente pedir desculpas.
english: He came in person to apologize.

target: Nós vimos o nascer do sol na montanha.
english: We saw the sunrise on the mountain.

target: Eu dei uma desculpa para não ir.
english: I made an excuse not to go.

target: Ela soube lidar com a pressão.
english: She knew how to handle the pressure.

target: Vocês foram ao Pelourinho?
english: Did you go to Pelourinho?

target: A gente fez planos para o ano novo.
english: We made plans for the new year.

target: Eu tive coragem de falar a verdade.
english: I had the courage to tell the truth.

target: Ele pôs a culpa em mim.
english: He blamed me.
grammar: 'Pôr a culpa em' means to blame: 'pôs a culpa em mim' = he put the blame on me. Very common expression.

target: Nós dissemos tchau e fomos embora.
english: We said goodbye and left.

target: Eu quis comprar, mas era caro demais.
english: I wanted to buy it, but it was too expensive.

target: Ela deu certo no novo emprego.
english: She did well at the new job.

target: Vocês viram a exposição no museu?
english: Did you see the exhibition at the museum?

target: A gente trouxe lembrancinhas para todos.
english: We brought souvenirs for everyone.

target: Eu fui embora sem me despedir.
english: I left without saying goodbye.

target: Quando eu era criança, brincava na rua.
english: When I was a kid, I used to play in the street.
grammar: The imperfeito describes habitual past actions — things you USED TO DO. Regular -ar: -ava, -ava, -ávamos, -avam.

target: Ela morava perto da praia antes.
english: She used to live near the beach before.
grammar: Imperfeito of -ar: morava (ela). Regular: morar → morava. Used for habitual past or background states.
