target: Nós almoçávamos juntos todo dia.
english: We used to have lunch together every day.
grammar: Imperfeito of -ar: almoçávamos (nós). The -ávamos ending is always stressed on the 'á.'

target: Vocês iam à praia todo verão?
english: Did you used to go to the beach every summer?
grammar: Only four verbs are irregular in the imperfeito: ser (era), ter (tinha), vir (vinha), and pôr (punha). All others are regular.

target: Eu comia muito doce quando era adolescente.
english: I used to eat a lot of candy as a teenager.
grammar: Regular -er verbs in the imperfeito: -ia, -ia, -íamos, -iam. 'Comer' → 'comia.'

target: Ele trabalhava num banco antes de mudar de área.
english: He used to work at a bank before switching fields.
grammar: Imperfeito of -ar: trabalhava (ele). 'Antes de' + infinitive = before doing something.

target: A gente fazia churrasco todo domingo.
english: We used to have a barbecue every Sunday.
grammar: Imperfeito of -er: fazia (a gente). 'Fazer' imperfeito: eu fazia, a gente fazia. Irregular stem.

target: Eu dormia tarde quando era estudante.
english: I used to go to bed late when I was a student.
grammar: Regular -ir verbs in the imperfeito: -ia, -ia, -íamos, -iam. Same endings as -er verbs!

target: Ela tinha medo do escuro quando era pequena.
english: She was afraid of the dark when she was little.
grammar: 'Ter' in the imperfeito: tinha, tinha, tínhamos, tinham. One of only four irregular imperfeito verbs.

target: Nós íamos ao parque depois da escola.
english: We used to go to the park after school.
grammar: Imperfeito of 'ir': íamos (nós). 'Ir' is irregular in imperfeito: ia, ia, íamos, iam.

target: Vocês moravam no mesmo bairro?
english: Did you used to live in the same neighborhood?
grammar: Imperfeito of -ar: moravam (vocês). The -avam ending for vocês/eles. Question by intonation only.

target: Eu era muito tímido na escola.
english: I was very shy at school.
grammar: 'Ser' in the imperfeito: era, era, éramos, eram. Used for descriptions of how things used to be.

target: Ele assistia desenho animado o dia inteiro.
english: He used to watch cartoons all day.
grammar: Imperfeito of -ir: assistia (ele). Regular -ir imperfeito: -ia. 'O dia inteiro' = all day long.

target: A gente brincava de esconde-esconde.
english: We used to play hide and seek.
grammar: Imperfeito of -ar: brincava (a gente). 'Brincar de' + game = to play (a game). 'Esconde-esconde' = hide and seek.

target: Minha avó cozinhava muito bem.
english: My grandmother used to cook very well.
grammar: Imperfeito of -ar: cozinhava (avó). Describes a habitual ability in the past — grandma used to cook well.

target: Eu estudava nessa escola quando era criança.
english: I used to study at this school as a kid.
grammar: Imperfeito of -ar: estudava (eu). 'Quando era criança' = when I was a child — also imperfeito.

target: Eles viajavam de carro para o litoral.
english: They used to drive to the coast.
grammar: Imperfeito of -ar: viajavam (eles). Habitual action: 'they used to travel.' 'Litoral' = coast/beach area.

target: Nós jogávamos futebol na rua.
english: We used to play soccer in the street.
grammar: Imperfeito of -ar: jogávamos (nós). 'Na rua' = in the street — common for childhood memories in Brazil.

target: Eu não sabia nadar quando era pequeno.
english: I didn't know how to swim when I was young.
grammar: Imperfeito of 'saber': sabia (eu). 'Saber' in imperfeito = used to know / didn't know (ongoing state).

target: Ela tomava sorvete depois da escola.
english: She used to eat ice cream after school.
grammar: Imperfeito of -ar: tomava (ela). Habitual past: 'she used to have ice cream.' 'Sorvete' = ice cream.

target: O bairro era mais tranquilo antigamente.
english: The neighborhood used to be quieter in the old days.
grammar: The imperfeito is also used to describe how things WERE — setting the scene, painting a picture of the past.

target: Eu lia gibis o dia inteiro nas férias.
english: I used to read comics all day during vacation.
grammar: 'Gibi' is the Brazilian word for comic book. It comes from a magazine that was extremely popular in the mid-20th century.

target: A gente ia à missa todo domingo.
english: We used to go to church every Sunday.
grammar: Imperfeito of 'ir': ia (a gente). 'Ir à missa' = to go to mass. 'Todo domingo' = every Sunday.

target: Ele falava pouco quando era mais novo.
english: He used to talk little when he was younger.
grammar: Imperfeito of -ar: falava (ele). 'Quando era mais novo' = when he was younger — imperfeito for age context.

target: Vocês tinham um cachorro antes?
english: Did you used to have a dog?
grammar: Imperfeito of 'ter': tinham (vocês). 'Ter' imperfeito: tinha, tinha, tínhamos, tinham.

target: Eu queria ser astronauta quando era criança.
english: I wanted to be an astronaut as a kid.
grammar: Imperfeito of -er: queria (eu). 'Querer' imperfeito is regular: queria. 'Quando era criança' sets the past scene.

target: Ela levava os filhos à escola de manhã.
english: She used to take the kids to school in the morning.
grammar: Imperfeito of -ar: levava (ela). Habitual action: 'she used to take.' 'Levar à escola' = take to school.

target: Nós corríamos no parque todos os dias.
english: We used to run in the park every day.
grammar: Imperfeito of -er: corríamos (nós). Regular -er imperfeito nós: -íamos. 'Todos os dias' = every day.

target: O preço da gasolina era mais baixo.
english: The price of gas used to be lower.
grammar: Imperfeito of 'ser': era (o preço). 'Ser' imperfeito: era, era, éramos, eram. Describes past states.

target: Eu acordava cedo para pegar o ônibus.
english: I used to wake up early to catch the bus.
grammar: Imperfeito of -ar: acordava (eu). Habitual: 'I used to wake up early.' 'Para pegar' = to catch.

target: A gente passava as férias na casa dos avós.
english: We used to spend vacations at our grandparents' house.
grammar: Imperfeito of -ar: passava (a gente). 'Passar as férias' = to spend vacation. 'Na casa dos avós' = at grandparents' house.

target: Ele escutava rádio enquanto dirigia.
english: He used to listen to the radio while driving.
grammar: Imperfeito of -ar: escutava (ele). Two simultaneous past habits: 'escutava rádio enquanto dirigia.'

target: Vocês faziam o quê nas férias?
english: What did you used to do during vacations?
grammar: Imperfeito of -er: faziam (vocês). 'Fazer' imperfeito: fazia/faziam. 'O quê' = what (in questions).

target: Eu sonhava em morar no exterior.
english: I used to dream of living abroad.
grammar: Imperfeito of -ar: sonhava (eu). 'Sonhar em' = to dream of. Describes an ongoing past aspiration.

target: A rua era de terra naquela época.
english: The street was dirt back then.
grammar: Imperfeito of 'ser': era (a rua). Describes how things used to be. 'De terra' = unpaved/dirt.

target: Ela tinha os cabelos longos quando jovem.
english: She had long hair when she was young.
grammar: Imperfeito of 'ter': tinha (ela). Past description: 'she used to have long hair.' 'Quando jovem' = when young.

target: Nós não tínhamos celular naquela época.
english: We didn't have cell phones back then.
grammar: Imperfeito of 'ter': tínhamos (nós). 'Naquela época' = at that time — signals imperfeito.

target: Eu achava que era difícil, mas não era.
english: I thought it was hard, but it wasn't.
grammar: Imperfeito of -ar: achava (eu). 'Achar que' = to think that. Imperfeito for past beliefs that changed.

target: A gente dançava forró toda sexta à noite.
english: We used to dance forró every Friday night.
grammar: Imperfeito of -ar: dançava (a gente). 'Toda sexta à noite' = every Friday night. Habitual pattern.

target: Ele vinha nos visitar todo mês.
english: He used to visit us every month.
grammar: 'Vir' in the imperfeito: vinha, vinha, vínhamos, vinham. One of the four irregular imperfeito verbs.

target: Vocês sabiam que ela era brasileira?
english: Did you know she was Brazilian?

target: Eu tomava café com leite todo dia.
english: I used to have coffee with milk every day.

target: A cidade era menor e mais calma.
english: The city was smaller and calmer.

target: Ela trabalhava demais e não descansava.
english: She used to work too much and didn't rest.

target: Nós íamos ao cinema todo sábado.
english: We used to go to the movies every Saturday.

target: Eu gostava de brincar de pique no quintal.
english: I used to like playing tag in the backyard.

target: A gente conhecia todos os vizinhos.
english: We used to know all the neighbors.

target: Ele era o melhor jogador do time.
english: He was the best player on the team.

target: Vocês tinham algum hobby quando eram jovens?
english: Did you have any hobbies when you were young?

target: Eu pensava que sabia tudo.
english: I thought I knew everything.

target: Ela cantava no coral da igreja.
english: She used to sing in the church choir.

target: A gente nadava no rio perto de casa.
english: We used to swim in the river near home.

target: Meu pai trabalhava numa fábrica.
english: My dad used to work at a factory.

target: Eu não entendia nada de português.
english: I didn't understand any Portuguese.

target: Nós morávamos numa casa com jardim.
english: We used to live in a house with a garden.

target: A vida era mais simples naquela época.
english: Life was simpler back then.

target: Ele jogava bola descalço no campinho.
english: He used to play soccer barefoot at the field.

target: A gente colecionava figurinhas.
english: We used to collect sticker cards.
grammar: Collecting figurinhas (sticker albums, especially for the World Cup) is a beloved Brazilian tradition that spans generations.

target: Vocês se divertiam bastante naquela época?
english: Did you have a lot of fun back then?

target: Eu sempre trazia lanche de casa.
english: I always used to bring lunch from home.

target: Ela era muito curiosa quando pequena.
english: She was very curious when she was little.

target: Nós brincávamos até o sol se pôr.
english: We used to play until the sun went down.

target: Eu ia à casa dela todo fim de semana.
english: I used to go to her house every weekend.

target: A gente fazia piquenique no parque.
english: We used to have picnics in the park.

target: Ele cuidava do jardim com carinho.
english: He used to take care of the garden with love.

target: Vocês praticavam algum esporte?
english: Did you used to play any sport?

target: Eu escrevia cartas para os meus primos.
english: I used to write letters to my cousins.

target: A escola era pequena, mas todo mundo se conhecia.
english: The school was small, but everyone knew each other.

target: Ela usava uniforme na escola.
english: She used to wear a uniform at school.

target: Nós escutávamos música no rádio.
english: We used to listen to music on the radio.

target: Eu ficava ansioso antes das provas.
english: I used to get anxious before exams.

target: A gente ia à feira com a minha mãe.
english: We used to go to the market with my mom.

target: Ele vendia pipoca na praça.
english: He used to sell popcorn in the square.

target: Vocês viajavam de trem naquela época?
english: Did you used to travel by train back then?

target: Eu adorava os domingos na casa da vovó.
english: I used to love Sundays at grandma's house.

target: As férias duravam dois meses.
english: Vacations used to last two months.

target: Ela ria de tudo quando era mais nova.
english: She used to laugh at everything when she was younger.

target: Nós fazíamos a lição de casa juntos.
english: We used to do homework together.

target: Eu não gostava de verduras quando era criança.
english: I didn't like vegetables as a kid.

target: A gente viajava de ônibus para o interior.
english: We used to travel by bus to the countryside.

target: Ele tinha uma bicicleta vermelha.
english: He had a red bicycle.

target: Vocês tinham algum animal de estimação?
english: Did you use to have any pets?

target: Eu andava de skate toda tarde.
english: I used to skateboard every afternoon.

target: A praia era deserta naquela época.
english: The beach was deserted back then.

target: Ela tocava piano desde os cinco anos.
english: She played piano since the age of five.

target: Nós tínhamos pouco dinheiro, mas éramos felizes.
english: We had little money, but we were happy.

target: Eu colecionava tampinhas de garrafa.
english: I used to collect bottle caps.

target: A gente compartilhava tudo entre os irmãos.
english: We used to share everything among siblings.

target: Ele queria ser jogador de futebol.
english: He wanted to be a soccer player.

target: Vocês iam à praia de ônibus?
english: Did you used to go to the beach by bus?

target: Eu ajudava minha mãe na cozinha.
english: I used to help my mom in the kitchen.

target: A comida da cantina era boa e barata.
english: The cafeteria food was good and cheap.

target: Ela desenhava muito bem quando era criança.
english: She used to draw really well as a kid.

target: Nós víamos os fogos de artifício do terraço.
english: We used to watch the fireworks from the rooftop.

target: Eu sabia todas as músicas de cor.
english: I used to know all the songs by heart.

target: A gente alugava filmes na locadora.
english: We used to rent movies at the video store.

target: Ele vinha de trem para a escola.
english: He used to come to school by train.

target: Vocês cozinhavam ou comiam fora?
english: Did you used to cook or eat out?

target: Eu precisava estudar mais naquela época.
english: I needed to study more back then.

target: O trânsito não era tão ruim antes.
english: Traffic wasn't as bad before.

target: Ela sonhava em ser cantora.
english: She used to dream of being a singer.

target: Nós voltávamos para casa à noite.
english: We used to go back home at night.

target: Eu adorava as festas juninas.
english: I used to love the June festivals.
grammar: Festas juninas are traditional June celebrations in Brazil with square dancing (quadrilha), corn-based foods, and bonfires. Huge cultural event.

target: A gente ia pescar no rio aos domingos.
english: We used to go fishing in the river on Sundays.

target: Ele morava num sítio no interior.
english: He used to live on a small farm in the countryside.

target: Vocês gostavam de estudar?
english: Did you used to like studying?

target: Eu caminhava até a escola com os amigos.
english: I used to walk to school with my friends.

target: O supermercado ficava mais longe daqui.
english: The supermarket used to be farther from here.

target: Ela fazia artesanato para vender.
english: She used to make crafts to sell.

target: Nós podíamos brincar na rua sem preocupação.
english: We could play in the street without worry.

target: Eu passava horas lendo gibis.
english: I used to spend hours reading comics.

target: A gente comia bolo de milho na festa junina.
english: We used to eat corn cake at the June festival.

target: Ele ia à padaria buscar pão de manhã.
english: He used to go to the bakery to get bread in the morning.

target: Vocês frequentavam alguma academia?
english: Did you used to go to any gym?

target: Eu lavava o carro todo sábado.
english: I used to wash the car every Saturday.

target: A escola tinha um pátio enorme.
english: The school had a huge courtyard.

target: Ela corria maratona naquela época.
english: She used to run marathons back then.

target: Nós saíamos para dançar toda sexta.
english: We used to go out dancing every Friday.

target: Eu ia à casa dele jogar videogame.
english: I used to go to his house to play video games.

target: A gente tomava banho de chuva no verão.
english: We used to shower in the rain in summer.

target: Ele era muito magro quando era jovem.
english: He was very thin when he was young.

target: Vocês tinham aula de música na escola?
english: Did you have music class at school?

target: Eu bebia muito suco de caju.
english: I used to drink a lot of cashew juice.

target: As ruas eram mais limpas antes.
english: The streets used to be cleaner before.

target: Ela sempre chegava atrasada.
english: She always used to arrive late.

target: Nós fazíamos trilha na serra.
english: We used to hike in the mountains.

target: Eu queria muito ter um irmão.
english: I really wanted to have a sibling.

target: A gente se reunia na casa da vovó.
english: We used to gather at grandma's house.

target: Ele praticava capoeira desde criança.
english: He practiced capoeira since childhood.

target: Vocês moravam perto da escola?
english: Did you used to live near the school?

target: Eu cozinhava todos os dias para a família.
english: I used to cook every day for the family.

target: O parque era o lugar favorito das crianças.
english: The park was the kids' favorite place.

target: Ela pedia presente de Natal por carta.
english: She used to ask for Christmas presents by letter.

target: Nós acampávamos perto do rio.
english: We used to camp near the river.

target: Eu assistia novela com a minha mãe.
english: I used to watch soap operas with my mom.
grammar: Brazilian telenovelas are a massive cultural phenomenon. The nightly novela was traditionally a time for the whole family to gather in front of the TV.

target: A gente conversava com os vizinhos na calçada.
english: We used to chat with the neighbors on the sidewalk.

target: Ele desenhava mapas de tesouros imaginários.
english: He used to draw maps of imaginary treasures.

target: Vocês iam acampar com frequência?
english: Did you used to go camping often?

target: Eu tinha vergonha de falar em público.
english: I used to be embarrassed to speak in public.

target: A casa era grande e tinha um quintal enorme.
english: The house was big and had a huge backyard.

target: Ela estudava à noite e trabalhava de dia.
english: She used to study at night and work during the day.

target: Nós comíamos pamonha na época da colheita.
english: We used to eat pamonha during harvest time.
grammar: Pamonha is a traditional Brazilian snack made from fresh corn wrapped in corn husks. Very popular in the countryside and during festas juninas.

target: Eu sentia falta de casa quando viajava.
english: I used to miss home when I traveled.

target: A gente cuidava uns dos outros.
english: We used to take care of each other.

target: Ele trabalhava de garçom para pagar a faculdade.
english: He used to work as a waiter to pay for college.

target: Vocês comiam muita fruta quando eram pequenos?
english: Did you eat a lot of fruit when you were little?

target: Eu dormia com a luz acesa.
english: I used to sleep with the light on.

target: A gente empinava pipa no morro.
english: We used to fly kites on the hill.
grammar: 'Empinar pipa' (flying kites) is a beloved Brazilian childhood activity, especially in favelas and working-class neighborhoods.

target: Ela ia de bicicleta para todo lugar.
english: She used to go everywhere by bicycle.

target: Nós ríamos de tudo naquela época.
english: We used to laugh at everything back then.

target: Eu estava dormindo quando o telefone tocou.
english: I was sleeping when the phone rang.
grammar: Classic past contrast: imperfeito sets the scene (was sleeping = ongoing), perfeito interrupts it (rang = sudden event).

target: Ela morava no Rio quando conheceu o marido.
english: She was living in Rio when she met her husband.
grammar: Past contrast: 'morava' (imperfeito, ongoing) + 'conheceu' (perfeito, punctual). Background + event.

target: Chovia muito quando a gente chegou.
english: It was raining a lot when we arrived.
grammar: Weather in the imperfeito describes the backdrop: 'chovia' = it was raining (the setting). The arrival (chegou) happened at a specific moment.

target: Eu trabalhava num banco, mas decidi mudar de carreira.
english: I was working at a bank, but I decided to change careers.
grammar: Past contrast: 'trabalhava' (imperfeito, habitual past) + 'decidi' (perfeito, completed decision).

target: Enquanto ele cozinhava, eu arrumei a mesa.
english: While he was cooking, I set the table.
grammar: 'Enquanto' + imperfeito = while something was happening. The completed action uses perfeito.

target: A gente caminhava na praia quando viu o golfinho.
english: We were walking on the beach when we saw the dolphin.
grammar: Past contrast: 'caminhava' (imperfeito, ongoing action) + 'viu' (perfeito, sudden event). 'Quando' often triggers this pattern.

target: Eu sempre tomava café às sete, mas ontem tomei às oito.
english: I always used to have coffee at seven, but yesterday I had it at eight.
grammar: Imperfeito for habitual routine (tomava = used to), perfeito for a specific one-time event (tomei = I had, just once).

target: Ele estudava quando a luz acabou.
english: He was studying when the power went out.
grammar: 'Estudava' (ongoing) + 'acabou' (interruption). 'A luz acabou' = the power went out — sudden event.

target: Nós morávamos em Curitiba, mas nos mudamos para São Paulo.
english: We were living in Curitiba, but we moved to São Paulo.
grammar: Two completed events in sequence: 'morávamos' (ongoing state) + 'nos mudamos' (completed change).

target: Eu ia sair quando ela ligou.
english: I was about to leave when she called.
grammar: 'Ia' + infinitive in the imperfeito means 'was about to' or 'was going to.' The perfeito (ligou) interrupts the intention.

target: Fazia calor e o sol brilhava quando saímos de casa.
english: It was hot and the sun was shining when we left the house.
grammar: Background description in imperfeito: 'fazia calor e o sol brilhava' sets the scene for 'saímos' (event).

target: Ela queria um sorvete, então parou no quiosque.
english: She wanted an ice cream, so she stopped at the kiosk.
grammar: 'Queria' (imperfeito, desire/state) + 'parou' (perfeito, action taken). Desire → action pattern.

target: Enquanto vocês dormiam, eu fiz o café da manhã.
english: While you were sleeping, I made breakfast.
grammar: 'Enquanto dormiam' (imperfeito, ongoing) + 'fiz' (perfeito, completed). 'Enquanto' almost always takes imperfeito.

target: Eu não sabia que ele estava aqui até que o vi.
english: I didn't know he was here until I saw him.
grammar: 'Sabia' (imperfeito, state of not knowing) + 'vi' (perfeito, moment of seeing). Discovery interrupted a state.

target: A gente conversava quando o chefe entrou.
english: We were chatting when the boss walked in.
grammar: 'Conversava' (imperfeito, ongoing) + 'entrou' (perfeito, interruption). Classic background + interruption.

target: Ele jogava futebol todo sábado, mas parou.
english: He used to play soccer every Saturday, but he stopped.
grammar: 'Jogava' (imperfeito, habitual) + 'parou' (perfeito, ended). A past habit that was completed/stopped.

target: O restaurante estava cheio quando chegamos.
english: The restaurant was full when we arrived.
grammar: 'Estava cheio' (imperfeito, description) + 'chegamos' (perfeito, event). Scene + arrival pattern.

target: Enquanto eu falava, ela anotava tudo.
english: While I was talking, she was writing everything down.
grammar: Two simultaneous ongoing actions both use imperfeito: 'falava' and 'anotava' were happening at the same time.

target: Nós íamos para a praia, mas começou a chover.
english: We were heading to the beach, but it started to rain.
grammar: 'Íamos' (imperfeito, intention/plan) + 'começou' (perfeito, interruption). Plans interrupted by events.

target: Eu pensava que era fácil, mas descobri que não era.
english: I thought it was easy, but I found out it wasn't.
grammar: 'Pensava' (imperfeito, ongoing belief) + 'descobri' (perfeito, completed discovery). Belief changed.

target: Ela trabalhava de garçonete quando recebeu a oferta de emprego.
english: She was working as a waitress when she received the job offer.
grammar: 'Trabalhava' (imperfeito, ongoing job) + 'recebeu' (perfeito, received). Career background + event.

target: Todo mundo dançava quando a música parou.
english: Everyone was dancing when the music stopped.
grammar: 'Dançava' (imperfeito, everyone was dancing) + 'parou' (perfeito, music stopped). Ongoing + sudden.

target: Eu sempre almoçava em casa, mas hoje almocei no restaurante.
english: I always used to eat lunch at home, but today I ate at the restaurant.
grammar: 'Almoçava' (imperfeito, habitual) + 'almocei' (perfeito, today's specific event). Habit vs. single action.

target: A gente assistia TV quando ouviu o barulho.
english: We were watching TV when we heard the noise.
grammar: 'Assistia' (imperfeito, ongoing) + 'ouviu' (perfeito, sudden). Classic 'was doing X when Y happened.'

target: Ele queria ir ao show, mas não conseguiu ingresso.
english: He wanted to go to the concert, but couldn't get a ticket.

target: Enquanto eu cozinhava, as crianças brincavam no quintal.
english: While I was cooking, the kids were playing in the backyard.

target: Eu corria no parque quando torci o tornozelo.
english: I was running in the park when I sprained my ankle.

target: O dia estava lindo quando saímos para passear.
english: The day was beautiful when we went out for a walk.

target: Ela lia um livro quando adormeceu.
english: She was reading a book when she fell asleep.

target: Nós viajávamos de carro, mas trocamos para avião.
english: We used to travel by car, but we switched to plane.

target: Eu ia te ligar quando você apareceu.
english: I was going to call you when you showed up.

target: Enquanto ele esperava, leu o jornal inteiro.
english: While he was waiting, he read the entire newspaper.

target: A gente nadava no mar quando viu a tartaruga.
english: We were swimming in the sea when we saw the turtle.

target: Eu achava que ia chover, mas o dia ficou bonito.
english: I thought it was going to rain, but the day turned out nice.

target: Ela estava triste, mas a notícia a alegrou.
english: She was sad, but the news cheered her up.

target: Enquanto a gente jantava, ele contou a novidade.
english: While we were having dinner, he shared the news.

target: O bebê dormia tranquilo quando o cachorro latiu.
english: The baby was sleeping peacefully when the dog barked.

target: Eu conhecia o caminho, mas mesmo assim me perdi.
english: I knew the way, but I still got lost.

target: Nós esperávamos bom tempo, mas nevou.
english: We were expecting good weather, but it snowed.

target: Ele sempre levava guarda-chuva, mas justo hoje esqueceu.
english: He always used to bring an umbrella, but today of all days he forgot.

target: Enquanto eu dirigia, meu filho dormiu no banco de trás.
english: While I was driving, my son fell asleep in the back seat.

target: A gente olhava o cardápio quando o garçom veio.
english: We were looking at the menu when the waiter came.

target: Eu queria viajar no feriado, mas tive que trabalhar.
english: I wanted to travel on the holiday, but I had to work.

target: Ela usava óculos, mas fez cirurgia e não precisa mais.
english: She used to wear glasses, but had surgery and doesn't need them anymore.

target: Enquanto vocês viajavam, cuidei das plantas.
english: While you were traveling, I took care of the plants.

target: O show já tinha começado quando nós chegamos.
english: The show had already started when we arrived.

target: Eu ia comprar o ingresso, mas esgotou.
english: I was going to buy the ticket, but it sold out.

target: A gente andava pelo centro quando começou a chover.
english: We were walking through downtown when it started to rain.

target: Ele era tímido, mas quando subiu no palco, brilhou.
english: He was shy, but when he got on stage, he shone.

target: Enquanto eu tomava banho, o telefone tocou três vezes.
english: While I was showering, the phone rang three times.

target: Nós não sabíamos que eles iam vir, e foi uma surpresa.
english: We didn't know they were coming, and it was a surprise.

target: O sol se punha quando a gente chegou ao mirante.
english: The sun was setting when we reached the lookout.

target: Eu precisava de ajuda e o vizinho me ajudou.
english: I needed help and the neighbor helped me.

target: Ela pensava em desistir, mas mudou de ideia.
english: She was thinking of giving up, but changed her mind.

target: Enquanto eu estudava, meu irmão jogava videogame.
english: While I was studying, my brother was playing video games.

target: A gente esperava o ônibus quando começou a trovejar.
english: We were waiting for the bus when it started to thunder.

target: Eu sempre ia de metrô, mas ontem fui de carro.
english: I always used to take the subway, but yesterday I drove.

target: Ele falava no telefone quando eu cheguei.
english: He was talking on the phone when I arrived.

target: Nós estávamos com fome quando encontramos o restaurante.
english: We were hungry when we found the restaurant.

target: Enquanto a mãe cozinhava, o pai lavava a louça.
english: While the mom was cooking, the dad was washing the dishes.

target: Eu achava que conhecia a cidade, mas me surpreendi.
english: I thought I knew the city, but I was surprised.

target: A gente passava férias no litoral todo ano, até que mudamos.
english: We used to vacation on the coast every year, until we moved.

target: Ela estudava medicina, mas trocou para engenharia.
english: She was studying medicine, but switched to engineering.

target: Eu ia ao mercado quando lembrei que já tinha leite.
english: I was going to the store when I remembered I already had milk.

target: Enquanto chovia, a gente jogou cartas.
english: While it was raining, we played cards.

target: O trânsito estava parado e a gente perdeu o voo.
english: Traffic was at a standstill and we missed the flight.

target: Ele fazia academia todo dia, mas machucou o joelho e parou.
english: He used to work out every day, but injured his knee and stopped.

target: Enquanto ela cantava, todo mundo aplaudiu.
english: While she was singing, everyone applauded.

target: Eu lia o livro quando percebi que era muito tarde.
english: I was reading the book when I realized it was very late.

target: A gente comia tranquilo quando alguém gritou.
english: We were eating calmly when someone screamed.

target: Nós não queríamos sair, mas fomos mesmo assim.
english: We didn't want to go out, but we went anyway.

target: Enquanto eu escrevia o e-mail, o computador travou.
english: While I was writing the email, the computer froze.

target: Ela já sabia a resposta quando ele perguntou.
english: She already knew the answer when he asked.

target: Eu estava no banho quando a campainha tocou.
english: I was in the shower when the doorbell rang.

target: A gente planejava uma festa quando ele apareceu de surpresa.
english: We were planning a party when he showed up by surprise.

target: Ele trabalhava como freelancer antes de ser contratado.
english: He worked as a freelancer before he was hired.

target: Enquanto a gente esperava, tomamos um café.
english: While we were waiting, we had a coffee.

target: Eu sabia que ia dar errado, e deu.
english: I knew it was going to go wrong, and it did.

target: Ela morava sozinha antes de casar.
english: She used to live alone before getting married.

target: Enquanto eu pagava a conta, ele foi buscar o carro.
english: While I was paying the bill, he went to get the car.

target: Eu me levanto às seis da manhã.
english: I get up at six in the morning.
grammar: Reflexive verbs use 'me, se, nos, se' before the verb in Brazilian Portuguese. 'Levantar-se' → 'eu me levanto.' The pronoun goes BEFORE the verb in Brazil.

target: Ela se veste rápido para não se atrasar.
english: She gets dressed quickly so she won't be late.
grammar: 'Se vestir' = to get dressed. 'Se atrasar' = to be late. Both reflexive — the action affects oneself.

target: Nós nos encontramos no café às três.
english: We meet at the café at three.
grammar: 'Encontrar-se' is reciprocal — 'nos encontramos' means we meet each other. Reflexive verbs can be truly reflexive (self) or reciprocal (each other).

target: Vocês se divertiram na festa?
english: Did you have fun at the party?
grammar: 'Se divertir' past: 'se divertiram.' The reflexive pronoun 'se' stays before the verb in statements.

target: Eu me chamo Lucas.
english: My name is Lucas.
grammar: 'Chamar-se' literally means 'to call oneself.' 'Me chamo Lucas' = I call myself Lucas. The most basic reflexive verb you'll learn.

target: Ele se sentou perto da janela.
english: He sat down near the window.
grammar: 'Se sentar' = to sit down. Past: 'se sentou.' The action of seating oneself — reflexive.

target: A gente se preocupa demais com bobagem.
english: We worry too much about silly things.
grammar: 'Se preocupar com' = to worry about. 'Demais' = too much. Reflexive: the worry affects yourself.

target: Eu me mudei para São Paulo há dois anos.
english: I moved to São Paulo two years ago.
grammar: 'Se mudar' = to move (house). Different from 'mudar' = to change. 'Há dois anos' = two years ago.

target: Ela se maquiou rapidinho antes de sair.
english: She put on makeup quickly before leaving.
grammar: 'Se maquiar' = to put on makeup. 'Rapidinho' = really quickly — diminutive of 'rápido' used as adverb.

target: Nós nos conhecemos numa festa.
english: We met each other at a party.
grammar: 'Nos conhecemos' — reciprocal: we met each other. 'Numa festa' = at a party. 'Nos' = ourselves/each other.

target: Vocês se lembram do aniversário dele?
english: Do you remember his birthday?
grammar: 'Lembrar-se de' means 'to remember.' The 'se' makes it reflexive. You can also say 'lembrar de' without 'se' — both are correct in Brazil.

target: Eu me deito cedo durante a semana.
english: I go to bed early during the week.
grammar: 'Me deitar' = to lie down / go to bed. 'Me deito' — first person, pronoun before verb.

target: Ele se barbeia todas as manhãs.
english: He shaves every morning.
grammar: 'Se barbear' = to shave. 'Todas as manhãs' = every morning. Daily routine verb, naturally reflexive.

target: A gente se despediu e foi embora.
english: We said goodbye and left.
grammar: 'Se despedir' = to say goodbye. Reciprocal here: 'a gente se despediu' = we said goodbye to each other.

target: Eu me esqueço de tudo o tempo todo.
english: I forget everything all the time.
grammar: 'Esquecer-se de' is the reflexive form of 'to forget.' Both 'eu me esqueço' and 'eu esqueço' are used in Brazil with no difference in meaning.

target: Ela se formou em administração.
english: She graduated with a degree in business administration.
grammar: 'Se formar em' = to graduate in/from. 'Administração' = business administration. 'Se' = oneself.

target: Nós nos divertimos muito no fim de semana.
english: We had a lot of fun on the weekend.
grammar: 'Nos divertimos' — reciprocal/reflexive: we had fun. 'Nos' before verb. 'No fim de semana' = on the weekend.

target: Vocês se acostumaram com o calor?
english: Did you get used to the heat?
grammar: 'Acostumar-se com' means 'to get used to.' Very useful for expats and travelers adjusting to life in Brazil.

target: Eu me arrumei para a entrevista.
english: I got ready for the interview.
grammar: 'Me arrumar' = to get ready / to dress up. 'Para a entrevista' = for the interview. Reflexive: preparing oneself.

target: Ele se machucou jogando futebol.
english: He got hurt playing soccer.

target: A gente se encontra toda semana para um café.
english: We get together every week for a coffee.

target: Eu me inscrevi no curso de culinária.
english: I enrolled in the cooking course.

target: Ela se olhou no espelho e sorriu.
english: She looked at herself in the mirror and smiled.

target: Nós nos casamos há cinco anos.
english: We got married five years ago.

target: Vocês se interessam por música brasileira?
english: Are you interested in Brazilian music?

target: Eu me sinto em casa aqui.
english: I feel at home here.
grammar: 'Sentir-se' means 'to feel' (about oneself). 'Me sinto bem' = I feel good. 'Me sinto mal' = I feel bad. Very common reflexive verb.

target: Ele se exercita todos os dias.
english: He exercises every day.

target: A gente se separou no shopping e depois se encontrou.
english: We separated at the mall and then met up again.

target: Eu me queimei no fogão.
english: I burned myself on the stove.

target: Ela se arrependeu de não ter ido.
english: She regretted not having gone.
grammar: 'Arrepender-se de' means 'to regret.' This verb is ALWAYS reflexive in Portuguese — there's no non-reflexive form.

target: Nós nos adaptamos rápido à nova cidade.
english: We adapted quickly to the new city.

target: Vocês se comunicam em português?
english: Do you communicate in Portuguese?

target: Eu me perdi no centro da cidade.
english: I got lost downtown.

target: Ele se cansou de esperar e foi embora.
english: He got tired of waiting and left.

target: A gente se abraçou e chorou de felicidade.
english: We hugged each other and cried with happiness.

target: Eu me cortei fazendo a barba.
english: I cut myself shaving.

target: Ela se queixa do barulho dos vizinhos.
english: She complains about the neighbors' noise.

target: Nós nos preparamos para a viagem.
english: We got ready for the trip.

target: Vocês se deram bem na empresa nova?
english: Did you get along well at the new company?
grammar: 'Dar-se bem' means 'to get along well.' 'Dar-se mal' = to not get along. Very common reflexive expression.

target: Eu me apaixonei pelo Brasil.
english: I fell in love with Brazil.

target: Ele se concentra melhor de manhã.
english: He concentrates better in the morning.

target: A gente se vê amanhã então.
english: We'll see each other tomorrow then.

target: Eu me molhei inteiro na chuva.
english: I got completely soaked in the rain.

target: Ela se dedica muito ao trabalho.
english: She dedicates herself a lot to work.

target: Nós nos demos conta do erro.
english: We realized the mistake.
grammar: 'Dar-se conta de' means 'to realize.' A very common reflexive expression in both formal and informal Portuguese.

target: Vocês se ofereceram para ajudar?
english: Did you offer to help?

target: Eu me tranquei fora de casa sem querer.
english: I accidentally locked myself out.

target: Ele se aposentou ano passado.
english: He retired last year.

target: A gente se reúne todo mês.
english: We get together every month.

target: Eu me arrependo de não ter estudado mais.
english: I regret not having studied more.

target: Ela se recuperou rápido da cirurgia.
english: She recovered quickly from the surgery.

target: Nós nos perdemos no caminho para o hotel.
english: We got lost on the way to the hotel.

target: Vocês se lembram de mim?
english: Do you remember me?

target: Eu me sinto melhor depois de comer.
english: I feel better after eating.

target: Ele se orgulha dos filhos.
english: He's proud of his children.

target: A gente se ajuda nos momentos difíceis.
english: We help each other in tough times.

target: Eu me matriculei na academia.
english: I signed up at the gym.

target: Ela se surpreendeu com a festa surpresa.
english: She was surprised by the surprise party.

target: Nós nos mudamos três vezes em cinco anos.
english: We moved three times in five years.

target: Vocês se interessaram pelo projeto?
english: Were you interested in the project?

target: Eu me visto em cinco minutos.
english: I get dressed in five minutes.

target: Ele se acordou com o despertador.
english: He woke up with the alarm.

target: A gente se respeita muito.
english: We respect each other a lot.

target: Eu me senti acolhido no Brasil.
english: I felt welcomed in Brazil.

target: Ela se demitiu do emprego.
english: She quit her job.
grammar: 'Demitir-se' means to resign/quit (reflexive). 'Ser demitido' means to be fired (passive). The reflexive implies it was YOUR choice.

target: Nós nos sentamos na primeira fila.
english: We sat in the first row.

target: Vocês se deram bem com os vizinhos novos?
english: Did you get along with the new neighbors?

target: Eu me confundi com as ruas.
english: I got confused with the streets.

target: Ele se comporta bem na escola.
english: He behaves well at school.

target: A gente se conhece há muito tempo.
english: We've known each other for a long time.

target: Eu me protejo do sol com chapéu.
english: I protect myself from the sun with a hat.

target: Ela se comprometeu a entregar na sexta.
english: She committed to delivering on Friday.

target: Nós nos despedimos com um abraço.
english: We said goodbye with a hug.

target: Vocês se divertem com jogos de tabuleiro?
english: Do you have fun with board games?

target: Eu me surpreendi com a comida daqui.
english: I was surprised by the food here.

target: Ele se levantou cedo para ver o nascer do sol.
english: He got up early to see the sunrise.

target: A gente se comunica pelo WhatsApp.
english: We communicate through WhatsApp.

target: Eu me apressei para não perder o voo.
english: I hurried so I wouldn't miss the flight.

target: Ela se emociona fácil com filmes.
english: She gets emotional easily with movies.

target: Nós nos organizamos para a mudança.
english: We organized ourselves for the move.

target: Eu fiz isso para ajudar você.
english: I did this to help you.
grammar: 'Para' expresses purpose — WHY you do something. 'Para ajudar' = in order to help. Think of 'para' as 'for the purpose of.'

target: Passamos por várias cidades durante a viagem.
english: We passed through several cities during the trip.
grammar: 'Por' expresses movement THROUGH a place. 'Passar por' = to pass through. Think of 'por' as 'through/via.'

target: Esse presente é para a minha mãe.
english: This gift is for my mom.
grammar: 'Para' for recipient — WHO something is for. 'Para a minha mãe' = for my mom.

target: Ele foi demitido por causa do atraso.
english: He was fired because of being late.
grammar: 'Por causa de' means 'because of.' 'Por' expresses cause/reason — WHY something happened.

target: Eu estudo para passar na prova.
english: I study to pass the exam.

target: A gente andou pela praia o dia inteiro.
english: We walked along the beach all day.
grammar: 'Pela' is the contraction of 'por + a' (through/along the). 'Pelo' = por + o. These contractions are mandatory.

target: Comprei flores para decorar a sala.
english: I bought flowers to decorate the living room.

target: Obrigado por tudo.
english: Thank you for everything.
grammar: 'Obrigado por' = thank you FOR. Use 'por' when thanking someone for something they already did. It's about the reason for your gratitude.

target: Para mim, café é essencial de manhã.
english: For me, coffee is essential in the morning.
grammar: 'Para mim' = for me (opinion/benefit). Don't confuse with 'por mim' = as far as I'm concerned / on my behalf.

target: Eu pago cem reais por mês de internet.
english: I pay a hundred reais a month for internet.
grammar: 'Por' for rate/exchange: 'por mês' = per month, 'por dia' = per day, 'por hora' = per hour.

target: Ela trabalha para sustentar a família.
english: She works to support her family.

target: O pacote foi enviado por correio.
english: The package was sent by mail.
grammar: 'Por' for means/method — BY what means something was done. 'Por correio' = by mail, 'por telefone' = by phone.

target: Para quando você precisa disso?
english: By when do you need this?
grammar: 'Para' for deadlines: 'para quando' = by when, 'para amanhã' = by tomorrow, 'para sexta' = by Friday.

target: Eu vim por causa dela.
english: I came because of her.

target: Esse livro é bom para aprender português.
english: This book is good for learning Portuguese.

target: Ele ligou por engano.
english: He called by mistake.

target: Eu vou para o Brasil mês que vem.
english: I'm going to Brazil next month.
grammar: 'Para' for destination with intention to stay: 'vou para o Brasil' implies going there for a while. 'Vou ao Brasil' is more neutral.

target: A gente passou pela casa dela, mas ela não estava.
english: We stopped by her house, but she wasn't there.

target: Para ser sincero, eu não gostei.
english: To be honest, I didn't like it.

target: Ela lutou por seus direitos.
english: She fought for her rights.
grammar: 'Por' for 'on behalf of' or 'in defense of': 'lutar por' = to fight for, 'votar por' = to vote for.

target: Eu trouxe comida para a viagem.
english: I brought food for the trip.

target: Ele me ligou por curiosidade.
english: He called me out of curiosity.

target: Estou guardando dinheiro para comprar uma casa.
english: I'm saving money to buy a house.

target: A notícia se espalhou por todo o país.
english: The news spread throughout the entire country.

target: Não tenho paciência para esperar mais.
english: I don't have patience to wait any longer.

target: Ele foi escolhido por unanimidade.
english: He was chosen unanimously.

target: Para onde vocês vão nas férias?
english: Where are you going on vacation?
grammar: 'Para onde' = to where (destination with purpose). Used when asking about travel destinations.

target: Eu tenho interesse por história brasileira.
english: I'm interested in Brazilian history.

target: Ela saiu para comprar remédio.
english: She went out to buy medicine.

target: A gente esperou por duas horas.
english: We waited for two hours.
grammar: 'Por' for duration: 'por duas horas' = for two hours. Indicates HOW LONG something lasted.

target: Para variar, ele chegou atrasado.
english: For a change, he arrived late.

target: O quadro foi pintado por um artista famoso.
english: The painting was painted by a famous artist.
grammar: 'Por' in passive voice — identifies WHO performed the action. 'Pintado por' = painted by.

target: Eu preciso terminar isso para amanhã.
english: I need to finish this by tomorrow.

target: Nós trocamos a TV por uma maior.
english: We exchanged the TV for a bigger one.
grammar: 'Por' for exchange/substitution: 'trocar A por B' = to exchange A for B.

target: Para uma pessoa tímida, ela fala muito bem em público.
english: For a shy person, she speaks very well in public.
grammar: 'Para' for 'considering/for': 'para uma pessoa tímida' = for a shy person (considering that she's shy).

target: Ele viajou por negócios.
english: He traveled for business.

target: Eu comprei uma bolsa para levar ao trabalho.
english: I bought a bag to take to work.

target: A carta foi assinada por todos os funcionários.
english: The letter was signed by all the employees.

target: Para mim, a coisa mais importante é a família.
english: For me, the most important thing is family.

target: Eu optei por ficar em casa.
english: I opted to stay home.
grammar: 'Optar por' means 'to opt for/choose.' Some verbs always take 'por': optar por, lutar por, pagar por.

target: Ela está se preparando para o concurso.
english: She's preparing for the civil service exam.

target: A gente passou por momentos difíceis.
english: We went through tough times.

target: Isso é bom demais para ser verdade.
english: This is too good to be true.

target: Ele foi preso por roubo.
english: He was arrested for robbery.

target: Estou aqui para ajudar no que precisar.
english: I'm here to help with whatever you need.

target: Mandei a encomenda por Sedex.
english: I sent the package by express mail.

target: Para que serve esse aplicativo?
english: What is this app for?
grammar: 'Para que serve' = what is it for / what is it used for. 'Para' here indicates purpose and function.

target: Ela tem paixão por culinária.
english: She has a passion for cooking.

target: Eu trouxe um casaco para o caso de esfriar.
english: I brought a jacket in case it gets cold.

target: A reunião foi adiada por falta de quórum.
english: The meeting was postponed due to lack of quorum.
grammar: 'Por falta de' = due to the lack of. 'Por' here expresses the reason/cause for the postponement.

target: Ela me ligou ontem à noite.
english: She called me last night.
grammar: 'Me' is the direct/indirect object pronoun for 'eu.' In Brazilian Portuguese, it goes BEFORE the verb: 'ela me ligou,' not 'ela ligou-me.'

target: Eu te falei sobre isso ontem.
english: I told you about this yesterday.
grammar: 'Te' is the object pronoun for 'tu/você' (informal). In Brazil, 'te' is used with 'você' even though grammatically it belongs to 'tu.'

target: A gente o viu no supermercado.
english: We saw him at the supermarket.
grammar: 'O' is the direct object pronoun for 'ele' (him). 'A' for 'ela' (her). In casual Brazilian speech, many people say 'ele' instead of 'o.'

target: Eu vou te mandar o endereço agora.
english: I'll send you the address now.
grammar: 'Te' = you (informal direct/indirect object). 'Vou te mandar' — pronoun before infinitive in spoken Brazilian Portuguese.

target: Ele nos convidou para o churrasco.
english: He invited us to the barbecue.
grammar: 'Nos' is the object pronoun for 'nós' (us). It also goes before the verb in Brazilian Portuguese.

target: Eu lhe dei o recado.
english: I gave him/her the message.
grammar: 'Lhe' is the indirect object pronoun meaning 'to him/her/you.' 'Eu lhe dei' = I gave to him/her. More formal than 'dei para ele.'

target: Ela me pediu um favor.
english: She asked me a favor.
grammar: 'Me' = to me (indirect object). 'Pedir' takes indirect object: 'pediu-me' or 'me pediu' = asked me.

target: Eu não te entendi, pode repetir?
english: I didn't understand you, can you repeat?
grammar: 'Te' as direct object: 'não te entendi' = I didn't understand you. Pronoun before verb in negatives.

target: A gente os encontrou na praia.
english: We found them at the beach.
grammar: 'Os' is the masculine plural direct object pronoun (them). 'As' is the feminine plural. In casual speech, 'eles/elas' is more common.

target: Ele me ajudou muito com a mudança.
english: He helped me a lot with the move.
grammar: 'Me' as direct object: 'me ajudou' = helped me. In spoken BP, pronouns go before the verb.

target: Eu te amo.
english: I love you.
grammar: 'Te amo' — the most direct way to say 'I love you.' 'Te' as direct object. Very common and natural.

target: Ela nos mostrou o caminho.
english: She showed us the way.
grammar: 'Nos' = us (indirect object). 'Nos mostrou' = showed us. 'Nos' functions as both direct and indirect.

target: Eu vou te esperar na porta.
english: I'll wait for you at the door.
grammar: 'Te' with future: 'vou te esperar' = I'm going to wait for you. Pronoun sits between 'ir' and infinitive.

target: Ele me deu um presente de aniversário.
english: He gave me a birthday present.
grammar: 'Me' as indirect object: 'me deu um presente' = gave me a gift. 'De aniversário' = for birthday.

target: A gente lhes explicou a situação.
english: We explained the situation to them.
grammar: 'Lhes' is the plural indirect object pronoun (to them/to you all). In casual speech, 'para eles' or 'para vocês' is more common.

target: Eu te mandei uma mensagem, viu?
english: I sent you a message, okay?
grammar: 'Te' as indirect: 'te mandei' = I sent you. 'Viu?' at the end = informal 'you know?' / 'okay?'

target: Ela me contou tudo sobre a viagem.
english: She told me everything about the trip.
grammar: 'Me' as indirect: 'me contou' = told me. 'Contar' takes indirect object for the listener.

target: Eu não o conheço pessoalmente.
english: I don't know him personally.
grammar: 'O' = him (formal direct object). 'Não o conheço' — 'o' is formal; spoken BP often uses 'ele': 'não conheço ele.'

target: Ele nos levou de carro até o aeroporto.
english: He drove us to the airport.
grammar: 'Nos' as direct object: 'nos levou' = took us. 'De carro' = by car. 'Até' = all the way to.

target: Eu te agradeço pela ajuda.
english: I thank you for the help.
grammar: 'Te agradeço' = I thank you. 'Agradecer' + direct object pronoun. 'Pela ajuda' = for the help.

target: A gente me pediu para ficar mais um pouco.
english: They asked me to stay a little longer.
grammar: Note: this should be 'Eles me pediram' — 'pedir' takes indirect object. 'Para ficar' = to stay.

target: Eu lhe desejo muita sorte.
english: I wish you a lot of luck.
grammar: 'Lhe' = to you (formal indirect). 'Lhe desejo' = I wish you. More formal than 'te desejo.'

target: Ela me fez uma surpresa.
english: She surprised me.
grammar: 'Me' as indirect: 'me fez uma surpresa' = made/gave me a surprise. 'Fazer surpresa' = to surprise.

target: Eu te levo de carro.
english: I'll drive you.
grammar: 'Te levo' = I'll take you. 'Te' as direct object. 'De carro' = by car.

target: Ele nos ensinou a cozinhar feijoada.
english: He taught us how to cook feijoada.
grammar: 'Nos' as indirect: 'nos ensinou' = taught us. 'A cozinhar' — 'ensinar a' + infinitive = to teach to.

target: Eu vou te buscar às oito.
english: I'll pick you up at eight.
grammar: 'Te buscar' = to pick you up. 'Buscar' = to fetch/pick up. 'Às oito' = at eight o'clock.

target: Ela me deixou esperando meia hora.
english: She left me waiting for half an hour.
grammar: 'Me' as direct: 'me deixou esperando' = left me waiting. 'Meia hora' = half an hour.

target: A gente os chamou para jantar.
english: We invited them for dinner.
grammar: 'Os' = them (masculine direct object). 'Os chamou' = called them. More formal; spoken: 'chamou eles.'

target: Eu te disse para não se preocupar.
english: I told you not to worry.
grammar: 'Te disse' = I told you. 'Te' as indirect object. 'Para não se preocupar' = not to worry.

target: Ele me emprestou o carro dele.
english: He lent me his car.
grammar: 'Me' as indirect: 'me emprestou' = lent me. 'O carro dele' = his car. 'Dele' = de + ele.

target: Eu lhe peço desculpas pelo atraso.
english: I apologize to you for the delay.
grammar: 'Lhe' = to you (formal). 'Peço desculpas' = I apologize. 'Lhe peço' is more formal than 'te peço.'

target: Ela nos trouxe um souvenir da viagem.
english: She brought us a souvenir from the trip.
grammar: 'Nos' as indirect: 'nos trouxe' = brought us. 'Souvenir' is used in Brazilian Portuguese as a loanword.

target: Eu te vi no shopping ontem.
english: I saw you at the mall yesterday.
grammar: 'Te vi' = I saw you. 'Te' as direct object. 'No shopping' — 'shopping' is commonly used in Brazil for mall.

target: Ele me prometeu que ia resolver.
english: He promised me he would sort it out.
grammar: 'Me' as indirect: 'me prometeu' = promised me. 'Que ia resolver' = that he was going to solve it.

target: A gente nos conheceu na faculdade.
english: We met each other in college.
grammar: 'Nos conheceu' should be 'nos conhecemos' (reciprocal). 'Na faculdade' = at university/college.

target: Eu vou te apresentar para os meus amigos.
english: I'm going to introduce you to my friends.
grammar: 'Te apresentar' = to introduce you. 'Para os meus amigos' = to my friends.

target: Ela me acordou às cinco da manhã.
english: She woke me up at five in the morning.
grammar: 'Me' as direct: 'me acordou' = woke me up. 'Às cinco da manhã' = at five in the morning.

target: Eu não a vi na festa.
english: I didn't see her at the party.
grammar: 'A' = her (formal direct object). 'Não a vi' = I didn't see her. Formal; spoken: 'não vi ela.'

target: Ele nos deu uma carona até o hotel.
english: He gave us a ride to the hotel.
grammar: 'Dar uma carona' means to give someone a ride. Very common expression in Brazil. 'Me dá uma carona?' = Can you give me a ride?

target: Eu te escutei, não precisa gritar.
english: I heard you, you don't need to shout.
grammar: 'Te escutei' = I heard you. 'Te' as direct object. 'Não precisa gritar' = no need to shout.

target: Ela me avisou sobre o atraso.
english: She warned me about the delay.
grammar: 'Me' as direct/indirect: 'me avisou' = warned/notified me. 'Sobre o atraso' = about the delay.

target: Eu te devo um favor.
english: I owe you a favor.
grammar: 'Te devo' = I owe you. 'Te' as indirect object. 'Um favor' = a favor.

target: A gente os ajudou com a mudança.
english: We helped them with the move.
grammar: 'Os' = them (direct object). 'Os ajudou' = helped them. Formal register; spoken: 'ajudou eles.'

target: Ele me tratou muito bem.
english: He treated me very well.
grammar: 'Me' as direct: 'me tratou' = treated me. 'Muito bem' = very well.

target: Eu lhe garanto que vai dar certo.
english: I guarantee you it will work out.
grammar: 'Lhe' = to you (formal indirect). 'Garantir' = to guarantee/assure. 'Que vai dar certo' = that it will work out.

target: Ela nos convidou para o casamento.
english: She invited us to the wedding.
grammar: 'Nos' as direct: 'nos convidou' = invited us. 'Para o casamento' = to the wedding.

target: Eu te ligo quando chegar.
english: I'll call you when I arrive.
grammar: 'Te ligo' = I'll call you. 'Te' as indirect. 'Quando chegar' = when I arrive (subjunctive).

target: Ele me indicou um bom restaurante.
english: He recommended a good restaurant to me.
grammar: 'Me' as indirect: 'me indicou' = recommended to me. 'Indicar' = to recommend/suggest.

target: Eu não te esqueci, calma.
english: I didn't forget about you, calm down.
grammar: 'Te esqueci' should be 'me esqueci de você' or informal 'te esqueci.' 'Calma' = calm down/relax.

target: A gente nos divertiu muito na festa.
english: We had a lot of fun at the party.
grammar: 'Nos' = ourselves (reflexive/reciprocal): 'nos divertimos' = we had fun. 'Na festa' = at the party.

target: Ela me perguntou se eu ia viajar.
english: She asked me if I was going to travel.
grammar: 'Me' as indirect: 'me perguntou' = asked me. 'Se eu ia viajar' = if I was going to travel.

target: Eu te peço um minuto de paciência.
english: I ask you for a minute of patience.
grammar: 'Te peço' = I ask you. 'Te' as indirect. 'Um minuto de paciência' = a minute of patience.

target: Ele me cobrou o dinheiro que emprestei.
english: He asked me to pay back the money I lent.
grammar: 'Me' as indirect: 'me cobrou' = charged me / demanded from me. 'Que emprestei' = that I lent.

target: Eu os encontrei por acaso na rua.
english: I ran into them by chance on the street.
grammar: 'Os' = them (direct object). 'Os encontrei' = I found/met them. 'Por acaso' = by chance.

target: Ela nos preparou um café da manhã especial.
english: She prepared a special breakfast for us.
grammar: 'Nos' as indirect: 'nos preparou' = prepared for us. 'Café da manhã especial' = special breakfast.

target: Eu te garanto que vale a pena.
english: I guarantee you it's worth it.
grammar: 'Te garanto' = I guarantee you. 'Te' as indirect. 'Que vale a pena' = that it's worth it.

target: Ele me deu uma dica ótima.
english: He gave me a great tip.
grammar: 'Me' as indirect: 'me deu uma dica' = gave me a tip. 'Dica' = tip/hint — very Brazilian word.

target: Eu não te ouvi, pode falar mais alto?
english: I didn't hear you, can you speak louder?

target: A gente os recebeu com muito carinho.
english: We welcomed them very warmly.

target: Ela me devolveu o livro que emprestei.
english: She returned the book I lent her.

target: Eu te entendo perfeitamente.
english: I understand you perfectly.

target: Ele nos acompanhou até a porta.
english: He walked us to the door.

target: Eu vou te contar um segredo.
english: I'm going to tell you a secret.

target: Ela me ensinou a fazer pão de queijo.
english: She taught me how to make cheese bread.

target: Eu lhe agradeço de coração.
english: I thank you from the heart.

target: A gente os levou para conhecer a cidade.
english: We took them to see the city.

target: Ele me avisou que ia chegar atrasado.
english: He let me know he was going to be late.

target: Eu te admiro muito.
english: I admire you a lot.

target: Ela nos emprestou o apartamento dela.
english: She lent us her apartment.

target: Eu não a reconheci de longe.
english: I didn't recognize her from far away.

target: Ele me passou o telefone do médico.
english: He gave me the doctor's phone number.

target: Eu te mando o endereço pelo WhatsApp.
english: I'll send you the address through WhatsApp.

target: A gente nos despedimos com saudade.
english: We said goodbye with longing.

target: Ela me fez rir com aquela história.
english: She made me laugh with that story.

target: Eu te espero lá embaixo.
english: I'll wait for you downstairs.

target: Ele nos deu a melhor mesa do restaurante.
english: He gave us the best table at the restaurant.

target: Eu lhe digo uma coisa: não vale a pena.
english: I'll tell you something: it's not worth it.

target: Ela me convidou para o aniversário.
english: She invited me to the birthday party.

target: Eu te pego no trabalho às seis.
english: I'll pick you up at work at six.

target: A gente os tratou como família.
english: We treated them like family.

target: Ele me deu um conselho valioso.
english: He gave me valuable advice.

target: Eu não te culpo por nada.
english: I don't blame you for anything.

target: Ela nos mandou fotos da viagem.
english: She sent us photos from the trip.

target: Eu te respeito muito.
english: I respect you a lot.

target: Ele me cobrou caro pelo serviço.
english: He charged me a lot for the service.

target: Eu vou te devolver amanhã.
english: I'll return it to you tomorrow.

target: A gente nos mudou para uma casa maior.
english: We moved to a bigger house.

target: Ela me impressionou com o talento dela.
english: She impressed me with her talent.

target: Eu te proíbo de fazer isso.
english: I forbid you from doing that.

target: Ele nos explicou como funciona.
english: He explained to us how it works.

target: Eu lhe asseguro que não há problema.
english: I assure you there's no problem.

target: Ela me inspirou a estudar mais.
english: She inspired me to study more.

target: Eu te achei muito engraçado.
english: I found you very funny.

target: A gente os esperou no saguão.
english: We waited for them in the lobby.

target: Ele me perguntou se eu sabia português.
english: He asked me if I knew Portuguese.

target: Eu te recomendo esse restaurante.
english: I recommend this restaurant to you.

target: Ela nos chamou para conversar.
english: She called us to talk.

target: Eu não te reconheci com esse chapéu.
english: I didn't recognize you with that hat.

target: Ele me ofereceu uma bebida.
english: He offered me a drink.

target: Eu vou te pedir uma coisa.
english: I'm going to ask you something.

target: A gente nos conheceu na fila do banco.
english: We met each other in the bank line.

target: Ela me apoiou em todos os momentos.
english: She supported me in every moment.

target: Eu te considero um grande amigo.
english: I consider you a great friend.

target: Ele nos salvou de uma enrascada.
english: He saved us from a jam.

target: Eu lhe peço que tenha paciência.
english: I ask you to be patient.

target: Ela me surpreendeu com uma visita.
english: She surprised me with a visit.

target: Eu te acho muito corajoso.
english: I think you're very brave.

target: A gente os deixou no hotel.
english: We dropped them off at the hotel.

target: Ele me convenceu a ir.
english: He convinced me to go.

target: Eu te desejo um bom dia.
english: I wish you a good day.

target: Ela nos contou a história da família dela.
english: She told us her family's story.

target: Eu não te vi entrar.
english: I didn't see you come in.

target: Ele me trouxe um café.
english: He brought me a coffee.

target: Eu te convido para jantar.
english: I invite you to dinner.

target: A gente nos reuniu para comemorar.
english: We got together to celebrate.

target: Ela me acordou com um telefonema.
english: She woke me up with a phone call.

target: Eu te acompanho até o carro.
english: I'll walk you to the car.

target: Ele nos levou a um restaurante incrível.
english: He took us to an amazing restaurant.

target: Eu lhe comunico que a reunião foi adiada.
english: I inform you that the meeting was postponed.

target: Ela me perdoou pelo que eu fiz.
english: She forgave me for what I did.

target: Eu te trago o cardápio.
english: I'll bring you the menu.

target: A gente os acompanhou no aeroporto.
english: We accompanied them at the airport.

target: Ele me elogiou pelo trabalho.
english: He praised me for the work.

target: Eu não te julgaria por isso.
english: I wouldn't judge you for that.

target: Ela nos deu uma aula de dança.
english: She gave us a dance lesson.

target: Eu me apresento: sou a Maria.
english: Allow me to introduce myself: I'm Maria.

target: Ele me motivou a continuar.
english: He motivated me to keep going.

target: Eu te aviso quando souber.
english: I'll let you know when I find out.

target: A gente os viu de longe.
english: We saw them from far away.

target: Ela me deixou sem palavras.
english: She left me speechless.

target: Eu te mostro as fotos depois.
english: I'll show you the photos later.

target: Ele nos alertou sobre o perigo.
english: He alerted us about the danger.

target: Eu lhe desejo tudo de bom.
english: I wish you all the best.

target: Ela me confortou quando eu precisei.
english: She comforted me when I needed it.

target: Eu te prometo que vou melhorar.
english: I promise you I'll do better.

target: A gente nos comprometeu a ajudar.
english: We committed to helping.

target: Ele me mandou embora mais cedo.
english: He sent me home early.

target: Eu te apresento o meu irmão.
english: Let me introduce you to my brother.
grammar: In Brazilian Portuguese, pronouns go between parts of the future periphrasis: 'vou te apresentar' or before in the present: 'te apresento.'

target: Ela nos serviu um prato típico.
english: She served us a typical dish.

target: Eu não a culpo por nada.
english: I don't blame her for anything.

target: Ele me corrigiu com educação.
english: He corrected me politely.

target: Eu te conto depois com mais calma.
english: I'll tell you later when things calm down.

target: A gente os surpreendeu com a notícia.
english: We surprised them with the news.

target: Ela me segurou para não cair.
english: She held me so I wouldn't fall.

target: Eu te explico a situação amanhã.
english: I'll explain the situation to you tomorrow.

target: Ele nos informou sobre a mudança.
english: He informed us about the change.

target: Eu lhe ofereço o meu apoio total.
english: I offer you my full support.

target: Ela me aconselhou a ir ao médico.
english: She advised me to go to the doctor.

target: Eu te trago uma lembrancinha.
english: I'll bring you a little souvenir.

target: A gente nos organizou para o evento.
english: We organized ourselves for the event.

target: Ele me provocou e eu perdi a paciência.
english: He provoked me and I lost my patience.

target: Eu te perdoo, não se preocupe.
english: I forgive you, don't worry.

target: Ela nos levou para ver o Cristo Redentor.
english: She took us to see Christ the Redeemer.

target: Eu não o entendi muito bem.
english: I didn't understand him very well.

target: Ele me decepcionou com aquela atitude.
english: He disappointed me with that attitude.

target: Eu te aviso se mudar alguma coisa.
english: I'll let you know if anything changes.

target: A gente os recebeu de braços abertos.
english: We welcomed them with open arms.

target: Ela me incentivou a mudar de emprego.
english: She encouraged me to change jobs.

target: Eu te achei mudado, está tudo bem?
english: I noticed you've changed, is everything okay?

target: Ele nos pagou com um mês de atraso.
english: He paid us a month late.

target: Eu lhe peço desculpas pelo engano.
english: I apologize to you for the misunderstanding.

target: Ela me deu força para continuar.
english: She gave me strength to keep going.

target: Eu te pago o almoço na próxima.
english: I'll pay for your lunch next time.

target: A gente nos adaptou à vida nova.
english: We adapted to the new life.

target: Ele me proibiu de usar o carro.
english: He forbade me from using the car.

target: Eu te considero parte da família.
english: I consider you part of the family.

target: Ela nos pediu para guardar segredo.
english: She asked us to keep a secret.

target: Eu o admiro pela coragem.
english: I admire him for his courage.

target: Ele me deixou confuso com a explicação.
english: He left me confused with the explanation.

target: Eu te mando o relatório por e-mail.
english: I'll send you the report by email.

target: A gente os levou para comer acarajé.
english: We took them to eat acarajé.
grammar: Acarajé is a famous Bahian street food — deep-fried black-eyed pea fritters filled with shrimp and spicy paste. A must-try in Salvador.

target: Ela me lembrou do compromisso.
english: She reminded me of the appointment.

target: Eu te apresento a minha família.
english: Let me introduce you to my family.

target: Ele nos convocou para uma reunião urgente.
english: He called us to an urgent meeting.

target: Eu lhe garanto que isso não vai se repetir.
english: I guarantee you this won't happen again.

target: Ela me abraçou quando me viu.
english: She hugged me when she saw me.

target: Eu te desejo boa sorte na entrevista.
english: I wish you good luck at the interview.

target: A gente nos reuniu para planejar a viagem.
english: We got together to plan the trip.

target: Ele me deu a oportunidade de aprender.
english: He gave me the opportunity to learn.

target: Eu te conto uma coisa: adorei o Brasil.
english: I'll tell you something: I loved Brazil.

target: Ela nos chamou de volta para ajudar.
english: She called us back to help.

target: Eu não a vi sair.
english: I didn't see her leave.

target: Ele me deu um aperto de mão firme.
english: He gave me a firm handshake.

target: Eu te vejo amanhã no trabalho.
english: I'll see you tomorrow at work.

target: A gente os esperou com ansiedade.
english: We waited for them with anticipation.

target: Ela me fez pensar sobre a vida.
english: She made me think about life.

target: Eu te conheço há muito tempo.
english: I've known you for a long time.

target: Ele nos pediu para chegar cedo.
english: He asked us to arrive early.

target: Eu lhe asseguro que está tudo certo.
english: I assure you everything is fine.

target: Ela me chamou pelo nome errado.
english: She called me by the wrong name.

target: Eu te desejo feliz aniversário.
english: I wish you a happy birthday.

target: A gente nos lembrou dos bons tempos.
english: We remembered the good old times.

target: Ele me escolheu para o time.
english: He chose me for the team.

target: Eu te dei a minha palavra.
english: I gave you my word.

target: Ela nos mostrou o caminho mais curto.
english: She showed us the shortest way.

target: Eu não o vi desde o ano passado.
english: I haven't seen him since last year.

target: Ele me convenceu com bons argumentos.
english: He convinced me with good arguments.

target: Eu te mando notícias quando chegar.
english: I'll send you news when I arrive.

target: A gente os convidou para o Réveillon.
english: We invited them for New Year's Eve.
grammar: 'Réveillon' is the French-origin word Brazilians use for New Year's Eve celebrations. The biggest one is on Copacabana beach in Rio.

target: Eu lhe devo muito por tudo que fez por mim.
english: I owe you a lot for everything you did for me.

target: É preciso que você estude mais para a prova.
english: You need to study more for the exam.
grammar: Subjunctive follows expressions of necessity like 'é preciso que'.

target: Espero que ela chegue a tempo.
english: I hope she arrives on time.
grammar: Esperar que always triggers the subjunctive.

target: Quero que você fale com o professor.
english: I want you to talk to the professor.

target: É importante que a gente participe da reunião.
english: It's important that we participate in the meeting.
grammar: 'É importante que' triggers subjunctive.

target: Talvez ele saiba a resposta.
english: Maybe he knows the answer.
grammar: Talvez triggers the subjunctive when it precedes the verb.

target: Duvido que eles terminem hoje.
english: I doubt they'll finish today.
grammar: Doubt expressions like 'duvidar que' require the subjunctive.

target: Embora eu esteja cansado, vou à festa.
english: Although I'm tired, I'll go to the party.
grammar: 'Embora' always requires the subjunctive.

target: Para que você entenda, vou explicar de novo.
english: So that you understand, I'll explain again.

target: Antes que ele saia, preciso falar com ele.
english: Before he leaves, I need to talk to him.
grammar: 'Antes que' always requires the subjunctive.

target: Não acredito que isso seja verdade.
english: I don't believe that's true.

target: É possível que chova amanhã.
english: It's possible that it rains tomorrow.
grammar: 'É possível que' triggers subjunctive.

target: Mesmo que você tente, não vai conseguir.
english: Even if you try, you won't succeed.

target: Quero que meus filhos sejam felizes.
english: I want my children to be happy.

target: É necessário que todos assinem o documento.
english: It's necessary that everyone signs the document.

target: Caso você precise de ajuda, me liga.
english: In case you need help, call me.
grammar: 'Caso' always triggers subjunctive.

target: Não acho que ele venha hoje.
english: I don't think he's coming today.
grammar: Negative opinion ('não acho que') triggers subjunctive.

target: Espero que o tempo melhore no fim de semana.
english: I hope the weather improves this weekend.

target: É provável que a gente viaje em julho.
english: It's likely that we'll travel in July.

target: Tomara que dê tudo certo.
english: I hope everything works out.
grammar: 'Tomara que' is a common subjunctive trigger expressing hope.

target: Preciso que você me envie o relatório.
english: I need you to send me the report.

target: Basta que ele peça desculpas.
english: It's enough for him to apologize.
grammar: 'Basta que' triggers the subjunctive.

target: A menos que você estude, vai reprovar.
english: Unless you study, you'll fail.

target: Exijo que todos cheguem no horário.
english: I demand that everyone arrives on time.

target: Pode ser que ele mude de ideia.
english: It could be that he changes his mind.
grammar: 'Pode ser que' triggers the subjunctive.

target: Até que prove o contrário, confio nele.
english: Until proven otherwise, I trust him.

target: É uma pena que você não possa ir.
english: It's a shame you can't go.
grammar: 'É uma pena que' expresses emotion and requires subjunctive.

target: Desde que você concorde, podemos começar.
english: As long as you agree, we can start.

target: Peço que tenham paciência.
english: I ask that you have patience.

target: Proíbo que usem o celular na aula.
english: I forbid using cell phones in class.

target: Sem que ninguém perceba, ele saiu.
english: Without anyone noticing, he left.
grammar: 'Sem que' always requires the subjunctive.

target: É raro que ele falte ao trabalho.
english: It's rare for him to miss work.

target: Sugiro que vocês pensem bem antes de decidir.
english: I suggest you think carefully before deciding.
grammar: Verbs of suggestion (sugerir) trigger the subjunctive.

target: É melhor que a gente vá embora agora.
english: It's better that we leave now.

target: Não há ninguém que saiba disso.
english: There's no one who knows about this.
grammar: Subjunctive is used after indefinite/negative antecedents.

target: Quero alguém que fale inglês.
english: I want someone who speaks English.
grammar: When seeking an unknown/hypothetical person, use subjunctive.

target: Ele pede que eu o ajude com a mudança.
english: He asks me to help him with the move.

target: Duvido que façam isso a tempo.
english: I doubt they'll do that in time.

target: Embora pareça fácil, é bastante difícil.
english: Although it seems easy, it's quite difficult.

target: Para que tudo funcione, precisamos nos organizar.
english: For everything to work, we need to get organized.

target: Espero que vocês gostem do presente.
english: I hope you like the gift.

target: É fundamental que a gente se prepare.
english: It's essential that we prepare ourselves.

target: Talvez eu vá ao cinema hoje à noite.
english: Maybe I'll go to the movies tonight.

target: Quero que ele entenda a situação.
english: I want him to understand the situation.

target: É inaceitável que tratem os funcionários assim.
english: It's unacceptable to treat employees like that.

target: Contanto que você pague, pode levar.
english: As long as you pay, you can take it.
grammar: 'Contanto que' triggers the subjunctive.

target: Deus queira que tudo dê certo.
english: God willing, everything will work out.

target: Recomendo que você consulte um médico.
english: I recommend you see a doctor.

target: É estranho que ele não tenha ligado.
english: It's strange that he hasn't called.
grammar: 'É estranho que' expresses emotion, triggering subjunctive.

target: Convém que você leia o contrato antes de assinar.
english: It's advisable that you read the contract before signing.

target: Não existe nada que me faça mudar de ideia.
english: There's nothing that will make me change my mind.

target: Embora eu more longe, venho todos os dias.
english: Although I live far away, I come every day.

target: Espero que a viagem seja tranquila.
english: I hope the trip goes smoothly.

target: É triste que tantas pessoas passem fome.
english: It's sad that so many people go hungry.

target: Para que ele aprenda, precisa praticar.
english: For him to learn, he needs to practice.

target: Não quero que você se preocupe.
english: I don't want you to worry.

target: Talvez a gente consiga terminar hoje.
english: Maybe we'll manage to finish today.

target: É urgente que resolvam esse problema.
english: It's urgent that they solve this problem.

target: Oxalá eu passe no concurso.
english: I hope I pass the exam.
grammar: 'Oxalá' is a subjunctive trigger expressing strong hope, from Arabic.

target: Lamento que você esteja doente.
english: I'm sorry you're sick.

target: A não ser que chova, vamos à praia.
english: Unless it rains, we'll go to the beach.
grammar: 'A não ser que' triggers the subjunctive.

target: Desejo que todos tenham um bom dia.
english: I wish everyone a good day.

target: É justo que ele receba uma promoção.
english: It's fair that he gets a promotion.

target: Permita que eu me apresente.
english: Allow me to introduce myself.

target: Prefiro que você venha de manhã.
english: I prefer you to come in the morning.
grammar: Preferir que triggers the subjunctive.

target: Antes que eu esqueça, anota meu telefone.
english: Before I forget, write down my number.

target: É improvável que neigem no Rio.
english: It's unlikely to snow in Rio.

target: Quero que meu filho estude medicina.
english: I want my son to study medicine.

target: Ainda que ele peça, não vou emprestar.
english: Even if he asks, I won't lend it.
grammar: 'Ainda que' triggers the subjunctive.

target: Torço para que o Brasil ganhe.
english: I'm rooting for Brazil to win.
grammar: 'Torcer para que' triggers subjunctive.

target: É bom que você tenha avisado com antecedência.
english: It's good that you gave notice in advance.

target: Não creio que ele consiga sozinho.
english: I don't believe he can do it alone.

target: Fico feliz que você esteja aqui.
english: I'm glad you're here.
grammar: Expressions of emotion (fico feliz que) trigger subjunctive.

target: Espero que a comida esteja gostosa.
english: I hope the food is tasty.

target: É difícil que ele aceite essa proposta.
english: It's unlikely that he'll accept this proposal.

target: Embora tenhamos pouco tempo, vamos tentar.
english: Although we have little time, we'll try.

target: Para que não haja confusão, vou explicar tudo.
english: So there's no confusion, I'll explain everything.

target: Talvez seja melhor esperar.
english: Maybe it's better to wait.

target: É natural que você sinta medo.
english: It's natural for you to feel scared.

target: Antes que a loja feche, preciso comprar pão.
english: Before the store closes, I need to buy bread.

target: Quero que a casa fique limpa antes da visita.
english: I want the house to be clean before the visit.

target: Duvido que ele se lembre do meu aniversário.
english: I doubt he remembers my birthday.

target: É hora de que a gente tome uma decisão.
english: It's time for us to make a decision.

target: Não há lugar que seja tão bonito quanto o Rio.
english: There's no place as beautiful as Rio.

target: Espero que o ônibus não demore.
