english: I asked him to bring the signed documents.

target: Se eu não estivesse tão cansado, sairia com vocês.
english: If I weren't so tired, I'd go out with you.

target: Se ela soubesse cantar, participaria do coral.
english: If she knew how to sing, she'd join the choir.

target: Era necessário que ele fizesse o exame antes da cirurgia.
english: It was necessary for him to have the exam before the surgery.

target: Se eu pudesse consertar o passado, consertaria.
english: If I could fix the past, I would.

target: Se houvesse mais vagas, todos seriam contratados.
english: If there were more positions, everyone would be hired.

target: Eu tratei a situação como se fosse urgente.
english: I treated the situation as if it were urgent.

target: Se eu tivesse mais horas no dia, leria mais.
english: If I had more hours in the day, I'd read more.

target: Se ele me convidasse, eu iria com prazer.
english: If he invited me, I'd go with pleasure.

target: Se eu não tivesse compromisso, ficaria mais tempo.
english: If I didn't have plans, I'd stay longer.

target: Quem dera a gente morasse perto um do outro.
english: I wish we lived close to each other.

target: Se eu fizesse dieta, perderia peso rapidamente.
english: If I went on a diet, I'd lose weight quickly.

target: Se ela não estivesse grávida, viajaria de avião.
english: If she weren't pregnant, she'd travel by plane.

target: Se ele me ouvisse, não cometeria esse erro.
english: If he listened to me, he wouldn't make that mistake.

target: Se eu tivesse uma máquina do tempo, voltaria aos anos noventa.
english: If I had a time machine, I'd go back to the nineties.

target: Se vocês lessem o manual, saberiam usar o equipamento.
english: If you read the manual, you'd know how to use the equipment.

target: Se a gente se organizasse melhor, sobraria tempo.
english: If we got better organized, there'd be time left over.

target: Se não fosse pelo meu irmão, eu não teria conseguido.
english: If it weren't for my brother, I wouldn't have made it.

target: Se eu pudesse, ficaria em casa o dia todo.
english: If I could, I'd stay home all day.

target: Se o professor explicasse melhor, os alunos entenderiam.
english: If the teacher explained better, the students would understand.

target: Se eu comprasse aquele apartamento, ficaria endividado.
english: If I bought that apartment, I'd be in debt.

target: Se ela viesse morar no Brasil, se adaptaria rápido.
english: If she came to live in Brazil, she'd adapt quickly.

target: Se não fosse pelo barulho, eu dormiria bem aqui.
english: If it weren't for the noise, I'd sleep well here.

target: Se a gente tivesse um quintal, plantaria uma horta.
english: If we had a backyard, we'd plant a garden.

target: Se eu estivesse de férias, iria à praia todos os dias.
english: If I were on vacation, I'd go to the beach every day.

target: Se não fosse tarde, a gente ficaria mais um pouco.
english: If it weren't late, we'd stay a bit longer.

target: Eu gostaria que ele levasse o trabalho mais a sério.
english: I'd like him to take work more seriously.

target: Se eu tivesse um jardim, plantaria árvores frutíferas.
english: If I had a garden, I'd plant fruit trees.

target: Se ele viesse de carro, não precisaríamos de táxi.
english: If he came by car, we wouldn't need a taxi.

target: Se eu tivesse estudado engenharia, estaria trabalhando em uma multinacional.
english: If I had studied engineering, I'd be working at a multinational.
grammar: Mixed conditional: past hypothesis (tivesse + participle) + present result (condicional).

target: Se ele tivesse aceitado a oferta, não estaria desempregado agora.
english: If he had accepted the offer, he wouldn't be unemployed now.

target: Se eu não tivesse saído de casa cedo, teria perdido o voo.
english: If I hadn't left home early, I would have missed the flight.
grammar: Full past conditional: se + mais-que-perfeito do subjuntivo + condicional composto.

target: Se nós tivéssemos comprado os ingressos antes, não teríamos ficado sem.
english: If we had bought the tickets earlier, we wouldn't have gone without.

target: Se eu fosse mais organizado, não perderia tantas coisas.
english: If I were more organized, I wouldn't lose so many things.

target: Se ela não tivesse me avisado, eu teria ido ao lugar errado.
english: If she hadn't warned me, I would have gone to the wrong place.

target: Se eu tivesse nascido em outra época, a vida seria diferente.
english: If I had been born in another era, life would be different.

target: Se eu economizasse desde jovem, já estaria aposentado.
english: If I had saved since I was young, I'd already be retired.

target: Se não fosse pela chuva, o evento teria sido perfeito.
english: If it weren't for the rain, the event would have been perfect.

target: Se ele tivesse me ouvido, não teria tido esse problema.
english: If he had listened to me, he wouldn't have had this problem.

target: Se eu soubesse que você estava aqui, teria vindo antes.
english: If I had known you were here, I would have come earlier.

target: Se nós tivéssemos mais recursos, expandiríamos o negócio.
english: If we had more resources, we'd expand the business.

target: Se ela não tivesse se atrasado, não teria perdido a entrevista.
english: If she hadn't been late, she wouldn't have missed the interview.

target: Se eu tivesse feito o curso, estaria mais preparado.
english: If I had taken the course, I'd be better prepared.

target: Se o governo tivesse agido mais cedo, a crise seria menor.
english: If the government had acted earlier, the crisis would be smaller.

target: Se eu não fosse tão teimoso, teria aceitado ajuda.
english: If I weren't so stubborn, I would have accepted help.

target: Se ele tivesse chegado a tempo, teria participado da decisão.
english: If he had arrived on time, he would have participated in the decision.

target: Se nós soubéssemos a verdade, agiríamos de outra forma.
english: If we knew the truth, we'd act differently.

target: Se eu tivesse investido naquela empresa, estaria rico agora.
english: If I had invested in that company, I'd be rich now.

target: Se ela falasse mais devagar, todos entenderiam.
english: If she spoke more slowly, everyone would understand.

target: Se eu não tivesse comido tanto, não estaria passando mal.
english: If I hadn't eaten so much, I wouldn't be feeling sick.

target: Se houvesse mais segurança, as pessoas andariam mais tranquilas.
english: If there were more security, people would walk more calmly.

target: Se eu tivesse seguido o mapa, não teria me perdido.
english: If I had followed the map, I wouldn't have gotten lost.

target: Se ele fosse mais paciente, seria um ótimo professor.
english: If he were more patient, he'd be a great teacher.

target: Se eu tivesse conhecido ela antes, tudo seria diferente.
english: If I had met her earlier, everything would be different.

target: Se não fosse pela minha família, eu não teria conseguido.
english: If it weren't for my family, I wouldn't have made it.

target: Se eu tivesse dormido melhor, estaria mais disposto hoje.
english: If I had slept better, I'd be more energized today.

target: Se a gente tivesse combinado antes, não haveria confusão.
english: If we had agreed beforehand, there wouldn't be any confusion.

target: Se eu pudesse voltar atrás, não teria dito aquilo.
english: If I could go back, I wouldn't have said that.

target: Se ele tivesse se esforçado mais, teria conseguido a vaga.
english: If he had tried harder, he would have gotten the position.

target: Se eu falasse francês, me candidataria àquela vaga.
english: If I spoke French, I'd apply for that position.

target: Se não tivesse chovido, teríamos feito o churrasco.
english: If it hadn't rained, we would have had the barbecue.

target: Se eu fosse mais velho, teria mais experiência.
english: If I were older, I'd have more experience.

target: Se ela não tivesse pedido demissão, estaria em uma posição melhor.
english: If she hadn't resigned, she'd be in a better position.

target: Se eu tivesse levado o guarda-chuva, não teria me molhado.
english: If I had brought the umbrella, I wouldn't have gotten wet.

target: Se nós morássemos mais perto, nos veríamos todo dia.
english: If we lived closer, we'd see each other every day.

target: Se ele tivesse parado de fumar antes, estaria mais saudável.
english: If he had quit smoking earlier, he'd be healthier.

target: Se eu pudesse escolher qualquer lugar do mundo, moraria em Lisboa.
english: If I could choose any place in the world, I'd live in Lisbon.

target: Se eu tivesse acordado mais cedo, teria chegado no horário.
english: If I had woken up earlier, I would have arrived on time.

target: Se ela não fosse tão ocupada, sairíamos mais.
english: If she weren't so busy, we'd go out more.

target: Se eu tivesse previsto o problema, teria evitado.
english: If I had foreseen the problem, I would have avoided it.

target: Se nós tivéssemos um quintal, as crianças brincariam ao ar livre.
english: If we had a backyard, the kids would play outside.

target: Se eu não tivesse esquecido a senha, não precisaria resetar.
english: If I hadn't forgotten the password, I wouldn't need to reset it.

target: Se ele fosse mais comunicativo, faria mais amizades.
english: If he were more communicative, he'd make more friends.

target: Se eu tivesse trazido mais dinheiro, poderia ter comprado.
english: If I had brought more money, I could have bought it.

target: Se a escola ensinasse finanças, os jovens seriam mais responsáveis.
english: If schools taught finance, young people would be more responsible.

target: Se eu tivesse pedido ajuda, teria terminado mais rápido.
english: If I had asked for help, I would have finished faster.

target: Se ela não tivesse me dito, eu nunca teria descoberto.
english: If she hadn't told me, I never would have found out.

target: Se eu pudesse recomeçar, escolheria outra carreira.
english: If I could start over, I'd choose another career.

target: Se nós não tivéssemos vendido a casa, moraríamos lá até hoje.
english: If we hadn't sold the house, we'd still be living there.

target: Se eu tivesse prestado atenção, não teria errado.
english: If I had paid attention, I wouldn't have made a mistake.

target: Se o tempo estivesse melhor, faríamos um piquenique.
english: If the weather were better, we'd have a picnic.

target: Se eu tivesse mais coragem, mudaria de vida.
english: If I had more courage, I'd change my life.

target: Se ela tivesse aceitado o emprego, estaria morando no exterior.
english: If she had accepted the job, she'd be living abroad.

target: Se eu soubesse cozinhar como ela, abriria um restaurante.
english: If I could cook like her, I'd open a restaurant.

target: Se não tivesse acontecido aquele acidente, ele estaria bem.
english: If that accident hadn't happened, he'd be fine.

target: Se eu tivesse lido as instruções, não teria quebrado o aparelho.
english: If I had read the instructions, I wouldn't have broken the device.

target: Se a gente se comunicasse melhor, haveria menos conflito.
english: If we communicated better, there'd be less conflict.

target: Se eu tivesse ido ao médico antes, o tratamento seria mais simples.
english: If I had gone to the doctor earlier, the treatment would have been simpler.

target: Se ele não tivesse mentido, eu ainda confiaria nele.
english: If he hadn't lied, I'd still trust him.

target: Se eu ganhasse mais, não precisaria de dois empregos.
english: If I earned more, I wouldn't need two jobs.

target: Se nós tivéssemos planejado melhor, a viagem teria sido incrível.
english: If we had planned better, the trip would have been amazing.

target: Se eu tivesse mais paciência, ensinaria crianças pequenas.
english: If I had more patience, I'd teach small children.

target: Se eu fosse fluente em português, conseguiria esse emprego.
english: If I were fluent in Portuguese, I'd get this job.

target: Se ela não tivesse cancelado, estaríamos jantando agora.
english: If she hadn't canceled, we'd be having dinner right now.

target: Se eu tivesse guardado o recibo, poderia trocar o produto.
english: If I had kept the receipt, I could exchange the product.

target: Se não fosse pela tecnologia, a comunicação seria mais difícil.
english: If it weren't for technology, communication would be harder.

target: Se ele tivesse se preparado, a apresentação teria sido melhor.
english: If he had prepared, the presentation would have been better.

target: Se eu pudesse dar um conselho, diria para ter paciência.
english: If I could give one piece of advice, I'd say to have patience.

target: Se eu tivesse aprendido a nadar, não teria tanto medo do mar.
english: If I had learned to swim, I wouldn't be so afraid of the ocean.

target: Se nós tivéssemos mais tempo juntos, a relação seria mais forte.
english: If we had more time together, the relationship would be stronger.

target: Se a reunião tivesse sido mais curta, teríamos terminado a tempo.
english: If the meeting had been shorter, we would have finished on time.

target: Se eu fosse escritor, escreveria sobre a vida no Brasil.
english: If I were a writer, I'd write about life in Brazil.

target: Se ela tivesse me ligado, eu teria ido buscá-la.
english: If she had called me, I would have gone to pick her up.

target: Se eu não estivesse doente, iria à festa com vocês.
english: If I weren't sick, I'd go to the party with you.

target: Se eles tivessem investido em treinamento, a equipe renderia mais.
english: If they had invested in training, the team would perform better.

target: Se eu tivesse mais espaço, compraria um piano.
english: If I had more space, I'd buy a piano.

target: Se nós tivéssemos chegado antes, teríamos conseguido lugar.
english: If we had arrived earlier, we would have gotten seats.

target: Se a comida não estivesse tão salgada, eu comeria tudo.
english: If the food weren't so salty, I'd eat it all.

target: Se eu tivesse sabido antes, teria tomado outra decisão.
english: If I had known earlier, I would have made a different decision.

target: Se ele não fosse tão orgulhoso, pediria desculpas.
english: If he weren't so proud, he'd apologize.

target: Se eu tivesse começado antes, já teria terminado.
english: If I had started earlier, I'd already be done.

target: Se nós tivéssemos um barco, pescaríamos nos fins de semana.
english: If we had a boat, we'd fish on weekends.

target: Se ela não tivesse mudado de cidade, nos veríamos com frequência.
english: If she hadn't moved to another city, we'd see each other often.

target: Se eu tivesse aprendido português na infância, seria bilíngue agora.
english: If I had learned Portuguese in childhood, I'd be bilingual now.

target: Se eu pudesse mudar o mundo, começaria pela educação.
english: If I could change the world, I'd start with education.

target: Se ele tivesse sido mais cuidadoso, o acidente não teria acontecido.
english: If he had been more careful, the accident wouldn't have happened.

target: Se a gente tivesse seguido o GPS, não teríamos nos perdido.
english: If we had followed the GPS, we wouldn't have gotten lost.

target: Se eu tivesse dinheiro sobrando, reformaria a casa.
english: If I had extra money, I'd renovate the house.

target: Se eles tivessem perguntado, eu teria explicado tudo.
english: If they had asked, I would have explained everything.

target: Se eu fosse mais jovem, aprenderia a surfar.
english: If I were younger, I'd learn to surf.

target: Se eu tivesse percebido o erro a tempo, teria corrigido.
english: If I had noticed the mistake in time, I would have corrected it.

target: Se não fosse tão longe, eu iria a pé.
english: If it weren't so far, I'd go on foot.

target: Se eu tivesse mais confiança, faria a apresentação sem nervosismo.
english: If I had more confidence, I'd do the presentation without nervousness.

target: Se nós tivéssemos escolhido outro caminho, teríamos evitado o engarrafamento.
english: If we had chosen another route, we would have avoided the traffic jam.

target: Se eu fosse presidente, investiria em energia renovável.
english: If I were president, I'd invest in renewable energy.

target: Se ela tivesse se esforçado mais, teria sido aprovada.
english: If she had tried harder, she would have passed.

target: Se eu tivesse avisado com antecedência, eles teriam se preparado.
english: If I had warned them in advance, they would have prepared.

target: Se eu fosse mais alto, jogaria basquete profissional.
english: If I were taller, I'd play professional basketball.

target: Se nós não tivéssemos nos atrasado, pegaríamos o trem.
english: If we hadn't been late, we would have caught the train.

target: O prédio foi construído nos anos cinquenta.
english: The building was built in the fifties.
grammar: Passive voice: ser + past participle. 'Foi construído' = was built.

target: A decisão foi tomada pelo conselho.
english: The decision was made by the board.

target: Os documentos foram entregues ontem.
english: The documents were delivered yesterday.
grammar: 'Entregues' agrees in gender and number with the subject 'documentos'.

target: A lei foi aprovada pelo congresso.
english: The law was approved by congress.

target: Aqui se fala português.
english: Portuguese is spoken here.
grammar: 'Se' + verb in 3rd person creates a passive/impersonal construction.

target: Vende-se apartamento de dois quartos.
english: Two-bedroom apartment for sale.
grammar: 'Vende-se' is a common impersonal construction seen in classified ads.

target: A conta foi paga pela empresa.
english: The bill was paid by the company.

target: É proibido fumar neste recinto.
english: Smoking is prohibited in this area.

target: Os resultados foram divulgados hoje de manhã.
english: The results were released this morning.

target: Precisa-se de funcionários com experiência.
english: Experienced employees needed.
grammar: 'Precisa-se de' — impersonal 'se' with verbs that take prepositions.

target: A carta foi escrita à mão.
english: The letter was written by hand.

target: Aluga-se sala comercial.
english: Commercial space for rent.

target: O projeto foi aprovado por unanimidade.
english: The project was approved unanimously.

target: Não se aceita cartão neste estabelecimento.
english: Cards are not accepted in this establishment.

target: A ponte foi inaugurada pelo prefeito.
english: The bridge was inaugurated by the mayor.

target: Come-se bem neste restaurante.
english: You eat well at this restaurant.
grammar: Impersonal 'se' — no specific subject, describes general experience.

target: A exposição será aberta ao público amanhã.
english: The exhibition will be open to the public tomorrow.

target: O carro foi roubado durante a madrugada.
english: The car was stolen during the early morning.

target: Fala-se muito sobre isso nos jornais.
english: There's a lot of talk about this in the newspapers.

target: A casa foi reformada recentemente.
english: The house was recently renovated.

target: É necessário apresentar documento com foto.
english: It's necessary to present a photo ID.
grammar: Impersonal 'é necessário' + infinitive — no specific subject.

target: Os livros foram doados para a biblioteca.
english: The books were donated to the library.

target: Aqui não se pode estacionar.
english: You can't park here.

target: O problema foi resolvido pela equipe técnica.
english: The problem was solved by the technical team.

target: Vive-se bem nesta cidade.
english: You live well in this city.

target: A festa foi organizada pelos funcionários.
english: The party was organized by the employees.

target: É preciso ter paciência nessa situação.
english: It's necessary to be patient in this situation.

target: Os prêmios foram distribuídos aos vencedores.
english: The prizes were distributed to the winners.

target: Diz-se que ele vai se candidatar.
english: It's said that he's going to run for office.
grammar: 'Diz-se que' — impersonal way to express hearsay/rumor.

target: A estrada foi bloqueada por causa do acidente.
english: The road was blocked because of the accident.

target: Constrói-se muito nesta região.
english: There's a lot of construction in this area.

target: A reunião foi adiada para a semana que vem.
english: The meeting was postponed to next week.

target: É proibido entrar sem autorização.
english: Entry without authorization is prohibited.

target: O contrato foi assinado pelas duas partes.
english: The contract was signed by both parties.

target: Trabalha-se demais no Brasil.
english: People work too much in Brazil.

target: A escola foi fundada em mil novecentos e sessenta.
english: The school was founded in nineteen sixty.

target: Não se pode fazer barulho depois das dez.
english: You can't make noise after ten.

target: O evento foi patrocinado por várias empresas.
english: The event was sponsored by several companies.

target: É importante respeitar as regras.
english: It's important to respect the rules.

target: As vagas foram preenchidas rapidamente.
english: The positions were filled quickly.

target: Sabe-se que o problema é antigo.
english: It's known that the problem is old.

target: A obra foi concluída antes do prazo.
english: The construction was completed before the deadline.

target: Aqui se respira ar puro.
english: You breathe clean air here.

target: O relatório foi elaborado pelo departamento financeiro.
english: The report was prepared by the finance department.

target: É conveniente reservar com antecedência.
english: It's advisable to book in advance.

target: Os alunos foram avaliados por uma banca.
english: The students were evaluated by a panel.

target: Ouve-se muito forró no Nordeste.
english: You hear a lot of forró in the Northeast.

target: A encomenda foi enviada pelo correio.
english: The package was sent by mail.

target: É vedado o uso de celulares durante o exame.
english: The use of cell phones during the exam is prohibited.
grammar: 'É vedado' is a formal way to say 'is forbidden'.

target: O quadro foi pintado por um artista local.
english: The painting was done by a local artist.

target: Estuda-se muito para passar no vestibular.
english: People study a lot to pass the college entrance exam.

target: A estátua foi erguida em homenagem ao fundador.
english: The statue was erected in honor of the founder.

target: É fundamental manter a calma em emergências.
english: It's essential to stay calm in emergencies.

target: Os ingressos foram esgotados em poucas horas.
english: The tickets were sold out in a few hours.

target: Conta-se que a cidade tem mais de trezentos anos.
english: It's said that the city is over three hundred years old.

target: O prédio foi evacuado por causa do incêndio.
english: The building was evacuated because of the fire.

target: Vê-se que ele está preocupado.
english: You can see he's worried.

target: A música foi composta por Tom Jobim.
english: The song was composed by Tom Jobim.

target: É recomendável beber bastante água.
english: It's recommended to drink plenty of water.

target: Os dados foram analisados pela equipe de pesquisa.
english: The data was analyzed by the research team.

target: Espera-se que a situação melhore em breve.
english: It's expected that the situation will improve soon.

target: A proposta foi rejeitada pela maioria.
english: The proposal was rejected by the majority.

target: É necessário que se faça um registro.
english: It's necessary to register.

target: O filme foi dirigido por Fernando Meirelles.
english: The movie was directed by Fernando Meirelles.

target: Comenta-se muito sobre a nova lei.
english: There's a lot of discussion about the new law.

target: As ruas foram alagadas pela chuva forte.
english: The streets were flooded by the heavy rain.

target: É obrigatório o uso de cinto de segurança.
english: Wearing a seatbelt is mandatory.

target: O gol foi marcado nos últimos minutos do jogo.
english: The goal was scored in the final minutes of the game.

target: Nota-se uma melhora significativa no desempenho.
english: A significant improvement in performance is noticeable.

target: A empresa foi vendida para um grupo estrangeiro.
english: The company was sold to a foreign group.

target: É difícil encontrar vaga de estacionamento no centro.
english: It's hard to find parking downtown.

target: O tratamento foi indicado pelo especialista.
english: The treatment was recommended by the specialist.

target: Não se deve julgar pelas aparências.
english: One shouldn't judge by appearances.

target: A nova sede foi inaugurada no mês passado.
english: The new headquarters was inaugurated last month.

target: Percebe-se que o clima está mudando.
english: You can see that the climate is changing.

target: O monumento foi tombado pelo patrimônio histórico.
english: The monument was listed as a historical landmark.
grammar: 'Tombado' means designated as protected heritage — Brazilian legal term.

target: É indispensável apresentar comprovante de residência.
english: It's essential to present proof of residence.

target: As medalhas foram entregues pelo presidente do comitê.
english: The medals were awarded by the committee president.

target: Acredita-se que a vacina será eficaz.
english: It's believed that the vaccine will be effective.

target: O acordo foi firmado entre as duas nações.
english: The agreement was signed between the two nations.

target: Ele é inteligente, porém não se esforça.
english: He's intelligent, but he doesn't make an effort.
grammar: 'Porém' is a formal alternative to 'mas' — it carries a stronger contrast.

target: A viagem foi cansativa, entretanto valeu a pena.
english: The trip was tiring, however it was worth it.
grammar: 'Entretanto' is more literary than 'mas' and is placed after the subject or at the start.

target: Embora esteja frio, vou sair de camiseta.
english: Although it's cold, I'm going out in a t-shirt.

target: Apesar de estar chovendo, as crianças querem brincar lá fora.
english: Despite the rain, the children want to play outside.
grammar: 'Apesar de' + infinitive/noun — doesn't require subjunctive like 'embora'.

target: Contanto que você estude, pode sair à noite.
english: As long as you study, you can go out at night.
grammar: 'Contanto que' always takes the subjunctive.

target: Ele saiu sem que ninguém percebesse.
english: He left without anyone noticing.

target: Ainda que pareça impossível, vamos tentar.
english: Even if it seems impossible, we'll try.

target: Visto que o prazo acabou, não aceitamos mais inscrições.
english: Since the deadline has passed, we no longer accept registrations.
grammar: 'Visto que' is a formal connector meaning 'given that/since'.

target: Por mais que eu tente, não consigo dormir.
english: No matter how much I try, I can't sleep.
grammar: 'Por mais que' + subjunctive = no matter how much.

target: À medida que envelhecemos, ficamos mais sábios.
english: As we age, we become wiser.
grammar: 'À medida que' means 'as/to the extent that' — indicates parallel progression.

target: Ele viajou apesar de estar doente.
english: He traveled despite being sick.

target: Conquanto haja dificuldades, vamos superar.
english: Although there are difficulties, we'll overcome them.
grammar: 'Conquanto' is a formal synonym of 'embora'.

target: Ao passo que uns trabalham, outros descansam.
english: While some work, others rest.
grammar: 'Ao passo que' introduces contrast between simultaneous situations.

target: Já que você está aqui, me ajuda com isso.
english: Since you're here, help me with this.
grammar: 'Já que' is more informal than 'visto que' — both mean 'since'.

target: Assim que eu chegar, te ligo.
english: As soon as I arrive, I'll call you.

target: A fim de que todos entendam, vou falar devagar.
english: So that everyone understands, I'll speak slowly.
grammar: 'A fim de que' + subjunctive = 'in order that'.

target: Não obstante as dificuldades, a empresa cresceu.
english: Notwithstanding the difficulties, the company grew.
grammar: 'Não obstante' is a highly formal connector meaning 'despite/notwithstanding'.

target: Ele é simpático, todavia nem sempre é sincero.
english: He's nice, however he's not always sincere.
grammar: 'Todavia' is a formal synonym of 'porém'.

target: Enquanto eu cozinho, você arruma a mesa.
english: While I cook, you set the table.

target: Desde que eu me lembro, ele sempre foi assim.
english: As far as I can remember, he's always been like that.
grammar: 'Desde que' can mean 'since' (time) or 'as long as' (condition).

target: Por outro lado, a proposta tem suas vantagens.
english: On the other hand, the proposal has its advantages.

target: Além disso, precisamos contratar mais gente.
english: Besides that, we need to hire more people.
grammar: 'Além disso' connects ideas by adding information.

target: No entanto, nem tudo é tão simples.
english: Nevertheless, not everything is that simple.
grammar: 'No entanto' is widely used in both spoken and written Portuguese.

target: Apesar de tudo, ainda acredito que vai dar certo.
english: Despite everything, I still believe it will work out.

target: Em vez de reclamar, tente resolver o problema.
english: Instead of complaining, try to solve the problem.

target: Uma vez que a decisão foi tomada, não há como voltar atrás.
english: Once the decision has been made, there's no going back.
grammar: 'Uma vez que' means 'since/given that'.

target: Quer chova, quer faça sol, o evento vai acontecer.
english: Whether it rains or shines, the event will take place.
grammar: 'Quer... quer' means 'whether... or'.

target: Logo que soube da notícia, liguei para minha mãe.
english: As soon as I heard the news, I called my mom.
grammar: 'Logo que' = 'as soon as'.

target: Tanto que chegou a ser promovido.
english: So much so that he ended up getting promoted.

target: Conforme combinamos, a reunião será às dez.
english: As we agreed, the meeting will be at ten.
grammar: 'Conforme' = 'as/according to'.

target: Por conseguinte, a empresa decidiu encerrar as atividades.
english: Consequently, the company decided to end operations.
grammar: 'Por conseguinte' is formal — 'consequently/therefore'.

target: Dado que os preços subiram, vamos procurar alternativas.
english: Given that prices went up, we'll look for alternatives.

target: Ainda por cima, ele chegou atrasado.
english: On top of that, he arrived late.
grammar: 'Ainda por cima' adds emphasis to a list of complaints.

target: Embora eu concorde com você, tenho algumas ressalvas.
english: Although I agree with you, I have some reservations.

target: De modo que possamos começar, precisamos de sua aprovação.
english: So that we can start, we need your approval.

target: Antes de mais nada, quero agradecer a todos.
english: First of all, I want to thank everyone.
grammar: 'Antes de mais nada' = 'first and foremost/first of all'.

target: Em contrapartida, o salário é muito bom.
english: On the other hand, the salary is very good.
grammar: 'Em contrapartida' introduces a compensating factor.

target: Sem contar que ele ainda me deve dinheiro.
english: Not to mention that he still owes me money.

target: Mesmo assim, decidi continuar tentando.
english: Even so, I decided to keep trying.
grammar: 'Mesmo assim' = 'even so/nevertheless'.

target: Haja vista os resultados, precisamos mudar de estratégia.
english: In view of the results, we need to change strategy.
grammar: 'Haja vista' is formal — 'in view of/given'.

target: Por mais difícil que seja, não desista.
english: No matter how hard it is, don't give up.

target: A menos que ele mude de atitude, vai ser demitido.
english: Unless he changes his attitude, he'll be fired.

target: Em suma, o projeto foi um sucesso.
english: In short, the project was a success.
grammar: 'Em suma' = 'in short/to sum up'.

target: Nem por isso deixei de tentar.
english: That didn't stop me from trying.
grammar: 'Nem por isso' = 'even so/that didn't mean'.

target: Sob a condição de que todos concordem, podemos prosseguir.
english: On the condition that everyone agrees, we can proceed.

target: Por essa razão, resolvemos adiar a viagem.
english: For that reason, we decided to postpone the trip.

target: De qualquer forma, obrigado pela ajuda.
english: In any case, thanks for the help.
grammar: 'De qualquer forma' = 'in any case/anyway'.

target: Tanto faz se ele vem ou não.
english: It doesn't matter whether he comes or not.
grammar: 'Tanto faz' = 'it doesn't matter/either way'.

target: Além do mais, a comida estava excelente.
english: Furthermore, the food was excellent.
grammar: 'Além do mais' = 'moreover/furthermore'.

target: Sendo assim, vamos para o plano B.
english: That being the case, let's go to plan B.
grammar: 'Sendo assim' = 'that being so/therefore'.

target: Apesar de cansados, continuamos caminhando.
english: Despite being tired, we kept walking.

target: Embora não pareça, ele é muito competente.
english: Although it doesn't seem like it, he's very competent.

target: De modo geral, estou satisfeito com o resultado.
english: Overall, I'm satisfied with the result.

target: Em virtude do mau tempo, o voo foi cancelado.
english: Due to bad weather, the flight was canceled.
grammar: 'Em virtude de' = 'due to/by virtue of' — formal.

target: Posto que todos concordaram, vamos seguir em frente.
english: Since everyone agreed, let's move forward.
grammar: 'Posto que' is a literary/formal synonym of 'já que'.

target: Em compensação, o hotel era maravilhoso.
english: To make up for it, the hotel was wonderful.

target: Por via das dúvidas, leva um casaco.
english: Just in case, bring a jacket.
grammar: 'Por via das dúvidas' = 'just in case'.

target: Tão logo receba a resposta, te aviso.
english: As soon as I receive the answer, I'll let you know.
grammar: 'Tão logo' = 'as soon as' — slightly more formal than 'assim que'.

target: A propósito, você já comprou o presente?
english: By the way, have you bought the gift yet?
grammar: 'A propósito' = 'by the way/on that note'.

target: Por sinal, ele mencionou que vai se mudar.
english: By the way, he mentioned he's going to move.
grammar: 'Por sinal' = 'by the way/incidentally'.

target: Em resumo, precisamos de mais investimento.
english: In summary, we need more investment.

target: Afinal de contas, quem vai pagar essa conta?
english: After all, who's going to pay this bill?
grammar: 'Afinal de contas' = 'after all/when all is said and done'.

target: Contudo, a decisão final é sua.
english: However, the final decision is yours.
grammar: 'Contudo' is a more formal alternative to 'porém'.

target: Tampouco ele sabia da reunião.
english: He didn't know about the meeting either.
grammar: 'Tampouco' = 'neither/nor' — more formal than 'também não'.

target: Ao invés de brigar, conversem.
english: Instead of fighting, talk it out.
grammar: 'Ao invés de' = 'instead of'.

target: Nesse sentido, o governo tomou providências.
english: In that regard, the government took measures.

target: De fato, a situação melhorou bastante.
english: In fact, the situation has improved a lot.

target: Por fim, gostaria de agradecer a todos os presentes.
english: Finally, I'd like to thank everyone present.
grammar: 'Por fim' = 'finally/lastly'.

target: Em decorrência da greve, as aulas foram suspensas.
english: As a result of the strike, classes were suspended.
grammar: 'Em decorrência de' = 'as a result of' — formal.

target: Ora, isso não faz o menor sentido.
english: Well, that doesn't make any sense at all.
grammar: 'Ora' expresses impatience or introduces an obvious conclusion.

target: Conquanto seja arriscado, vale a tentativa.
english: Although it's risky, it's worth a try.

target: Com efeito, os números comprovam a tendência.
english: Indeed, the numbers confirm the trend.
grammar: 'Com efeito' = 'indeed/in fact' — formal confirmation.

target: Apesar de não concordar, respeitou a decisão do grupo.
english: Despite not agreeing, he respected the group's decision.

target: Seja como for, precisamos encontrar uma solução.
english: Be that as it may, we need to find a solution.
grammar: 'Seja como for' = 'be that as it may'.

target: Para tanto, será necessário reunir mais dados.
english: To that end, it will be necessary to gather more data.
grammar: 'Para tanto' = 'to that end/for that purpose'.

target: Até porque ele nunca foi muito pontual.
english: Especially since he was never very punctual.
grammar: 'Até porque' adds a reinforcing reason — 'especially since'.

target: A despeito das críticas, o projeto seguiu em frente.
english: Despite the criticism, the project moved forward.
grammar: 'A despeito de' is a formal synonym of 'apesar de'.

target: Em outras palavras, estamos falidos.
english: In other words, we're broke.

target: Porquanto os dados são confiáveis, podemos avançar.
english: Inasmuch as the data is reliable, we can proceed.
grammar: 'Porquanto' is very formal — 'inasmuch as/since'.

target: De toda forma, agradeço o convite.
english: In any case, I appreciate the invitation.

target: Não só estudou como também trabalhou durante a faculdade.
english: He not only studied but also worked during college.
grammar: 'Não só... como também' = 'not only... but also'.

target: Com tudo isso, ainda manteve o bom humor.
english: With all of this, he still kept his good spirits.

target: Nada obstante, o resultado foi satisfatório.
english: Notwithstanding, the result was satisfactory.

target: Isto é, precisamos rever o orçamento.
english: That is, we need to review the budget.
grammar: 'Isto é' = 'that is/in other words'.

target: Ou seja, a reunião foi cancelada.
english: In other words, the meeting was canceled.
grammar: 'Ou seja' = 'in other words/that is'.

target: Em particular, o setor de tecnologia teve destaque.
english: In particular, the technology sector stood out.

target: Quanto mais estudo, mais percebo que não sei nada.
english: The more I study, the more I realize I know nothing.
grammar: 'Quanto mais... mais' = 'the more... the more'.

target: Tanto quanto eu sei, o evento ainda vai acontecer.
english: As far as I know, the event is still happening.
grammar: 'Tanto quanto eu sei' = 'as far as I know'.

target: Assim sendo, não temos outra escolha.
english: That being so, we have no other choice.

target: Bem como seus colegas, ela recebeu o certificado.
english: As well as her colleagues, she received the certificate.
grammar: 'Bem como' = 'as well as'.

target: A princípio, concordei, mas depois mudei de ideia.
english: At first, I agreed, but then I changed my mind.
grammar: 'A princípio' = 'at first/initially'.

target: Mal chegou, já quis ir embora.
english: No sooner had he arrived than he wanted to leave.
grammar: 'Mal' + verb = 'as soon as/no sooner... than'.

target: Em face dos acontecimentos, decidimos cancelar.
english: In light of the events, we decided to cancel.
grammar: 'Em face de' = 'in light of/in the face of'.

target: De maneira que todos possam ouvir, fale mais alto.
english: So that everyone can hear, speak louder.

target: Ao contrário do que pensam, ele é muito humilde.
english: Contrary to what people think, he's very humble.

target: Nem que eu quisesse, não conseguiria fazer isso.
english: Even if I wanted to, I wouldn't be able to do that.
grammar: 'Nem que' = 'even if' — strong emphasis.

target: Nesse meio-tempo, podemos preparar o material.
english: In the meantime, we can prepare the material.

target: Por enquanto, vamos manter o plano original.
english: For now, let's keep the original plan.

target: Com base nisso, tomamos a decisão.
english: Based on that, we made the decision.

target: Isso posto, passemos ao próximo tópico.
english: That said, let's move on to the next topic.
grammar: 'Isso posto' = 'that said/that being established' — formal.

target: Desde que todos participem, o resultado será positivo.
english: As long as everyone participates, the result will be positive.

target: Estou trabalhando de casa hoje.
english: I'm working from home today.
grammar: Estar + gerúndio (-ando, -endo, -indo) forms the present continuous in Brazilian Portuguese.

target: Ele acabou de chegar do aeroporto.
english: He just arrived from the airport.
grammar: 'Acabar de' + infinitive = to have just done something.

target: Você tem que entregar o relatório até sexta.
english: You have to submit the report by Friday.
grammar: 'Ter que' + infinitive = to have to. Slightly more informal than 'ter de'.

target: Ela deixou de fumar há dois anos.
english: She quit smoking two years ago.
grammar: 'Deixar de' + infinitive = to stop/quit doing something.

target: Ele anda preocupado com o trabalho.
english: He's been worried about work.
grammar: 'Andar' + adjective/gerund indicates an ongoing state or repeated action.

target: Estávamos jantando quando a luz acabou.
english: We were having dinner when the power went out.

target: Vou acabar ficando doente se não descansar.
english: I'm going to end up getting sick if I don't rest.
grammar: 'Acabar' + gerúndio = to end up doing something.

target: Ela costuma acordar cedo durante a semana.
english: She usually wakes up early during the week.
grammar: 'Costumar' + infinitive = to usually do something — habitual action.

target: Tenho que resolver esse problema hoje.
english: I have to solve this problem today.

target: Ele estava falando no telefone quando entrei.
english: He was talking on the phone when I came in.

target: Ela começou a estudar português no ano passado.
english: She started studying Portuguese last year.
grammar: 'Começar a' + infinitive = to start doing something.

target: Pare de fazer barulho, por favor.
english: Stop making noise, please.
grammar: 'Parar de' + infinitive = to stop doing something.

target: Eles continuam morando no mesmo bairro.
english: They continue living in the same neighborhood.
grammar: 'Continuar' + gerúndio = to continue doing.

target: Estou pensando em mudar de emprego.
english: I'm thinking about changing jobs.

target: Ele voltou a estudar depois de dez anos.
english: He went back to studying after ten years.
grammar: 'Voltar a' + infinitive = to go back to doing something.

target: Passei a entender melhor depois da explicação.
english: I started to understand better after the explanation.
grammar: 'Passar a' + infinitive = to start doing something (gradual change).

target: Ela anda reclamando de tudo ultimamente.
english: She's been complaining about everything lately.

target: Acabei comprando mais do que precisava.
english: I ended up buying more than I needed.

target: Estou tentando comer menos açúcar.
english: I'm trying to eat less sugar.

target: Ele precisa de melhorar a comunicação.
english: He needs to improve his communication.

target: Ela ficou esperando por mais de uma hora.
english: She was left waiting for over an hour.
grammar: 'Ficar' + gerúndio emphasizes duration or a resulting state.

target: Vim te pedir um favor.
english: I came to ask you a favor.
grammar: 'Vir' + infinitive = to come to do something.

target: Ele tornou a ligar no dia seguinte.
english: He called again the next day.
grammar: 'Tornar a' + infinitive = to do something again — formal.

target: Estou morrendo de fome.
english: I'm dying of hunger.
grammar: Estar + gerúndio with 'morrer' is a common hyperbolic expression in Brazilian Portuguese.

target: Deixei de ir à academia por falta de tempo.
english: I stopped going to the gym due to lack of time.

target: Eles andam brigando muito ultimamente.
english: They've been fighting a lot lately.

target: Estou louca para chegar em casa.
english: I'm dying to get home.
grammar: 'Estar louco/a para' + infinitive = to be eager/dying to do something.

target: Ela saiu correndo quando ouviu a notícia.
english: She ran out when she heard the news.
grammar: 'Sair' + gerúndio = to go out doing something.

target: Tenho que ir ao dentista esta semana.
english: I have to go to the dentist this week.

target: Ele vive reclamando da vida.
english: He's always complaining about life.
grammar: 'Viver' + gerúndio = to always be doing something — habitual.

target: Acabamos de nos mudar para essa casa.
english: We just moved to this house.

target: Estou aprendendo a dirigir.
english: I'm learning to drive.

target: Ela deixou de acreditar em Papai Noel cedo.
english: She stopped believing in Santa Claus early.

target: Começo a entender o que você quer dizer.
english: I'm beginning to understand what you mean.

target: Ele anda meio estranho esses dias.
english: He's been a bit strange these days.

target: Estou doido para viajar.
english: I'm dying to travel.
grammar: 'Estar doido para' is very colloquial — 'can't wait to'.

target: Ela costumava tomar café da manhã cedo.
english: She used to have breakfast early.

target: Preciso parar de procrastinar.
english: I need to stop procrastinating.

target: Ele ficou olhando para mim sem dizer nada.
english: He kept looking at me without saying anything.

target: Estávamos prestes a sair quando ele ligou.
english: We were about to leave when he called.
grammar: 'Estar prestes a' + infinitive = to be about to do something.

target: Ela continua trabalhando mesmo doente.
english: She keeps working even when sick.

target: Acabei dormindo no sofá.
english: I ended up falling asleep on the sofa.

target: Estou precisando de férias.
english: I'm needing a vacation.
grammar: In Brazilian Portuguese, 'estar precisando' is natural, unlike English where 'need' is usually stative.

target: Ele volta e meia esquece as chaves.
english: He keeps forgetting his keys.
grammar: 'Volta e meia' = every now and then/keeps on.

target: Ela anda comendo muito fast food.
english: She's been eating a lot of fast food.

target: Tenho de admitir que você tinha razão.
english: I have to admit you were right.
grammar: 'Ter de' + infinitive is slightly more formal than 'ter que'.

target: Fiquei sabendo que ele foi promovido.
english: I found out he was promoted.
grammar: 'Ficar sabendo' = to find out/learn about something.

target: Estou a fim de ir ao cinema.
english: I feel like going to the movies.
grammar: 'Estar a fim de' = to feel like doing something — very Brazilian.

target: Ela passou a prestar mais atenção nas aulas.
english: She started paying more attention in class.

target: Ele vive esquecendo o aniversário da esposa.
english: He's always forgetting his wife's birthday.

target: Acabei de ler um livro incrível.
english: I just finished reading an incredible book.

target: Estou começando a gostar dessa cidade.
english: I'm starting to like this city.

target: Ela deixou de frequentar as aulas.
english: She stopped attending classes.

target: Ele anda se exercitando bastante.
english: He's been exercising a lot.

target: Continuamos procurando uma solução.
english: We kept looking for a solution.

target: Estou me sentindo melhor hoje.
english: I'm feeling better today.

target: Ele costuma almoçar no mesmo restaurante.
english: He usually has lunch at the same restaurant.

target: Acabei aceitando o convite dele.
english: I ended up accepting his invitation.

target: Estou querendo aprender a tocar violão.
english: I've been wanting to learn to play guitar.

target: Ela voltou a praticar natação.
english: She went back to practicing swimming.

target: Tenho que tomar uma decisão até amanhã.
english: I have to make a decision by tomorrow.

target: Ele ficou pensando no assunto a noite toda.
english: He spent all night thinking about the matter.

target: Estou chegando, espera só mais cinco minutos.
english: I'm arriving, just wait five more minutes.

target: Ela anda meio distante ultimamente.
english: She's been kind of distant lately.

target: Acabamos de receber a notícia.
english: We just received the news.

target: Ele passou a questionar tudo depois daquele dia.
english: He started questioning everything after that day.

target: Estávamos conversando sobre a viagem quando você chegou.
english: We were talking about the trip when you arrived.

target: Ela deixou de responder minhas mensagens.
english: She stopped answering my messages.

target: Ele vive dizendo que vai mudar, mas nunca muda.
english: He keeps saying he's going to change, but he never does.

target: Estou planejando uma surpresa para ela.
english: I'm planning a surprise for her.

target: Tenho que dar um jeito nisso.
english: I have to figure this out.
grammar: 'Dar um jeito' = to find a way/fix something — very Brazilian expression.

target: Ela ficou me esperando no portão.
english: She was waiting for me at the gate.

target: Acabei gastando mais do que deveria.
english: I ended up spending more than I should have.

target: Estou morrendo de saudade de vocês.
english: I miss you so much.

target: Ele anda trabalhando até tarde todo dia.
english: He's been working late every day.

target: Comecei a me interessar por arte contemporânea.
english: I started to get interested in contemporary art.

target: Ela parou de se preocupar com o que os outros pensam.
english: She stopped worrying about what others think.

target: Eles continuam se amando depois de trinta anos.
english: They continue loving each other after thirty years.

target: Estou tentando me organizar melhor.
english: I'm trying to organize myself better.

target: Acabei me acostumando com o calor.
english: I ended up getting used to the heat.

target: Ele disse que estava cansado.
english: He said he was tired.
grammar: In reported speech, the present tense shifts to imperfect: 'estou' → 'estava'.

target: Ela me contou que ia viajar no feriado.
english: She told me she was going to travel on the holiday.

target: O professor avisou que a prova seria na sexta.
english: The teacher announced that the test would be on Friday.
grammar: Future 'vai ser' shifts to conditional 'seria' in reported speech.

target: Ele perguntou se eu queria ir ao cinema.
english: He asked if I wanted to go to the movies.
grammar: Questions in reported speech use 'se' (if/whether) instead of direct question form.

target: Ela disse que tinha terminado o trabalho.
english: She said she had finished the work.
grammar: Past tense shifts to pluperfect: 'terminei' → 'tinha terminado'.

target: O médico recomendou que eu descansasse mais.
english: The doctor recommended that I rest more.

target: Ele falou que não podia ir à festa.
english: He said he couldn't go to the party.

target: Ela me contou que estava procurando emprego.
english: She told me she was looking for a job.

target: O chefe pediu que todos chegassem no horário.
english: The boss asked that everyone arrive on time.

target: Ele disse que gostaria de nos visitar.
english: He said he would like to visit us.

target: Ela perguntou onde ficava a estação de metrô.
english: She asked where the subway station was.

target: O vizinho reclamou que estávamos fazendo muito barulho.
english: The neighbor complained that we were making too much noise.

target: Ele afirmou que já tinha pago a conta.
english: He stated that he had already paid the bill.

target: Ela disse que não sabia nada sobre o assunto.
english: She said she didn't know anything about the matter.

target: O professor explicou que a matéria cairia na prova.
english: The teacher explained that the material would be on the test.

target: Ele me pediu que não contasse para ninguém.
english: He asked me not to tell anyone.

target: Ela prometeu que voltaria antes das dez.
english: She promised she would be back before ten.

target: O garçom perguntou o que gostaríamos de beber.
english: The waiter asked what we would like to drink.

target: Ele comentou que o filme tinha sido muito bom.
english: He commented that the movie had been very good.

target: Ela mencionou que estava pensando em mudar de cidade.
english: She mentioned she was thinking about moving to another city.

target: O dentista disse que eu precisava usar aparelho.
english: The dentist said I needed to wear braces.

target: Ele me contou que tinha se separado da esposa.
english: He told me he had separated from his wife.

target: Ela perguntou quando a reunião ia começar.
english: She asked when the meeting was going to start.

target: O técnico informou que o conserto demoraria dois dias.
english: The technician informed us that the repair would take two days.

target: Ele disse que não concordava com a decisão.
english: He said he didn't agree with the decision.

target: Ela me avisou que ia se atrasar.
english: She warned me she was going to be late.

target: O gerente comunicou que haveria demissões.
english: The manager announced that there would be layoffs.

target: Ele perguntou se eu já tinha jantado.
english: He asked if I had already had dinner.

target: Ela disse que queria aprender a cozinhar.
english: She said she wanted to learn how to cook.

target: O professor sugeriu que lêssemos aquele livro.
english: The teacher suggested we read that book.

target: Ele afirmou que estaria lá às oito.
english: He stated he would be there at eight.

target: Ela me contou que o namorado tinha pedido ela em casamento.
english: She told me her boyfriend had proposed to her.

target: O vendedor garantiu que o produto era de qualidade.
english: The salesman guaranteed that the product was good quality.

target: Ele disse que ia ligar no dia seguinte.
english: He said he would call the next day.
grammar: 'Amanhã' becomes 'no dia seguinte' in reported speech.

target: Ela perguntou se podia trazer uma amiga.
english: She asked if she could bring a friend.

target: O médico explicou que o tratamento levaria seis meses.
english: The doctor explained that the treatment would take six months.

target: Ele me pediu que esperasse do lado de fora.
english: He asked me to wait outside.

target: Ela disse que já tinha visto aquele filme.
english: She said she had already seen that movie.

target: O motorista informou que a rua estava interditada.
english: The driver informed us that the street was blocked.

target: Ele admitiu que tinha errado.
english: He admitted that he had been wrong.

target: Ela perguntou quanto custava a passagem.
english: She asked how much the ticket cost.

target: O diretor anunciou que a empresa ia expandir.
english: The director announced that the company was going to expand.

target: Ele disse que adoraria ir, mas não podia.
english: He said he would love to go, but couldn't.

target: Ela me contou que estava grávida.
english: She told me she was pregnant.

target: O professor pediu que fizéssemos silêncio.
english: The teacher asked that we be quiet.

target: Ele respondeu que não tinha tempo naquele momento.
english: He replied that he didn't have time at that moment.
grammar: 'Agora' becomes 'naquele momento' in reported speech.

target: Ela disse que precisava falar comigo urgente.
english: She said she needed to talk to me urgently.

target: O mecânico avisou que o carro precisava de reparo.
english: The mechanic warned that the car needed repair.

target: Ele me perguntou por que eu estava triste.
english: He asked me why I was sad.

target: Ela garantiu que entregaria o projeto no prazo.
english: She guaranteed she would deliver the project on time.

target: O corretor disse que o apartamento tinha sido vendido.
english: The realtor said the apartment had been sold.

target: Ele comentou que o novo restaurante era excelente.
english: He commented that the new restaurant was excellent.

target: Ela nos avisou que não poderia comparecer à reunião.
english: She told us she wouldn't be able to attend the meeting.

target: O pai disse ao filho que precisava estudar mais.
english: The father told his son he needed to study more.

target: Ele perguntou se eu conhecia aquela pessoa.
english: He asked if I knew that person.

target: Ela me contou que tinha encontrado um novo trabalho.
english: She told me she had found a new job.

target: O chefe ordenou que todos saíssem da sala.
english: The boss ordered everyone to leave the room.

target: Ele explicou que a situação era mais complicada do que parecia.
english: He explained that the situation was more complicated than it seemed.

target: Ela disse que sentiria falta de todos.
english: She said she would miss everyone.

target: O advogado informou que o processo demoraria anos.
english: The lawyer informed us that the lawsuit would take years.

target: Ele me pediu que o ajudasse com a mudança.
english: He asked me to help him with the move.

target: Ela perguntou como eu tinha descoberto.
english: She asked how I had found out.

target: O guia disse que o museu fecharia às cinco.
english: The guide said the museum would close at five.

target: Ele me contou que tinha se arrependido da decisão.
english: He told me he had regretted the decision.

target: Ela mencionou que o marido tinha sido transferido.
english: She mentioned that her husband had been transferred.

target: O atendente disse que lá não aceitavam devolução.
english: The clerk said they didn't accept returns there.
grammar: 'Aqui' becomes 'lá' in reported speech when shifting location.

target: Ele afirmou que nunca tinha visto nada parecido.
english: He stated that he had never seen anything like it.

target: Ela me contou que ia fazer uma surpresa para o marido.
english: She told me she was going to plan a surprise for her husband.

target: O funcionário explicou que o sistema estava fora do ar.
english: The employee explained that the system was down.

target: Ele perguntou se eu podia emprestar meu carro.
english: He asked if I could lend him my car.

target: Ela disse que se sentira muito mal na véspera.
english: She said she had felt very sick the day before.
grammar: 'Ontem' becomes 'na véspera' or 'no dia anterior' in reported speech.

target: O treinador disse que o time precisava melhorar a defesa.
english: The coach said the team needed to improve its defense.

target: Ele me contou que estava pensando em se aposentar.
english: He told me he was thinking about retiring.

target: Ela perguntou se eu ia à festa no sábado.
english: She asked if I was going to the party on Saturday.

target: O professor avisou que quem não entregasse o trabalho levaria zero.
english: The teacher warned that whoever didn't turn in the paper would get a zero.

target: Ele disse que faria o possível para ajudar.
english: He said he would do whatever he could to help.

target: Ela me contou que tinha se reconciliado com a irmã.
english: She told me she had reconciled with her sister.

target: O piloto informou que aterrissaríamos em vinte minutos.
english: The pilot informed us that we would be landing in twenty minutes.

target: Ele perguntou se a gente queria mais alguma coisa.
english: He asked if we wanted anything else.

target: Ela disse que estava orgulhosa dos filhos.
english: She said she was proud of her children.

target: O político prometeu que reduziria os impostos.
english: The politician promised he would reduce taxes.

target: Ele me contou que a empresa estava passando por dificuldades.
english: He told me the company was going through difficulties.

target: Ela perguntou aonde eu estava indo.
english: She asked where I was going.
grammar: 'Aonde' is used with verbs of movement in reported speech too.

target: O cientista afirmou que a descoberta revolucionaria a medicina.
english: The scientist stated that the discovery would revolutionize medicine.

target: Ele disse que não tinha feito por mal.
english: He said he hadn't done it on purpose.
grammar: 'Fazer por mal' = to do something intentionally/with bad intent.

target: Ela me avisou que a estrada estava perigosa.
english: She warned me that the road was dangerous.

target: O dono do restaurante contou que a receita era da avó dele.
english: The restaurant owner said the recipe was from his grandmother.

target: Ele explicou que tinha chegado atrasado por causa do trânsito.
english: He explained that he had arrived late because of traffic.

target: Ela disse que pensaria na proposta e daria uma resposta.
english: She said she would think about the proposal and give an answer.

target: O presidente declarou que tomaria todas as medidas necessárias.
english: The president declared he would take all necessary measures.

target: Ele me pediu que mantivesse sigilo sobre o assunto.
english: He asked me to keep the matter confidential.

target: Ela contou que aquele tinha sido o melhor dia da vida dela.
english: She said that had been the best day of her life.
grammar: 'Esse/este' becomes 'aquele' in reported speech.

target: O técnico garantiu que o problema seria resolvido naquele dia.
english: The technician guaranteed the problem would be solved that day.
grammar: 'Hoje' becomes 'naquele dia' in reported speech.

target: Ele disse que nós nos veríamos em breve.
english: He said we would see each other soon.

target: Ela me contou que tinha decidido adotar uma criança.
english: She told me she had decided to adopt a child.

target: O médico recomendou que ele tomasse mais água.
english: The doctor recommended he drink more water.

target: Ela disse que precisava sair mais cedo naquele dia.
english: She said she needed to leave early that day.

target: Acho que ele está certo.
english: I think he's right.
grammar: With 'achar que' in the affirmative, use the indicative. Negated → subjunctive.

target: Não acho que ele esteja certo.
english: I don't think he's right.
grammar: Negated opinion triggers subjunctive: 'esteja' instead of 'está'.

target: Acredito que ela vai conseguir.
english: I believe she'll make it.

target: Não acredito que ela consiga sozinha.
english: I don't believe she can do it alone.

target: É claro que ele sabe a resposta.
english: It's clear he knows the answer.
grammar: Expressions of certainty (é claro que, é certo que) take the indicative.

target: Não é claro que ele saiba a resposta.
english: It's not clear that he knows the answer.

target: Parece que vai chover.
english: It looks like it's going to rain.
grammar: 'Parece que' + indicative when expressing likelihood.

target: Não parece que vá chover hoje.
english: It doesn't seem like it's going to rain today.
grammar: Negated 'parece' can trigger subjunctive.

target: Talvez eu vá à festa.
english: Maybe I'll go to the party.

target: Eu talvez vou à festa.
english: I might go to the party.
grammar: When 'talvez' comes after the subject, the indicative is sometimes used in casual speech.

target: Espero que tudo se resolva.
english: I hope everything gets resolved.

target: Tenho certeza de que ele vem.
english: I'm sure he's coming.
grammar: 'Ter certeza de que' takes the indicative — it expresses certainty.

target: Não tenho certeza de que ele venha.
english: I'm not sure he's coming.

target: Pode ser que ela mude de ideia.
english: It could be that she changes her mind.

target: O fato é que precisamos agir.
english: The fact is we need to act.
grammar: 'O fato é que' takes indicative.

target: Duvido que isso aconteça novamente.
english: I doubt that will happen again.

target: Supondo que ele aceite, o que fazemos?
english: Assuming he accepts, what do we do?
grammar: 'Supondo que' takes subjunctive — hypothetical scenario.

target: Desde que me avisem, não tem problema.
english: As long as they let me know, no problem.
grammar: 'Desde que' as a condition requires subjunctive.

target: Desde que me lembro, sempre foi assim.
english: Since I can remember, it's always been like this.
grammar: 'Desde que' meaning 'since' (time) takes indicative.

target: Quem quer que seja, diga que não estou.
english: Whoever it is, say I'm not here.
grammar: 'Quem quer que' + subjunctive = 'whoever'.

target: Onde quer que você vá, te encontro.
english: Wherever you go, I'll find you.
grammar: 'Onde quer que' + subjunctive = 'wherever'.

target: O que quer que aconteça, estou do seu lado.
english: Whatever happens, I'm on your side.

target: Por mais que eu explique, ele não entende.
english: No matter how much I explain, he doesn't understand.

target: Qualquer que seja a decisão, vou respeitar.
english: Whatever the decision may be, I'll respect it.
grammar: 'Qualquer que seja' + subjunctive.

target: É verdade que ele trabalha muito.
english: It's true that he works a lot.
grammar: Affirmative truth statements use indicative.

target: Não é verdade que ele trabalhe tanto assim.
english: It's not true that he works that much.

target: Basta que ele peça, e eu ajudo.
english: All he has to do is ask, and I'll help.

target: Nem que eu implore, ele não vai.
english: Even if I beg, he won't go.

target: Tomara que não demore.
english: I hope it doesn't take long.

target: Que ele faça o que quiser.
english: Let him do whatever he wants.
grammar: Subjunctive used in the third person to express 'let him/her'.

target: Quer queira, quer não, vai ter que ir.
english: Whether he likes it or not, he'll have to go.
grammar: 'Quer... quer' + subjunctive = 'whether... or'.

target: Se bem que ele tem um ponto.
english: Although, he does have a point.
grammar: 'Se bem que' takes indicative — it introduces a concession of fact.

target: Aconteça o que acontecer, mantenha a calma.
english: Whatever happens, stay calm.
grammar: Repeated subjunctive for emphasis: 'aconteça o que acontecer'.

target: Custe o que custar, vamos terminar.
english: Whatever it costs, we'll finish.

target: Caso contrário, teremos que cancelar.
english: Otherwise, we'll have to cancel.

target: Não que eu não queira, é que não posso.
english: It's not that I don't want to, it's that I can't.
grammar: 'Não que' takes subjunctive — negates a possible reason.

target: Dado que todos concordam, podemos avançar.
english: Given that everyone agrees, we can move forward.
grammar: 'Dado que' + indicative when stating a fact.

target: Queira ou não queira, ele vai ter que aceitar.
english: Like it or not, he'll have to accept.

target: Sempre que eu puder, vou te ajudar.
english: Whenever I can, I'll help you.
grammar: 'Sempre que' takes subjunctive when referring to future/hypothetical times.

target: Sempre que eu posso, ajudo meus vizinhos.
english: Whenever I can, I help my neighbors.
grammar: 'Sempre que' + indicative for habitual actions.

target: Por pouco que seja, qualquer doação ajuda.
english: However little it may be, any donation helps.
grammar: 'Por pouco que' + subjunctive.

target: Talvez seja melhor a gente ir embora.
english: Maybe it's better for us to leave.

target: Eu sei que ele está mentindo.
english: I know he's lying.
grammar: Verbs of knowing (saber, conhecer) take indicative.

target: Não sei se ele está mentindo.
english: I don't know if he's lying.
grammar: 'Não sei se' takes indicative — uncertainty doesn't trigger subjunctive here.

target: Ele não acredita que a Terra seja redonda.
english: He doesn't believe the Earth is round.

target: É provável que eles cheguem amanhã.
english: It's likely they'll arrive tomorrow.

target: É certo que a vida tem altos e baixos.
english: It's certain that life has ups and downs.

target: Não é certo que ele venha à reunião.
english: It's not certain he'll come to the meeting.

target: Mesmo que eu quisesse, não conseguiria.
english: Even if I wanted to, I couldn't.

target: Ele age como se fosse o dono do lugar.
english: He acts as if he were the owner of the place.

target: Antes que eu me esqueça, tem um recado pra você.
english: Before I forget, there's a message for you.

target: Sem que percebessem, ele já tinha saído.
english: Without them realizing, he had already left.

target: Mal ele chegou, já começou a reclamar.
english: No sooner had he arrived than he started complaining.
grammar: 'Mal' with indicative — 'as soon as' with past events.

target: A não ser que chova, o evento será ao ar livre.
english: Unless it rains, the event will be outdoors.

target: Embora esteja sol, faz frio.
english: Although it's sunny, it's cold.

target: Por menor que seja o esforço, já é alguma coisa.
english: However small the effort may be, it's already something.

target: Até onde eu sei, o projeto foi aprovado.
english: As far as I know, the project was approved.
grammar: 'Até onde eu sei' + indicative — states speaker's knowledge.

target: Quem me dera poder viajar o ano inteiro.
english: I wish I could travel all year.
grammar: 'Quem me dera' + imperfect subjunctive = strong wish.

target: É possível que a gente se encontre lá.
english: It's possible we'll meet there.

target: É fato que a economia está crescendo.
english: It's a fact that the economy is growing.

target: Nego que ele tenha me dito isso.
english: I deny that he told me that.
grammar: 'Negar que' always triggers subjunctive.

target: Espero que vocês tenham gostado.
english: I hope you liked it.

target: Quando eu puder, te ligo.
english: When I can, I'll call you.
grammar: 'Quando' + subjunctive for future/uncertain time.

target: Quando eu posso, saio pra caminhar.
english: When I can, I go out for a walk.
grammar: 'Quando' + indicative for habitual actions.

target: Se eu tiver tempo, passo na sua casa.
english: If I have time, I'll stop by your place.
grammar: 'Se' + future subjunctive (tiver) for possible future conditions.

target: Se eu tenho tempo, gosto de ler.
english: If I have time, I like to read.
grammar: 'Se' + indicative for general truths/habits.

target: Após terminar, avise-me.
english: After finishing, let me know.

target: Diga o que disser, não vou mudar de opinião.
english: Say whatever you want, I won't change my mind.

target: Venha quem vier, a festa vai acontecer.
english: Come what may, the party will happen.

target: Convém que você tome cuidado.
english: It's advisable that you be careful.

target: É questionável que essa estratégia funcione.
english: It's questionable whether that strategy works.

target: Eu insisto que ele venha à reunião.
english: I insist that he come to the meeting.
grammar: 'Insistir que' + subjunctive when expressing demand/wish.

target: Eu insisto que ele está errado.
english: I insist that he's wrong.
grammar: 'Insistir que' + indicative when asserting a belief/fact.

target: É inegável que ele tem talento.
english: It's undeniable that he has talent.

target: Não é que eu não goste, é que prefiro outra coisa.
english: It's not that I don't like it, it's that I prefer something else.

target: Por pior que seja, temos que encarar.
english: However bad it may be, we have to face it.

target: Resta saber se ele aceita a proposta.
english: It remains to be seen whether he accepts the proposal.

target: Quem dera eu tivesse mais tempo livre.
english: I wish I had more free time.

target: Supondo que tudo dê certo, quando começamos?
english: Assuming everything goes well, when do we start?

target: A verdade é que ninguém sabe o que vai acontecer.
english: The truth is that nobody knows what's going to happen.

target: Haja o que houver, estou do seu lado.
english: Come what may, I'm on your side.

target: Ainda que eu esteja errado, pelo menos tentei.
english: Even if I'm wrong, at least I tried.

target: É hora de que todos assumam suas responsabilidades.
english: It's time for everyone to take on their responsibilities.

target: Quanto mais cedo ele chegar, melhor.
english: The sooner he arrives, the better.

target: Quaisquer que sejam as consequências, estou preparado.
english: Whatever the consequences may be, I'm ready.

target: Não há quem aguente essa situação.
english: There's no one who can stand this situation.

target: Por mais que doa, a verdade precisa ser dita.
english: No matter how much it hurts, the truth needs to be told.

target: Faça o que fizer, faça bem feito.
english: Whatever you do, do it well.
grammar: Repeated subjunctive structure emphasizes 'whatever/no matter what'.

target: E aí, beleza? Tudo tranquilo?
english: Hey, what's up? All good?
grammar: Very informal Brazilian greeting — 'e aí' + 'beleza' + 'tudo tranquilo'.

target: Prezado senhor, venho por meio desta solicitar informações.
english: Dear sir, I am writing to request information.
grammar: Highly formal letter opening — 'venho por meio desta' is standard business Portuguese.

target: Cara, essa festa vai ser irada!
english: Dude, this party is going to be awesome!
grammar: 'Cara' = dude. 'Irada' = awesome/sick — Brazilian slang.

target: Valeu, irmão! Tamo junto!
english: Thanks, bro! We're in this together!
grammar: 'Valeu' = thanks (informal). 'Tamo junto' = 'we're together' — solidarity expression.

target: Gostaríamos de agendar uma reunião para discutir os termos.
english: We would like to schedule a meeting to discuss the terms.

target: Mano, tu é muito gente boa!
english: Bro, you're such a great person!
grammar: 'Mano' (from 'irmão') is very common in São Paulo slang.

target: Vossa Excelência está convidada para a cerimônia.
english: Your Excellency is invited to the ceremony.
grammar: 'Vossa Excelência' — used for judges, governors, and other dignitaries.

target: Tá ligado no que eu tô falando?
english: You know what I'm saying?
grammar: 'Tá ligado' = 'you know/you follow' — very colloquial.

target: Solicito a gentileza de encaminhar os documentos.
english: I kindly request that you forward the documents.

target: Qual é, meu parceiro? Vamo nessa!
english: What's up, partner? Let's go!
grammar: 'Qual é' = 'what's up'. 'Vamo nessa' = 'let's do it'.

target: O supracitado relatório encontra-se em anexo.
english: The aforementioned report is attached herein.
grammar: 'Supracitado' is formal bureaucratic language — 'aforementioned'.

target: Nossa, que da hora esse lugar!
english: Wow, this place is so cool!
grammar: 'Da hora' = cool/awesome — São Paulo slang.

target: Cumpre-me informar que o prazo foi prorrogado.
english: I must inform you that the deadline has been extended.
grammar: 'Cumpre-me informar' is very formal — official correspondence.

target: Ele é muito mala, ninguém aguenta.
english: He's so annoying, nobody can stand him.
grammar: 'Mala' = annoying person — Brazilian slang.

target: Vou ficar te devendo essa, tá?
english: I'll owe you one, okay?

target: Outrossim, informamos que os valores foram atualizados.
english: Furthermore, we inform you that the amounts have been updated.
grammar: 'Outrossim' is extremely formal — 'furthermore/moreover' in legal/official texts.

target: Esse rolê vai ser sinistro!
english: This outing is going to be insane!
grammar: 'Rolê' = outing/hangout. 'Sinistro' = amazing/insane — slang.

target: Diante do exposto, requeremos a devida providência.
english: In view of the above, we request appropriate action.

target: Boto fé que ele vai dar conta.
english: I believe he'll handle it.
grammar: 'Botar fé' = to believe in/trust — informal expression.

target: Por oportuno, informo que estarei ausente na próxima semana.
english: On this occasion, I inform you I will be absent next week.

target: Pô, que vacilo! Esqueci a carteira em casa.
english: Man, what a blunder! I forgot my wallet at home.
grammar: 'Pô' = man/dude (exclamation). 'Vacilo' = mistake/blunder.

target: Aguardamos vossa manifestação no prazo de cinco dias úteis.
english: We await your response within five business days.

target: Ele é muito zueiro, não leva nada a sério.
english: He's such a joker, he doesn't take anything seriously.
grammar: 'Zueiro' = person who's always joking around — slang.

target: Venho respeitosamente solicitar a revisão do processo.
english: I respectfully request a review of the proceedings.

target: Ela é show de bola, faz tudo direitinho.
english: She's awesome, she does everything just right.
grammar: 'Show de bola' = awesome/fantastic — informal expression.

target: Subscrevo-me atenciosamente.
english: I remain respectfully yours.
grammar: Very formal letter closing — 'subscrevo-me atenciosamente'.

target: Tô na correria, não posso parar agora.
english: I'm super busy, I can't stop now.
grammar: 'Correria' = hustle/being rushed — very Brazilian.

target: Conforme deliberação da assembleia, ficou decidido que...
english: As per the assembly's resolution, it was decided that...

target: Que mico! Tropecei na frente de todo mundo.
english: How embarrassing! I tripped in front of everyone.
grammar: 'Mico' = embarrassment/embarrassing situation — Brazilian slang.

target: Sem condição, esse trânsito! Não anda nada.
english: This traffic is impossible! Not moving at all.
grammar: 'Sem condição' = no way/impossible — informal.

target: Informamos que o atendimento será suspenso temporariamente.
english: We inform you that service will be temporarily suspended.

target: Ele me passou a perna no negócio.
english: He ripped me off in the deal.
grammar: 'Passar a perna' = to cheat/rip off someone.

target: Encaminho em anexo o parecer técnico solicitado.
english: I am forwarding the requested technical opinion attached.

target: Que saudade de vocês! Bora marcar algo?
english: I miss you guys! Shall we plan something?
grammar: 'Bora' = 'vamos embora' contracted = let's go/let's.

target: Ficou massa essa comida!
english: This food turned out awesome!
grammar: 'Massa' = awesome/great — common in Northeastern Brazilian slang.

target: Tendo em vista o acima exposto, solicito deferimento.
english: In view of the above, I request approval.
grammar: 'Solicito deferimento' is standard closing for official petitions.

target: Ele é muito pilha, tá sempre animado.
english: He's so energetic, he's always pumped.
grammar: 'Pilha' = energetic/hyped — Southern Brazilian slang.

target: Firmeza! Nos vemos amanhã então.
english: For sure! See you tomorrow then.
grammar: 'Firmeza' = for sure/understood — informal agreement.

target: Nestes termos, peço deferimento.
english: In these terms, I request approval.
grammar: Standard formal petition closing.

target: Partiu praia no fim de semana?
english: Beach trip this weekend?
grammar: 'Partiu' + noun = 'let's go to' — very popular informal expression.

target: Ante o exposto, requer-se a concessão do benefício.
english: In view of the foregoing, the granting of the benefit is requested.

target: Eita, que susto! Você me assustou.
english: Whoa, what a scare! You scared me.
grammar: 'Eita' is an exclamation common in the Northeast and spreading nationwide.

target: A gente se fala, falou?
english: We'll talk later, deal?
grammar: 'Falou' at the end = 'deal?/okay?' — informal confirmation.

target: Segue abaixo a relação dos itens solicitados.
english: Below is the list of requested items.

target: Não sei não, acho meio furada.
english: I don't know, I think it's a bad idea.
grammar: 'Furada' = bad idea/trap — informal.

target: Em atendimento à sua solicitação, informo que...
english: In response to your request, I inform you that...

target: Ele é muito pão-duro, nunca paga nada.
english: He's so stingy, he never pays for anything.
grammar: 'Pão-duro' = stingy person — common Brazilian expression.

target: O negócio é o seguinte: precisamos conversar.
english: Here's the thing: we need to talk.
grammar: 'O negócio é o seguinte' = 'here's the deal/thing'.

target: Atenciosamente, fulano de tal.
english: Sincerely, so-and-so.
grammar: 'Atenciosamente' = 'sincerely' — standard formal closing.

target: Fica frio, vai dar tudo certo.
english: Chill out, everything will be fine.
grammar: 'Fica frio' = stay cool/chill — very informal.

target: Vimos, pelo presente, requerer a expedição do certificado.
english: We hereby request the issuance of the certificate.
grammar: 'Vimos requerer' — extremely formal request opening.

target: Fala sério! Não acredito nisso.
english: Come on! I can't believe that.
grammar: 'Fala sério' = come on/no way — disbelief expression.

target: Ô de casa! Tem alguém aí?
english: Hello! Is anyone home?
grammar: 'Ô de casa' is a traditional way to announce yourself at someone's door.

target: Comunico que estarei à disposição para esclarecimentos.
english: I communicate that I will be available for clarification.

target: Sujou! A polícia tá vindo.
english: Trouble! The police are coming.
